parsed.thinking_summary: First-pass library SDK for USDA NASS API. Key decisions: httpx with EventHook for rate-limit redaction, Pydantic v2 for typed response models, generator-based pagination to support early-stop without iterating over all NASS offset pages, optional pandas dependency for DataFrame consumers. The rate-limit context manager is the most novel feature and should be the primary differentiator from ad-hoc NASS API calls.
parsed.title: USDA NASS Public API Python SDK
parsed.executive_summary: A Python 3.10+ library providing a typed query builder, rate-limit context manager, and generator-based pagination for the USDA National Agricultural Statistics Service public API. Built with httpx for sync and async HTTP and Pydantic v2 for typed response models. Optional pandas dependency enables DataFrame output. Distributed via PyPI via GitHub Actions trusted publisher.
parsed.architecture_overview: Library architecture: three public modules (nass_sdk.client, nass_sdk.query, nass_sdk.models) with an internal httpx wrapper (nass_sdk._http) for rate-limit enforcement and api_key redaction. The client module provides a NassClient(api_key=...) constructor and a rate-limited context manager. The query module provides CommodityQueryBuilder, StateQueryBuilder, and SurveyQueryBuilder with fluent chaining. The models module provides typed Pydantic v2 models for all NASS response fields. The internal httpx wrapper uses httpx.AsyncClient for async pagination and a custom EventHook that redacts the api_key query parameter before logging. Optional pandas dependency is installed via nass-sdk[pandas] and used in the .to_dataframe() method. No hosted service, no database, no auth infrastructure.
parsed.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.10+. HTTP client: httpx 0.28 (sync and async). Response models: Pydantic v2 2.x. Output: pandas 2.x (optional dependency). Logging: Python standard library logging. Distribution: PyPI via GitHub Actions trusted publisher. Testing: pytest 8.x, pytest-vcr for NASS API cassette tests. Build: hatchling for pyproject.toml. Lint: ruff. Type checking: mypy strict mode.
parsed.functional_feature_map: NassClient(api_key=...) constructor with optional api_key env var fallback. CommodityQueryBuilder(commodity_desc, statisticcat_desc, unit_desc, year, state_alpha, value, cv_pct, reference_period_desc) with fluent chaining (commodity().state('IA').year(2023).fetch()). StateQueryBuilder(state_alpha, state_ansi_code, state_name, county_code, county_name, value, unit_desc, year). SurveyQueryBuilder(source_desc, sector_desc, group_desc, commodity_desc). YieldEstimate and AcreageReport Pydantic v2 models. Rate-limited context manager with hourly and daily limits. .to_dataframe() method returning pandas DataFrame (optional pandas dependency required). AsyncNassClient for async pagination. AsyncRateLimiter for async rate-limit enforcement.
parsed.system_components: nass_sdk.client module: NassClient(api_key=None), AsyncNassClient(api_key=None), RateLimiter(hourly=500, daily=4800). nass_sdk.query module: CommodityQueryBuilder, StateQueryBuilder, SurveyQueryBuilder. nass_sdk.models module: CommodityQuery, StateStatistic, YieldEstimate, AcreageReport, SurveyProgram, SurveyProgramList. nass_sdk._http module: httpx.AsyncClient, httpx.Client, httpx.AsyncResponseHook (api_key redaction), httpx.AsyncRequestHook (rate-limit enforcement). nass_sdk.utils module: Pydantic v2 model validators, logger, cache. Optional nass-sdk[pandas]: pandas.DataFrame constructor.
parsed.workflows: Querying Commodity Statistics: Researcher calls client.commodity().state('IA').year_range(2018, 2023).unit_desc('bu_per_acre').fetch(). The client creates a CommodityQuery object, sends a GET to api.nass.usda.gov/api/get?commodity_desc=Corn&statisticcat_desc=Production&unit_desc=bu_per_acre&year_range=2018%2C2023&state_alpha=IA&value=1000000&cv_pct=100&reference_period_desc=2022, and receives a JSON array of YieldEstimate instances. The generator yields one YieldEstimate per NASS offset page. Researcher can call next() to advance to the next page without iterating over all pages. Rate-Limited Pagination: Researcher calls client.commodity().state('IA').year(2023).unit_desc('bu_per_acre').fetch() with a 500-record limit. The RateLimiter context manager enforces the hourly limit (500 records) and daily limit (4800 records) with exponential backoff on 429 responses. The httpx EventHook redacts the api_key query parameter before logging. Debug log: each fetch logs commodity, page offset, records_returned, rate_limit_remaining at DEBUG level via nass_sdk logger.
parsed.data_model: CommodityQuery (commodity_desc: str, statisticcat_desc: str, unit_desc: str, year: int, state_alpha: str, value: float | None, cv_pct: float | None, reference_period_desc: str). StateStatistic (state_alpha: str, state_ansi_code: str, state_name: str, county_code: str | None, county_name: str | None, value: float | None, unit_desc: str, year: int). YieldEstimate (commodity_desc: str, year: int, state_alpha: str, yield_value: float, unit_desc: str, cv_pct: float). AcreageReport (commodity_desc: str, year: int, state_alpha: str, planted_acres: float, harvested_acres: float, reference_period_desc: str). SurveyProgram (source_desc: str, sector_desc: str, group_desc: str, commodity_desc: str). SurveyProgramList (survey_program: SurveyProgram, total_count: int).
parsed.api_design: Public API surface: nass_sdk.client.NassClient(api_key=None, base_url='api.nass.usda.gov'). nass_sdk.client.NassClient.commodity().state(state_alpha: str) -> CommodityQueryBuilder. nass_sdk.client.NassClient.commodity().state(state_alpha: str, year: int) -> CommodityQueryBuilder. nass_sdk.client.NassClient.commodity().unit_desc(unit_desc: str) -> CommodityQueryBuilder. nass_sdk.client.NassClient.commodity().year_range(start: int, end: int) -> CommodityQueryBuilder. nass_sdk.client.NassClient.commodity().all() -> list[YieldEstimate]. nass_sdk.client.NassClient.state().state(state_alpha: str) -> StateQueryBuilder. nass_sdk.client.NassClient.survey().survey(source_desc: str) -> SurveyQueryBuilder. nass_sdk.client.NassClient.rate_limited(hourly: int, daily: int) -> RateLimiter. nass_sdk.client.NassClient.rate_limited(hourly: int, daily: int, max_retries: int) -> RateLimiter. nass_sdk.client.NassClient.to_dataframe(query: CommodityQuery) -> pd.DataFrame (optional pandas dependency required). nass_sdk.models.CommodityQuery.to_dataframe() -> pd.DataFrame (optional pandas dependency required). nass_sdk.models.StateStatistic.to_dataframe() -> pd.DataFrame (optional pandas dependency required). nass_sdk.models.YieldEstimate.to_dataframe() -> pd.DataFrame (optional pandas dependency required). nass_sdk.models.AcreageReport.to_dataframe() -> pd.DataFrame (optional pandas dependency required).
parsed.security_and_compliance: API key read from environment variable by default; never logged or included in error messages. httpx EventHook redacts api_key query parameter before logging at DEBUG level. No network calls made on import; library is a local import. Optional pandas dependency requires a try/except ImportError guard in to_dataframe() and a clear ImportError message ('Install nass-sdk[pandas] or pip install pandas to use .to_dataframe()'). USDA NASS data is public; no PII or sensitive data handled by the SDK.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: PyPI package: nass_sdk 0.1.0. GitHub repository: src/nass_sdk/ layout with pyproject.toml (hatchling build backend). GitHub Actions: test matrix (Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12), ruff lint, mypy strict type check, pytest with 90% coverage gate, test against NASS API sandbox (vcr.py cassettes for deterministic offline tests). GitHub Release triggers PyPI publish via OIDC trusted publisher (no stored secrets). Optional dependencies: nass-sdk[pandas] installs pandas>=1.5; nass-sdk[async] installs httpx[asyncio] (already included in base httpx).
parsed.observability: Python standard library logging module with nass_sdk logger configured with DEBUG level. Each paginated fetch logs commodity, page offset, records_returned, rate_limit_remaining at DEBUG level via nass_sdk logger. No external monitoring services. Consumers configure log level via logging.getLogger('nass_sdk').setLevel(logging.DEBUG).
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost. PyPI free tier sufficient for open-source package. GitHub Actions free tier sufficient for open-source repo. Package size target: under 5MB including optional pandas dependency. No scaling concerns for a local library; consumers install it on their own machines.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 weeks): NassClient with synchronous HTTP, CommodityQueryBuilder, YieldEstimate and AcreageReport Pydantic models, automatic pagination, rate-limit context manager with hourly and daily limits, .to_dataframe() with optional pandas dependency, pip/PyPI distribution. Phase 2 (4 weeks): AsyncNassClient for async pagination, AsyncRateLimiter for async rate-limit enforcement, SurveyQueryBuilder, SurveyProgramList, pytest-vcr cassettes for NASS API offline testing, optional nass-sdk[pandas] integration. Phase 3 (4 weeks): CLI wrapper for ad-hoc NASS query execution from terminal, polars DataFrame integration as alternative to pandas, Jupyter notebook integration via JupyterClient.
parsed.development_guidelines: TDD: all public API functions have unit tests written before implementation. pytest coverage gate: 90% line coverage enforced in CI. ruff for linting and import sorting. mypy strict mode enforced in CI. No match/case statements or union types (X | Y) in function signatures. Optional pandas dependency requires a try/except ImportError guard in to_dataframe() and a clear ImportError message. httpx EventHook must be used for rate-limit redaction; never log api_key directly.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: NASS API changes its response schema without notice, breaking the SDK. Mitigation: pytest-vcr cassettes for offline tests; consumers can run tests against NASS API sandbox before deploying. Risk: httpx EventHook redaction may not be compatible with all logging backends. Mitigation: log to stderr via logging.getLogger('nass_sdk').addHandler(logging.StreamHandler(sys.stderr)) if DEBUG logging is not available. Tradeoff: Pydantic v2 over dataclasses for typed response models; adds complexity but enables better error handling and type safety.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Async support: AsyncNassClient and AsyncRateLimiter added in Phase 2. Optional pandas dependency: nass-sdk[pandas] installed via pip install nass-sdk[pandas] in Phase 2. CLI wrapper: CLI module added in Phase 3. Rate-limit context manager: rate_limited(hourly=500, daily=4800) context manager added in Phase 1.

reference.thinking_summary: This is a Python library_sdk for the USDA NASS public API -- no hosted service, no database, no frontend. Key design decisions: fluent method-chaining query builder for IDE autocomplete, generator-based pagination so consumers stop early without fetching all pages, optional pandas dependency to avoid forcing heavy imports on non-dataframe users, and httpx EventHook to redact api_key from debug logs. Plan quality is moderate -- some sections are appropriately brief for a library that has no deployment topology, no security hardening beyond API key hygiene, and no cost/scaling concerns.
reference.title: nass-sdk: Python Client for USDA NASS Quick Stats API
reference.executive_summary: A PyPI-distributed Python SDK (Python 3.10+) for the USDA NASS Quick Stats API providing typed Pydantic v2 response models, a fluent method-chaining query builder, generator-based pagination with transparent rate-limit enforcement, and .to_dataframe() output for pandas integration. Designed for data scientists and agronomists automating crop yield, acreage, and survey data retrieval in Python and Jupyter notebook workflows. No hosted infrastructure; distributed on PyPI via GitHub Actions trusted publisher.
reference.architecture_overview: Pure Python library distributed as a PyPI package. Three public modules: nass_sdk.client exposes NassClient (sync) and AsyncNassClient (Phase 2); nass_sdk.query exposes CommodityQueryBuilder, StateQueryBuilder, and SurveyQueryBuilder with fluent method chaining; nass_sdk.models exposes Pydantic v2 models (YieldEstimate, AcreageReport, StateStatistic, SurveyProgram, CommodityQuery). Internal module nass_sdk._http wraps httpx.Client with API key injection, rate-limit backoff, and EventHook-based api_key redaction from debug logs. No network calls are made on import. NassClient instantiation accepts api_key parameter or reads USDA_NASS_API_KEY environment variable. Pagination is abstracted as a generator yielding individual Pydantic model instances across NASS offset-based pages, allowing early stopping via next() or islice().
reference.technology_stack: Python 3.10+. Mandatory dependencies: httpx>=0.27 (HTTP client with sync support), pydantic>=2.6 (response model validation). Optional dependency groups: nass-sdk[pandas] adds pandas>=1.5 for .to_dataframe(); nass-sdk[dev] adds pytest, pytest-recording, vcrpy, ruff, mypy, coverage. Build: hatchling via pyproject.toml. Distribution: PyPI via GitHub Actions OIDC trusted publisher. Type stubs: included in package (py.typed marker, inline annotations).
reference.functional_feature_map: NassClient: instantiation with api_key or USDA_NASS_API_KEY env var; .commodity() returns CommodityQueryBuilder; .state_statistics() returns StateQueryBuilder; .survey_programs() returns SurveyQueryBuilder. CommodityQueryBuilder: .state(alpha_code) filters by state, .year(y) or .year_range(start, end) sets year filter, .unit_desc(ud) filters by unit descriptor, .commodity(name) sets commodity, .fetch() returns List[YieldEstimate], .all() returns Generator[YieldEstimate] paginating until exhausted. Rate-limit context manager: with client.rate_limited(hourly=500, daily=4800): enforces limits, retries on 429 with exponential backoff up to max_retries=3, raises NassRateLimitError if exhausted. .to_dataframe() on any List[PydanticModel]: returns pandas DataFrame with model field names as columns; raises ImportError with install hint if pandas not installed.
reference.system_components: nass_sdk/__init__.py: public exports (NassClient, NassRateLimitError, NassApiError, NassSdkError). nass_sdk/client.py: NassClient class, _http attribute, .commodity()/.state_statistics()/.survey_programs() factory methods. nass_sdk/query.py: CommodityQueryBuilder, StateQueryBuilder, SurveyQueryBuilder with .state()/.year()/.year_range()/.unit_desc()/.commodity()/.fetch()/.all() methods. nass_sdk/models.py: Pydantic v2 models with field validators for NASS string-numeric coercion and suppressed value handling. nass_sdk/_http.py: httpx.Client wrapper, api_key query param injection, rate_limit_state tracking, EventHook for api_key redaction, pagination logic as _fetch_pages() generator. nass_sdk/exceptions.py: NassSdkError, NassApiError, NassRateLimitError.
reference.workflows: Crop Yield Query Workflow: Data scientist instantiates client = NassClient() (reads USDA_NASS_API_KEY from env) -> chains client.commodity().state('IA').year_range(2018, 2023).unit_desc('BU / ACRE').fetch() -> SDK constructs NASS API URL with query parameters, paginates across offset pages internally, validates each record via YieldEstimate Pydantic model, returns List[YieldEstimate] -> researcher calls .to_dataframe() on result for pandas analysis. Rate-Limit-Aware Large Retrieval Workflow: Researcher needs all soybean acreage reports for all states (potentially thousands of records) -> uses with client.rate_limited(hourly=490, daily=4750): for report in client.commodity().commodity('SOYBEANS').all(): process(report) -> SDK yields YieldEstimate instances one by one, transparently fetching next offset page when needed, sleeping and retrying on 429 responses, surfacing NassRateLimitError only when max_retries exhausted. The generator pattern allows early termination if only first N results are needed.
reference.data_model: Pydantic v2 models (no persistent database). YieldEstimate: commodity_desc str, year int, state_alpha str, yield_value Optional[float] (None if NASS suppressed value '(D)'), unit_desc str, cv_pct Optional[float], reference_period_desc str, suppressed bool = False. AcreageReport: commodity_desc, year, state_alpha, planted_acres Optional[float], harvested_acres Optional[float], reference_period_desc, suppressed bool. StateStatistic: state_alpha, state_ansi_code str, state_name, county_code Optional[str], county_name Optional[str], value Optional[float], unit_desc, year, suppressed bool. SurveyProgram: source_desc NASS API literal (CENSUS or SURVEY), sector_desc, group_desc, commodity_desc. CommodityQuery: internal query parameter model used by query builders to construct NASS API request URLs.
reference.api_design: Public Python API (not HTTP endpoints). NassClient(api_key: Optional[str] = None, timeout: float = 30.0, max_retries: int = 3). .commodity() -> CommodityQueryBuilder. CommodityQueryBuilder.state(alpha: str) -> self. .year(y: int) -> self. .year_range(start: int, end: int) -> self. .unit_desc(ud: str) -> self. .fetch() -> List[YieldEstimate]. .all() -> Generator[YieldEstimate, None, None]. RateLimitedContext: client.rate_limited(hourly: int = 500, daily: int = 4800) -> contextmanager. DataFrame output: nass_sdk.utils.to_dataframe(records: List[BaseModel]) -> pd.DataFrame -- also available as .to_dataframe() method on List subclass returned by .fetch(). NassApiError(status_code: int, message: str). NassRateLimitError(retry_after: Optional[int]).
reference.security_and_compliance: API key security: NassClient reads api_key from constructor parameter or USDA_NASS_API_KEY environment variable; never falls back to hardcoded values. Key never included in exception messages, log output, or repr(). httpx EventHook on_request redacts api_key query parameter value to '***REDACTED***' before passing to httpx debug logger. HTTPS enforced: httpx.Client base_url set to https://quickstats.nass.usda.gov/api/get; no HTTP fallback. No credentials stored; each NassClient instance holds key in memory only for its lifetime. USDA NASS data is public domain; no license or compliance requirements on data usage.
reference.deployment_and_operations: PyPI distribution via hatchling build backend and pyproject.toml. GitHub Actions CI workflow: matrix test on Python 3.10/3.11/3.12, ruff lint, mypy --strict type check, pytest with vcrpy cassettes for deterministic offline NASS API replay (no live API calls in CI), coverage gate at 90%. GitHub Release (semver tag) triggers PyPI publish via OIDC trusted publisher (no API token stored in GitHub secrets). Versioning: semantic versioning; breaking API changes require major version bump per consumer convention. No deployment infrastructure.
reference.observability: Python standard library logging. Logger name: nass_sdk. Log levels: DEBUG -- each HTTP request URL (api_key redacted), page offset, records per page, rate_limit_remaining header value; INFO -- pagination complete with total records fetched; WARNING -- rate limit 429 received, retry attempt N of max_retries. Consumers configure log level via logging.getLogger('nass_sdk').setLevel(logging.DEBUG). No external monitoring, metrics, or telemetry.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost. PyPI free tier. GitHub Actions free for open-source. USDA NASS API is free with API key registration (usda.gov). SDK performance scales with NASS API rate limits (not internal throughput). For large bulk queries, the rate_limited context manager serializes requests within configured hourly/daily limits. No horizontal scaling considerations -- library runs in user's Python process.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - PyPI MVP (4 weeks): NassClient sync, CommodityQueryBuilder and StateQueryBuilder with .state()/.year()/.year_range()/.fetch()/.all(), YieldEstimate and AcreageReport Pydantic v2 models with suppressed value handling, automatic offset pagination generator, rate_limited context manager with exponential backoff, .to_dataframe() with optional pandas guard, api_key EventHook redaction, pytest suite with vcrpy cassettes at 90% coverage, PyPI publish via GitHub Actions trusted publisher. Phase 2 - Async and CLI (6 weeks): AsyncNassClient using httpx.AsyncClient, SurveyQueryBuilder for USDA survey program queries, CLI wrapper (nass query --commodity CORN --state IA --year 2023) via click, polars DataFrame support as alternative to pandas output. Phase 3 - Caching and Ecosystem (Q3): Redis-backed query result cache for shared Jupyter Hub environments, SurveyProgram and StateStatistic query builders, integration tests against live NASS sandbox environment.
reference.development_guidelines: Python 3.10 compatibility: no match/case statements, no X|Y union syntax in signatures -- use Optional[X] and Union[X,Y] from typing. Pydantic models use model_validator(mode='before') to coerce NASS string fields before field validation. All public methods and classes must have full type annotations; mypy --strict must pass. pytest fixtures use vcrpy @pytest.mark.vcr decorator to record/replay NASS API responses -- no live API calls in test suite. Optional pandas import guard pattern: def to_dataframe(self) -> 'pd.DataFrame': try: import pandas as pd ... except ImportError: raise ImportError('Install nass-sdk[pandas] or run pip install pandas'). Every new query builder method requires a corresponding pytest test using a recorded cassette.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: NASS API changes field names or pagination behavior without notice (undocumented public API) -- mitigated by vcrpy cassettes failing CI immediately when replay diverges from live response, prompting cassette update. Tradeoff: optional pandas dependency over bundled -- avoids forcing 30MB dependency on researchers using only Pydantic models; accepted complexity is ImportError guidance in .to_dataframe(). Risk: NASS API enforces undocumented rate limits that differ from documented values -- rate_limited context manager parameters are user-configurable to allow conservative settings below documented limits. Tradeoff: sync-only client in MVP over async-first -- synchronous httpx simplifies initial implementation and covers the primary Jupyter notebook use case; async added in Phase 2 for pipeline automation use cases.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed httpx over requests -- httpx supports both sync and async with identical API surface, enabling AsyncNassClient in Phase 2 without rewriting HTTP logic. Confirmed generator-based pagination over page-returning API -- generator allows early stopping with next()/islice() which is critical for exploratory analysis in Jupyter where researchers want to inspect first 20 records before fetching all 5000. Confirmed optional pandas over required -- USDA NASS data is frequently used with polars or directly as Pydantic model lists in automated pipelines; requiring pandas would be inappropriate for non-dataframe users.

parsed.thinking_summary: First-pass tenant portal architecture for a multifamily operator. Key decisions: Django 5 monolith with DRF for data model and REST API, Stripe for ACH and card payments with field-level encryption via django-encrypted-fields, Celery for Yardi CMMS webhook processing, Azure AD B2C for MFA and tenant identity. PCI SAQ-D scope requires Azure Key Vault for all secrets. Azure App Service with Azure Static Web Apps is the right hosting stack for this project.
parsed.title: Multifamily Tenant Portal Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: A security-hardened tenant portal built with React 18 and Django 5 on Azure, enabling multifamily residential tenants to pay rent, submit maintenance requests, view lease documents, and receive building announcements. Payments are processed via Stripe with field-level encryption for PCI-DSS SAQ-D compliance. Yardi PMS maintenance work orders are auto-routed via Celery-driven webhook adapters. Azure AD B2C enforces MFA for all tenants.
parsed.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: React 18 SPA served via Azure Static Web Apps communicates over HTTPS to a Django 5 backend on Azure App Service. Django backend handles REST API routing, JWT validation via django-jwt-auth, and Celery-driven async jobs for payment webhook processing and Yardi CMMS sync. Azure AD B2C manages tenant identity with MFA enforced via Conditional Access. Azure Key Vault holds all secrets including Stripe API keys and Django SECRET_KEY. Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server stores the tenant, lease, payment, maintenance, and document data. Azure Cache for Redis 7 provides Celery broker and session storage. Azure Blob Storage holds lease PDF documents.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, React Router v6, MSAL for Azure AD B2C, React Query, Tailwind CSS. Backend: Python 3.12, Django 5, Django REST Framework, Stripe Python SDK, Celery 5, django-jwt-auth, django-encrypted-fields. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server. Cache: Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis C1. Storage: Azure Blob Storage for lease documents. Auth: Azure AD B2C with Conditional Access MFA. Secrets: Azure Key Vault. Hosting: Azure App Service (B2), Azure Static Web Apps. CI/CD: Azure DevOps. Monitoring: Azure Monitor + Application Insights.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Tenant Profile: Azure AD B2C profile management with MFA enforcement, unit assignment, and contact details. Rent Payment: Stripe Elements form for ACH or card payment; payment_attempts tracking; tenant notification on payment failure. Maintenance Request: submit with category and photo upload; status tracking from submitted to assigned to in_progress to completed; Yardi CMMS auto-routing via Celery adapter. Lease Document Viewer: Azure Blob Storage access with PDF viewer; document version history. Building Announcements: building-scoped feed of notices and reminders; staff can publish announcements. Payment History: tenant-scoped ledger of all rent payments with status and amount.
parsed.system_components: Azure Static Web Apps (React frontend, CDN-served static assets). Azure App Service (Django backend, B2 SKU, auto-scale 1-4 instances). Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server (2 vCores, General Purpose, automated failover). Azure Cache for Redis 7 (C1, Celery broker and session store). Azure Blob Storage (lease documents and maintenance photos, versioning enabled). Azure Key Vault (Stripe API keys, Django SECRET_KEY, database password). Azure AD B2C tenant (MFA-enforced, Conditional Access). Azure AD B2C tenant (MFA-enforced, Conditional Access). Azure Container Registry (Docker image storage). Azure DevOps (CI/CD pipeline). Azure Monitor + Application Insights (Django backend metrics).
parsed.workflows: Rent Payment Flow: Tenant logs in via MSAL, navigates to /payments/new, selects ACH or card, Stripe Elements renders token form; payment_methods record created with type=ach or card, stripe_payment_method_id_encrypted stored via django-encrypted-fields, Stripe.pay() creates payment_intent; on success, payment_intent.confirm() creates rent_payment record with status=succeeded and paid_at; on failure, payment_intent.cancel() creates payment_attempts record with status=failed and tenant notification email sent via Azure Logic Apps. Maintenance Request Workflow: Tenant submits maintenance request with category and photo upload; Django view creates maintenance_request record with status=submitted; Celery adapter checks Yardi CMMS webhook endpoint for work order status updates; on Yardi response, status transitions to assigned, in_progress, or completed; Celery task sends staff email notification. Lease Document Access: Tenant opens /documents/lease; Azure Blob Storage returns lease PDF; tenant can download or view in-browser; document version history tracked in lease_documents table.
parsed.data_model: tenants (id UUID PK, azure_ad_oid VARCHAR UNIQUE, name, email, phone, unit_id UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). leases (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID FK, unit_id UUID FK, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, monthly_rent NUMERIC(10,2), document_blob_key VARCHAR, status ENUM active/expired). rent_payments (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID FK, lease_id UUID FK, amount NUMERIC(10,2), payment_method_id UUID FK, stripe_payment_intent_id VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/succeeded/failed, paid_at TIMESTAMPTZ). maintenance_requests (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID FK, unit_id UUID FK, category ENUM plumbing/electrical/hvac/general, description, photo_blob_keys ARRAY, status ENUM submitted/assigned/in_progress/completed, yardi_work_order_id VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). payment_methods (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID FK, type ENUM card/ach, stripe_payment_method_id_encrypted VARCHAR, last4, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). lease_documents (id UUID PK, lease_id UUID FK, doc_type ENUM lease/addendum/inspection, blob_key VARCHAR, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). building_announcements (id UUID PK, building_id UUID FK, title, body, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
parsed.api_design: Django REST Framework ViewSets with DRF router. GET /tenants/:id (tenant profile). POST /tenants (create tenant). GET /tenants/:id/leases (tenant's active leases). POST /tenants/:id/leases (create lease). GET /tenants/:id/payments?status= (payment history). POST /payments/new (initiate rent payment). GET /maintenance/requests?status= (maintenance request list). POST /maintenance/requests (submit maintenance request). GET /maintenance/requests/:id (maintenance request detail). POST /maintenance/requests/:id/assign (assign to staff). GET /documents/:lease_id/versions (document version history). POST /documents/:lease_id/versions (upload new document). GET /announcements?building_id= (building announcements). POST /announcements (publish announcement). All endpoints require Azure AD B2C JWT; Django csrf_exempt=False on all views.
parsed.security_and_compliance: Azure AD B2C MFA enforced via Conditional Access policy for all tenants. Azure Key Vault for all secrets: Stripe API keys, Django SECRET_KEY, database password, Yardi CMMS webhook endpoint. Azure App Service MSI (Managed Identity) for Key Vault access — no credentials in environment variables. django-encrypted-fields encrypts payment_methods.stripe_payment_method_id_encrypted at rest. PCI-DSS SAQ-D scope: Stripe payment_intent.confirm() triggers SAQ-D scope; Stripe.pay() returns payment_intent_id only. GDPR: tenant data retention 7 years; lease documents retained 3 years post-tenancy; communication logs retained 2 years. State landlord-tenant record requirements: all rent payment records and lease documents retained 7 years per state law.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps pipeline: lint (flake8, black check), test (pytest 80% coverage), Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry, deploy to App Service via az webapp deployment. React: eslint, jest, build, Azure Static Web Apps deploy action. Environments: dev (Azure Container Registry, Azure Static Web Apps dev URL), staging (production-equivalent, Yardi webhook adapter tested), production (App Service B2, Azure Static Web Apps production URL, Yardi webhook adapter tested). Rollback: App Service auto-deploy with rollback to previous revision. Secrets: Azure Key Vault versioning enabled; rotation tested before production payment launch.
parsed.observability: Azure Monitor + Application Insights: Django backend metrics (request count, payment_attempts count, maintenance_requests count, Yardi CMMS sync success rate). Custom alerts: payment_attempts rate > 2% in 1-hour window (PCI compliance check), maintenance_requests backlog > 50 unassigned (staff response time), Yardi CMMS sync failure rate > 5% (Yardi webhook adapter health check). Application Insights traces on Celery tasks for payment webhook and Yardi CMMS sync jobs.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $350/month: App Service B2 ($180), Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server General Purpose 2 vCores ($150), Azure Cache for Redis C1 ($50), Azure Blob Storage ($20), Azure Static Web Apps ($20), Azure Container Registry ($10). Scaling: App Service auto-scales 1-4 instances; PostgreSQL scales to 4 vCores if tenant count exceeds 500. Redis scales to 2 C1 instances if Celery task queue depth exceeds 1000.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Azure AD B2C tenant setup, React 18 SPA with MSAL, Django 5 backend with DRF, rent payment with Stripe Elements and field-level encryption, maintenance request with Celery Yardi adapter, lease document viewer, Azure Key Vault secrets, Azure DevOps CI/CD, PCI SAQ-D assessment. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Building announcement feed, payment history ledger, staff management dashboard, resident mobile app (React Native), package delivery notifications. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Resident mobile app (React Native), resident portal analytics, Yardi CMMS auto-routing optimization.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python: ruff for linting, black for formatting, mypy strict mode. Django: django-jwt-auth for JWT validation, django-encrypted-fields for payment_methods encryption, django-storages for Blob Storage. React: ESLint, Prettier, React Testing Library for component tests. pytest with 80% coverage gate. No hardcoded Stripe keys in code — all secrets in Azure Key Vault. Celery tasks must be idempotent; Yardi CMMS work order IDs are idempotent. PCI SAQ-D scope must be tested in staging before production payment launch.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Yardi CMMS webhook schema changes break auto-routing. Mitigation: dedicated webhook adapter service with schema validation; Yardi API documentation reviewed before each webhook schema change. Risk: Azure App Service auto-scale lag causes payment processing delays during peak rent payment window. Mitigation: Celery task retry with exponential backoff; payment_attempts record provides grace period. Tradeoff: Azure Static Web Apps over Azure App Service Plan for React SPA — lower cost at current tenant count; revisit if concurrent users exceed 500. Tradeoff: Django monolith over microservices — simpler for MVP but may require refactoring if tenant portal grows beyond 500 tenants.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Yardi webhook schema confirmed via Yardi API documentation review. Azure Key Vault chosen over Azure Container Registry for secrets — no container registry required for this project. Stripe Elements chosen over Stripe Checkout for simpler payment form. Celery chosen over Azure Logic Apps for Yardi CMMS sync — Celery provides task idempotency and retry semantics.

reference.thinking_summary: First-pass plan for a security-hardened tenant portal. Weak quality per manifest — plan sections are functional and correct but deliberately thinner than a strong plan. Key decisions: Django with DRF for rapid development, Azure AD B2C for MFA authentication, Stripe for PCI-compliant payment processing with SAQ-D scope managed through field-level encryption on stored ACH tokens. Yardi webhook integration needs a dedicated adapter to handle Yardi's unpredictable payload format.
reference.title: Security-Hardened Tenant Portal
reference.executive_summary: A fullstack tenant portal built with React 18 and Django 5 on Azure, enabling multifamily tenants to pay rent via ACH or card, submit maintenance requests, and access lease documents with MFA enforced by Azure AD B2C. PCI-DSS SAQ-D compliance is maintained through Stripe tokenization and field-level encryption on stored payment method tokens. Yardi CMMS integration routes maintenance requests to work orders via Celery async jobs.
reference.architecture_overview: Two-tier architecture: React 18 SPA on Azure Static Web Apps communicates over HTTPS to Django 5 REST API on Azure App Service. Tenants authenticate via Azure AD B2C with MFA; MSAL handles token refresh in the React client. Stripe Elements handles card and ACH tokenization in the browser — no raw payment credentials reach the Django server. Celery workers on a separate App Service process Stripe payment webhooks and sync maintenance request status from Yardi via outbound webhook calls. Lease documents stored in Azure Blob Storage with SAS URL generation for time-limited tenant access. Azure Key Vault holds all application secrets accessed via Managed Identity.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, React Router v6, React Query, MSAL for Azure AD B2C, Stripe.js Elements. Backend: Python 3.12, Django 5, Django REST Framework, Celery 5, django-encrypted-fields for payment token encryption, Stripe Python SDK. Database: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server. Cache/Queue: Azure Cache for Redis 7 (Celery broker + Django sessions). Storage: Azure Blob Storage for documents and maintenance photos. Auth: Azure AD B2C. Secrets: Azure Key Vault with MSI. CI/CD: Azure DevOps.
reference.functional_feature_map: Rent Payment: Stripe Elements form collects card or ACH; PaymentIntent confirmed server-side; payment_methods stored with field encryption; payment history in ledger. Maintenance Request: tenant submits category and description with photo uploads to Blob Storage; Celery job creates Yardi work order via API; tenant receives status updates as work order progresses. Lease Documents: tenant views and downloads PDFs from Azure Blob Storage via short-lived SAS URLs. Building Announcements: staff publishes notices visible to all tenants in assigned building. Staff Dashboard: management view of maintenance queue by building and assignment status.
reference.system_components: Azure Static Web Apps: React 18 SPA with Azure CDN. Azure App Service: Django 5 API (B2 SKU). Azure App Service Worker: Celery async jobs. Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16: primary datastore. Azure Cache for Redis 7: Celery broker and Django sessions. Azure Blob Storage: lease documents and maintenance photos. Azure Key Vault: Stripe keys, Django secret, DB password. Azure AD B2C: tenant and staff authentication with MFA.
reference.workflows: Rent Payment: tenant opens Pay Rent page; React renders Stripe Elements card or ACH form; on submit, Stripe.js creates PaymentMethod token; React POSTs token to POST /api/payments/; Django creates Stripe PaymentIntent, confirms charge, writes rent_payments record; Stripe webhook delivers payment_intent.succeeded to /api/webhooks/stripe/; Celery task updates rent_payments.status to succeeded and sends receipt email. Maintenance Request: tenant fills category, description, and uploads up to 3 photos; React POSTs to POST /api/maintenance/; Django stores maintenance_request record and uploads photos to Blob Storage; Celery job calls Yardi API to create work order and stores yardi_work_order_id; Yardi sends status webhook to /api/webhooks/yardi/ as work order progresses; Django updates maintenance_requests.status and emails tenant.
reference.data_model: tenants (id UUID PK, azure_ad_oid, name, email, phone, unit_id FK, created_at). leases (id UUID PK, tenant_id FK, unit_id FK, start_date, end_date, monthly_rent NUMERIC, document_blob_key, status ENUM active/expired). rent_payments (id UUID PK, tenant_id FK, lease_id FK, amount NUMERIC, payment_method_id FK, stripe_payment_intent_id, status ENUM pending/succeeded/failed, paid_at). maintenance_requests (id UUID PK, tenant_id FK, unit_id FK, category ENUM plumbing/electrical/hvac/general, description, photo_blob_keys JSONB, status ENUM submitted/assigned/in_progress/completed, yardi_work_order_id, created_at). payment_methods (id UUID PK, tenant_id FK, type ENUM card/ach, stripe_payment_method_id_encrypted BYTEA, last4, created_at, deleted_at). lease_documents (id UUID PK, lease_id FK, doc_type ENUM lease/addendum/inspection, blob_key, uploaded_at). building_announcements (id UUID PK, building_id FK, title, body, published_at).
reference.api_design: GET /api/tenants/me/ — tenant profile. GET /api/leases/current/ — active lease details. POST /api/payments/ — initiate rent payment with Stripe token. GET /api/payments/ — payment history. POST /api/maintenance/ — submit maintenance request. GET /api/maintenance/ — tenant's request list. GET /api/documents/ — lease document list with SAS URL. GET /api/announcements/ — building announcements. POST /api/webhooks/stripe/ — Stripe event receiver (signature verified). POST /api/webhooks/yardi/ — Yardi work order status receiver (HMAC verified).
reference.security_and_compliance: Azure AD B2C MFA enforced for all tenants and staff. MSAL handles silent token refresh; tokens expire after 1 hour. PCI-DSS SAQ-D: Stripe Elements tokenizes card and ACH in browser; Django stores only stripe_payment_method_id encrypted via django-encrypted-fields with Azure Key Vault key. Django CSRF middleware enabled on all non-webhook endpoints. Webhook endpoints use Stripe signature verification and Yardi HMAC. Azure Key Vault MSI for secret access — no credentials in App Service configuration. Object-level permission checks on all DRF ViewSets ensure tenants cannot access other tenants' records. Azure App Service TLS minimum version 1.2.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps CI pipeline: flake8 lint, pytest with 80% coverage gate, build Docker image, push to Azure Container Registry. CD: az webapp deployment container config to deploy to App Service. React pipeline: eslint, jest unit tests, bun run build, Azure Static Web Apps deploy action. Environments: dev (App Service F1 free tier, PostgreSQL Burstable B1ms), staging (B1 + General Purpose 2 vCore), production (B2 + General Purpose 2 vCore, high-availability enabled). Database migrations via Django management command in release phase. Secrets rotated via Azure Key Vault 90-day rotation policy.
reference.observability: Azure Application Insights SDK in Django for request tracing and exception tracking. Custom events: payment_succeeded, payment_failed, maintenance_request_created, yardi_sync_failed. Azure Monitor alerts: payment failure rate > 2% triggers PagerDuty; Celery queue depth > 100 jobs alerts ops channel; PostgreSQL CPU > 80% for 10 minutes alerts DBA.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Azure App Service B2 ~$75/month. PostgreSQL General Purpose 2 vCore ~$140/month. Azure Cache for Redis C1 ~$55/month. Azure Blob Storage ~$5/month. Total ~$275/month for MVP. Scaling: App Service scale-out to 3 instances on CPU 70% target covers rent payment peak on the 1st of each month.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): React SPA with MSAL auth, rent payment via Stripe, maintenance request submission, lease document viewer, Django API, Yardi webhook adapter, PCI pre-assessment. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Staff management dashboard, building announcements, payment history analytics, Celery monitoring dashboard. Phase 3 (future): Mobile app, smart lock integration, amenity booking.
reference.development_guidelines: Python: black formatter, flake8, mypy strict mode. Django: DRF serializers for all API inputs; no direct model access in views. Field-level encryption applied to all payment_methods.stripe_payment_method_id fields via django-encrypted-fields decorator. PR requires security team review for any payment or auth code changes. No payment data in application logs.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Yardi API reliability — Yardi webhooks are documented inconsistently and sometimes drop events. Mitigation: Celery periodic reconciliation job polls Yardi work order status for open maintenance requests every 15 minutes as a fallback. Risk: PCI SAQ-D scope creep if additional payment methods added beyond Stripe-tokenized card and ACH. Mitigation: all payment method handling routed through Stripe SDK; no direct card data processing. Tradeoff: Django monolith chosen over microservices for MVP development speed; module boundaries maintained for future service extraction.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Yardi webhook HMAC secret obtained from property management team. Stripe ACH tokenization uses Stripe Financial Connections for bank account verification. Azure AD B2C selected over Entra External ID for tenant auth due to existing B2C tenant in org. Lease document SAS URL expiry set to 15 minutes to minimize exposure window.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve the two outstanding issues: specify the saga pattern (orchestrator-based with saga-orchestrator-svc) and define the Kafka topic schema and partition strategy. The data_model section must be corrected to separate Kafka topics from database entities. The plan quality is intentionally thin; the research findings section is the primary deliverable and the plan quality is acceptable for a research prototype.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified orchestrator-based saga with saga-orchestrator-svc as a dedicated service; defined compensation events for each downstream failure case: revert_document_revision for scheduling, revert_cost_commitments for cost-mgmt-svc. Saga orchestration logic now flows through saga-orchestrator-svc.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Saga pattern is now specified; the cross-service consistency implementation is unambiguous

fix_report.action_taken: Defined Kafka topics: document_revised (3 partitions, key=doc_id, Avro schema), document_revised_dlq (dead-letter topic), scheduling_ack (1 partition), cost_ack (1 partition), document_revised_ack (1 partition). Partition count and key strategy chosen to support eventual consistency window of 2-3 seconds.

reference.thinking_summary: This is a weak revision round for a microservices research prototype. ISS-001 is addressed: orchestrator-based saga with saga-orchestrator-svc is chosen, with compensating revert_document_revision events on downstream failure. ISS-002 is addressed: Kafka topics specified with partition counts and Avro schema format. However, plan quality is intentionally weak: the analytics_and_benchmarking section is thin (does not detail the k6 load test scenarios or the specific monolith baseline comparison methodology), and the phased_implementation section is at minimum word count.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified orchestrator-based saga pattern with dedicated saga-orchestrator-svc tracking saga_transactions; defined compensation event revert_document_revision emitted on downstream service timeout or nack.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Cross-service transaction pattern is now implementable; saga orchestrator tracks state in saga_transactions table with rollback path defined

fix_report.action_taken: Defined Kafka topics: document_revised (3 partitions, key=doc_id), scheduling_ack (1 partition), cost_ack (1 partition), document_revised_dlq (1 partition); Avro schema with Schema Registry chosen; consistency_audit_logs.resolution_lag_ms defined as downstream ack timestamp minus event publish timestamp.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Kafka topic design now implementable; resolution lag measurement methodology defined for research outputs

reference.title: Construction Platform Microservices Decomposition Research Architecture v2
reference.executive_summary: A research prototype on GKE Autopilot decomposing a monolithic construction project management platform into three services (document-control-svc, scheduling-svc, cost-mgmt-svc) communicating over a Kafka 3.6 event bus. An orchestrator-based saga pattern manages cross-domain transactions with compensating events on failure. Per-service PostgreSQL schemas enforce data isolation boundaries. Prometheus metrics capture inter-service event latency, saga transaction duration, and eventual-consistency resolution lag for comparison against a monolith baseline under equivalent synthetic k6 load, producing an architecture decision record for the enterprise committee.
reference.architecture_overview: Three bounded-context services deployed on GKE Autopilot in a research namespace: document-control-svc (Go 1.22) owns document_revisions in document_schema; scheduling-svc (Go 1.22) owns schedule_baselines in scheduling_schema; cost-mgmt-svc (Python 3.12) owns cost_commitments in cost_schema. A dedicated saga-orchestrator-svc (Go 1.22) receives publish_document commands, coordinates the cross-service transaction by emitting document_revised to Kafka (key=doc_id, 3 partitions), awaiting scheduling_ack and cost_ack events within a 30-second window, and marking the saga complete or emitting revert_document_revision on timeout or nack. Kafka 3.6 on Confluent Cloud with Avro schemas managed in Schema Registry. consistency_audit_logs in a shared analytics schema records the resolution_lag_ms from event publish to downstream ack for each service, providing the primary research metric. Prometheus scrapes custom metrics from each service; Grafana renders saga timeline and consistency latency distribution dashboards. A Python 3.12 analytics script aggregates audit logs into weekly benchmark comparison reports against the monolith baseline.
reference.technology_stack: Services: Go 1.22 (document-control-svc, scheduling-svc, saga-orchestrator-svc), Python 3.12 (cost-mgmt-svc, analytics scripts). Event bus: Kafka 3.6 on Confluent Cloud with Avro schema serialisation and Schema Registry. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL (four isolated schemas via separate PostgreSQL roles). Orchestration: GKE Autopilot with GitLab CI. Observability: Prometheus + Grafana on GKE, custom metrics per service. Load testing: k6 with synthetic construction project event scripts.
reference.functional_feature_map: Document Revision Publication: saga-orchestrator-svc receives publish_document command, document-control-svc writes document_revision and emits document_revised Avro event. Downstream Consistency Updates: scheduling-svc and cost-mgmt-svc consume document_revised and update task_dependencies and cost_code_mappings respectively, emitting ack events. Saga Rollback: orchestrator emits revert_document_revision if any downstream service times out or returns a nack event; services revert their local changes. Consistency Measurement: consistency_audit_logs records event publish_at and downstream service ack_at timestamps; resolution_lag_ms computed per saga step. Research Benchmarks: k6 load test runs against microservices cluster and monolith baseline.
reference.system_components: document-control-svc (Go, Kafka producer, document_schema owner), scheduling-svc (Go, Kafka consumer for document_revised, scheduling_schema owner), cost-mgmt-svc (Python, Kafka consumer for document_revised, cost_schema owner), saga-orchestrator-svc (Go, Kafka producer of document_revised and revert_document_revision, Kafka consumer of scheduling_ack and cost_ack, saga_schema owner), Kafka 3.6 Confluent Cloud (document_revised 3-partition topic, scheduling_ack, cost_ack, document_revised_dlq), Schema Registry (Avro schemas), PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL (four schemas), Prometheus + Grafana, k6 load tester.
reference.workflows: Cross-Service Document Publish: Research engineer calls POST /simulate on saga-orchestrator-svc with a publish_document payload; orchestrator writes saga_transactions record with status pending, calls document-control-svc to create a document_revision, then produces an Avro document_revised event to Kafka with doc_id as partition key. scheduling-svc consumer group processes the event, updates schedule_baselines.task_dependencies to reference the new revision, writes a consistency_audit_log entry (service=scheduling, resolution_lag_ms=event.ack_at - event.publish_at), and emits scheduling_ack to Kafka. cost-mgmt-svc Python consumer similarly updates cost_commitments and emits cost_ack. Orchestrator receives both acks, marks saga_transaction completed. Saga Rollback: if cost-mgmt-svc consumer raises an exception (simulated via a chaos flag in /simulate payload), orchestrator receives no cost_ack within 30 seconds; it emits revert_document_revision event; document-control-svc and scheduling-svc consumers handle the revert by deleting the provisional changes; orchestrator marks saga_transaction rolled_back.
reference.data_model: document_schema.document_revisions (id UUID PK, doc_id UUID, revision_number INT, content_hash TEXT, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ, saga_id UUID). scheduling_schema.schedule_baselines (id UUID PK, project_id UUID, task_dependencies JSONB, last_sync_doc_revision_id UUID, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). cost_schema.cost_commitments (id UUID PK, project_id UUID, cost_code TEXT, amount NUMERIC, linked_doc_revision_id UUID, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). saga_schema.saga_transactions (id UUID PK, command_type TEXT, status ENUM pending/completed/rolled_back, initiated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, participant_acks JSONB). analytics_schema.consistency_audit_logs (id UUID PK, saga_id UUID FK, service_name TEXT, event_type TEXT, event_publish_at TIMESTAMPTZ, service_ack_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolution_lag_ms INT). Kafka topics: document_revised (Avro: {doc_id: string, revision_id: string, content_hash: string, publish_at: long}), scheduling_ack and cost_ack (Avro: {saga_id: string, service: string, status: enum, ack_at: long}), document_revised_dlq.
reference.api_design: Minimal REST on each service for research tooling: GET /health (liveness check); GET /metrics (Prometheus exposition format); POST /simulate on saga-orchestrator-svc accepts {doc_id: string, inject_failure: bool} to trigger a publish_document saga with optional downstream failure injection for rollback testing. Python analytics script exposes no HTTP API; it queries consistency_audit_logs directly via SQL and outputs Markdown benchmark tables.
reference.security_and_compliance: Service-to-service JWT using a shared HS256 signing key distributed via Kubernetes Secret; each service validates the JWT on incoming REST calls. Kafka consumers use Confluent Cloud API keys stored in Kubernetes Secrets. Kubernetes NetworkPolicy restricts: document-control-svc can reach PostgreSQL document_schema port only; scheduling-svc to scheduling_schema; cost-mgmt-svc to cost_schema; saga-orchestrator-svc to saga_schema. No external ingress; research engineers access services via kubectl port-forward. Shared PostgreSQL cluster is a prototype simplification; finding will note that production requires separate databases per service to achieve true data isolation.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot with four Deployments and one ConfigMap per service. GitLab CI: go test, golangci-lint, pytest, Docker build, push to GCR, kubectl apply --dry-run in staging then apply in research namespace. Benchmark pipeline: GitLab scheduled job runs k6 against microservices and monolith endpoints weekly, stores JSON results in GCS, triggers Python analytics script to produce Markdown comparison report committed to the research repo.
reference.observability: Prometheus ServiceMonitors scrape /metrics from each service. Custom Prometheus histograms: document_event_publish_latency_seconds, saga_transaction_duration_seconds, consistency_resolution_lag_seconds labelled by service_name. Grafana dashboard panels: Saga Transaction Timeline (Gantt-style per saga step), Consistency Resolution Latency Distribution (histogram by service), DLQ Depth over time. Research output: weekly k6 benchmark comparison showing microservices p50/p99 latency vs monolith baseline under 100 concurrent synthetic document publish events.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated USD 310/month: Confluent Cloud Kafka basic USD 60/month, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL db.f1-micro USD 80/month, GKE Autopilot 4 lightweight services USD 120/month, GCS for benchmark results USD 5/month, Grafana Cloud free tier. Under USD 500/month budget. Load test burst costs covered by budget buffer. Prototype is not designed for scale; GKE Autopilot handles the 4 services without manual node management.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (4 weeks): Scaffold three services, PostgreSQL schema setup, Kafka topics and Avro schemas, document_revised event flow end-to-end, consistency_audit_logs recording. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Saga orchestrator with rollback path, chaos failure injection via /simulate, k6 load test scripts and monolith baseline comparison runs, Grafana dashboards. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Analytics script producing comparative benchmark report, architecture decision record draft, operational complexity findings documented.
reference.development_guidelines: Go services use golangci-lint with errcheck and staticcheck. Python cost-mgmt-svc uses ruff and mypy. All Kafka consumer groups use explicit consumer group IDs to prevent partition assignment conflicts. Each service owns its schema migration files; no cross-schema migrations allowed. Research findings documented in /docs/findings at end of each sprint. Avro schema changes versioned in Schema Registry with backward-compatibility enforcement.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Confluent Cloud Kafka on basic tier has limited partition throughput; insufficient for production-scale document events but adequate for synthetic research load of 100 concurrent events. Risk: shared PostgreSQL cluster undermines true data isolation; findings must note this limitation explicitly. Tradeoff: orchestrator-based saga over choreography chosen because orchestrator state in saga_transactions table makes rollback paths observable and testable, which is more valuable for research insights than choreography's lower coupling. Risk: GKE Autopilot node provisioning latency inflates p99 latency measurements during cold-start periods; pre-warm jobs added to benchmark pipeline.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Orchestrator-based saga chosen over choreography for research observability: saga_transactions table makes every transaction step queryable. Avro with Schema Registry chosen over JSON for schema evolution research as an additional data point. Synthetic data seeded from anonymised production project snapshots; real project data not used. PostgreSQL shared cluster confirmed acceptable for prototype; production recommendation will specify separate databases per service.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 3 must resolve the emergency access timer persistence gap (ISS-001). The fix is to store all pending emergency access requests in audit.db with requested_at and expiry_at timestamps, and on app start query all requests and enforce the 24h window without any in-memory timer. The ECDH key agreement for team vault key sharing is also deferred to phase 2. The plan quality is intentionally weak per manifest: the security_and_compliance section is thin and the deployment_and_operations section is minimal.

fix_report.action_taken: Added emergency_access_requests table in audit.db with requested_at and expiry_at DATETIME columns. On app start, Rust vault_service queries all rows where expiry_at < now() and evaluates (now - expiry_at) >= approval_delay_hours. If any request exceeds the window, the app displays a warning and aborts the access request. No in-memory timer is used.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Emergency access approval window is enforced across app restarts; no in-memory timer is required

parsed.title: Hardened Desktop Credential Manager
parsed.executive_summary: A Tauri-based desktop application for macOS, Windows, and Linux that stores passwords, OTP seeds, and SSH keys in a hardware-backed SQLCipher vault with NIST SP 800-132 key derivation. Round 3 resolves the emergency access timer persistence gap by storing all pending requests in audit.db with timestamp-based enforcement. Team vault sharing uses ECDH per-member encryption of the team vault key.
parsed.architecture_overview: Tauri desktop shell with Svelte+TypeScript frontend communicating over a local Unix domain socket (macOS/Linux) or named pipe (Windows) to a Rust Axum IPC server. The Axum server owns a SQLCipher vault database (vault.db) and an audit database (audit.db) with independent keys derived from the master key. Vault entries are AES-256-GCM encrypted with a per-entry key derived from the master key via PBKDF2-SHA256. Emergency access requests are stored in audit.db with requested_at and expiry_at timestamps and enforced by a timestamp-based window check on app start. Team vault sharing uses ECDH per-member encryption of the team vault key. No cloud server or sync service exists for vault data.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Tauri 1.x, Svelte 3, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, ZeroizingVec for plaintext field zeroing. Backend: Rust 1.78, Axum 0.7, SQLCipher crate, argon2 crate, ring crate, serde_json, Tauri command macros. Database: SQLite with SQLCipher AES-256-CBC, page_size=4096, key derived from master key. Audit: SQLite with SQLCipher AES-256-CBC, independent key XOR audit_key_salt. Crypto: ring crate for PBKDF2-SHA256, argon2 crate for PIN stretching, OpenSSL crate for ECDH. CI: GitHub Actions with cross-platform matrix. Signing: Apple notarisation, Windows Authenticode, Linux GPG.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Vault Unlock: OS biometric dialog (TouchID/FaceID/Windows Hello) or PIN with argon2id stretching. Vault Entry CRUD: create password/OTP seed/SSH key entry with AES-256-GCM per-entry encryption. Team Vault: share vault with other users via ECDH per-member encryption. Emergency Access: request access from another user; owner approves or denies; 24h window enforced by timestamp check on app start. Audit Log: query audit_log_entries by action and entry_id. Settings: biometric fingerprint storage, screen capture protection flag.
parsed.system_components: Tauri shell (desktop window, OS dialogs, IPC server). Svelte+TypeScript frontend SPA (vault views, entry detail, team vault panel, emergency access panel, audit log viewer). Rust Axum IPC server (vault_service module, SQLCipher connection, audit.db access, ECDH key agreement for team vault). SQLite vault.db (AES-256-GCM encrypted, page_size=4096, key derived from master key). SQLite audit.db (AES-256-CBC encrypted, independent key XOR audit_key_salt, hash chain). ECDH key ring (per-member public key pairs for team vault).
parsed.workflows: Vault Unlock: OS biometric dialog (TouchID/FaceID/Windows Hello) or PIN entry with argon2id(PIN, salt=device_id_hash, m=65536, t=3, p=4) produces 32-byte key; master key wrapped with AES-256-GCM; vault_metadata table updated with vault_mac_computed and vault_mac_computed_prev; vault_entries table updated with ciphertext; ZeroizingVec fetches plaintext from IPC; vault_service returns DecryptedEntry. Emergency Access: user sends emergency_access_request command; Rust vault_service writes row to audit.db with requested_at and expiry_at; on app start, Rust queries all rows where expiry_at < now() and (now - expiry_at) >= approval_delay_hours; if any request exceeds window, app displays warning and aborts; if no requests exceed window, app proceeds to ECDH key agreement for team vault key; owner receives ECDH public key; owner opens team vault, enters ECDH public key, vault_service encrypts team vault key with owner's public key using ECDH; team vault key stored in team_vaults table; team vault key is never stored in plaintext on sync server.
parsed.data_model: vault_entries (id UUID PK, entry_type ENUM password/otp_seed/ssh_key, title VARCHAR, username VARCHAR, url VARCHAR, notes_encrypted BLOB, ciphertext BLOB, created_at DATETIME, updated_at DATETIME). otp_seeds (id UUID PK, entry_id UUID FK, seed_encrypted BLOB, issuer VARCHAR, digits INT DEFAULT 6, period INT DEFAULT 30). ssh_key_pairs (id UUID PK, entry_id UUID FK, private_key_encrypted BLOB, public_key TEXT, key_type ENUM ed25519/rsa4096, fingerprint VARCHAR). team_vaults (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, owner_id VARCHAR, created_at DATETIME). access_grants (id UUID PK, vault_id UUID FK, grantee_identity VARCHAR, role ENUM owner/editor/viewer, encrypted_vault_key BLOB, granted_at DATETIME). audit_log_entries (id UUID PK, action ENUM unlock/lock/read_entry/write_entry/share_vault/emergency_access_request/emergency_access_approved/emergency_access_denied, entry_id UUID nullable, actor_identity VARCHAR, timestamp DATETIME, prev_hash VARCHAR, row_hash VARCHAR).
parsed.api_design: Tauri invoke() commands: vault:unlock (biometric/pin), vault:create_entry (entry_type, title, username, url), vault:read_entry (entry_id), vault:delete_entry (entry_id), vault:list_entries (entry_type), vault:list_entries_by_type (type ENUM password/otp_seed/ssh_key), vault:list_team_vaults, vault:share_vault (grantee_identity, vault_id), vault:emergency_access_request (grantee_identity, reason), vault:emergency_access_approved (entry_id), vault:emergency_access_denied (entry_id). Audit: audit:query_log (action, entry_id, actor_identity, timestamp), audit:query_log_by_action (action, timestamp). Team: team:list_members, team:add_member (identity), team:remove_member (identity). Emergency: emergency:request_access (grantee_identity, reason), emergency:approve_access (entry_id), emergency:deny_access (entry_id).
parsed.security_and_compliance: NIST SP 800-132: master key derived from TPM/Secure Enclave via PBKDF2-SHA256 with 65536 iterations, 32-byte key; vault key wrapped with AES-256-GCM; vault_metadata table stores vault_mac_computed and vault_mac_computed_prev. Audit.db: independent key XOR audit_key_salt; hash chain: each row includes prev_hash = SHA256(prev_row.action + prev_row.entry_id + prev_row.actor_identity + prev_row.timestamp); chain verified on every open. Vault MAC: vault_metadata updated with vault_mac_computed and vault_mac_computed_prev; verified on every open via SHA256(vault_mac_computed + vault_mac_computed_prev) == vault_metadata.vault_mac_computed. FIPS 140-3: NIST SP 800-132 key derivation, SQLCipher AES-256-CBC, ECDH per-member encryption for team vault key, audit.db hash chain, vault MAC on every open. Corporate secret storage: vault key never transmitted to servers; all audit_log_entries include actor_identity and timestamp to prevent deletion by compromise.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions matrix: macOS universal binary (lipo x86_64+aarch64), Windows x64 MSVC, Linux x64 AppImage and .deb. Code-signed on each platform: Apple notarisation (macOS), Windows Authenticode (Windows), Linux GPG (Linux). Release artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases. Auto-update via Tauri updater with signature check. Rollback: vault schema migrations are additive-only; rollback to previous app version always succeeds on existing vault schema. CI: lint (clippy + eslint), test (cargo test + vitest), build (tauri build per platform), sign+notarize (macOS), package (AppImage, .deb, .exe, .dmg), release (GitHub Releases).
parsed.observability: Local structured audit log file (audit.db) with SHA256 hash chain. Sentry (no vault data in reports) captures audit log entries with actor_identity and timestamp. Crash reports via Sentry (no vault data in reports) include Tauri IPC command stack. No remote metrics or dashboards. Sentry data scrubbed before release artifacts.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost; local SQLite and SQLCipher. GitHub Actions free tier covers CI for open-source. Apple notarisation and Windows Authenticode free for open-source. Linux GPG free for open-source. Tauri updater free for open-source. Scaling: SQLite vault.db supports up to 10,000 entries without query performance issues; team vault sync is P2P encrypted blob exchange with no central server bottleneck.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 months): personal vault with TPM unlock, password and OTP seed storage, SQLCipher encryption, local audit log, auto-lock on idle, Tauri cross-platform build. Phase 2 (3 months): emergency access delegation with ECDH per-member encryption, team vault sharing, E2EE sync, SSH agent integration. Phase 3 (3 months): browser extension autofill, SOC 2 certification, FIDO2 hardware key auto-enrollment.
parsed.development_guidelines: Rust: clippy with deny(warnings) and deny(warnings) enforced in CI. All SQLCipher operations wrapped in a single transaction. ECDH key agreement must use a cryptographically secure key exchange protocol (X25519) and never use a weak key exchange (RSA-OAEP). Vault MAC must be verified on every open via SHA256 hash chain check. No vault data in Sentry or crash reports. All Tauri IPC commands must have a Rust command macro definition.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TPM unavailability on VM or containers falls back to PIN-only; argon2id parameters must be enforced in UI. Mitigation: argon2id parameters documented in UI; PIN complexity requirements enforced in Rust vault_service. Risk: SQLCipher key compromise allows attacker to delete audit log entries; mitigation is audit.db hash chain with SHA256 verification. Tradeoff: ECDH per-member encryption for team vault key adds complexity but eliminates the risk of a compromised team vault key being shared with an attacker. Tradeoff: Tauri IPC over a native process adds a network round-trip for every command but provides better cross-platform portability.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Emergency access approval window enforced by timestamp check on app start (ISS-001 resolved). ECDH per-member encryption for team vault key (ISS-002 deferred to Phase 2). Tauri IPC over native process for cross-platform portability (ISS-001 resolved). Audit.db hash chain with SHA256 verification (ISS-003 resolved).

reference.thinking_summary: Resolving ISS-001 by replacing the in-memory emergency access timer with a persistent timestamp-based approach: emergency_access_requests stored as rows in audit.db with requested_at and approval_delay_hours. On every app start, the vault_service queries pending emergency_access_requests and evaluates (now - requested_at) >= approval_delay_hours; if elapsed, the access is auto-approved or expired depending on owner response. This design is restart-safe because the state lives in audit.db, not in memory. Also specifying X25519 ECDH for team vault key handoff to close the key sharing gap from round 2.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced in-memory emergency access timer with persistent emergency_access_requests table in audit.db storing requested_at DATETIME and approval_delay_hours INT. On app start, vault_service queries SELECT * FROM emergency_access_requests WHERE status = 'pending' and evaluates (julianday('now') - julianday(requested_at)) * 24 >= approval_delay_hours. Expired pending requests auto-resolve. Notifications sent to vault owner via OS notification on app start if action required.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Emergency access approval window is enforced correctly even after multiple app restarts between request submission and expiry, preserving the security guarantee of the time-delay mechanism.

reference.title: Hardened Desktop Credential Manager
reference.executive_summary: A Tauri and Svelte desktop application with a Rust Axum IPC backend storing passwords, OTP seeds, and SSH keys in SQLCipher-encrypted local vaults. Master key is sealed to device TPM or Secure Enclave; fallback uses argon2id PIN stretching. Team vault sharing uses X25519 ECDH per-member key encryption. Emergency access requests persist across app restarts via audit.db timestamp tracking. Distributed as signed installers for macOS, Windows, and Linux.
reference.architecture_overview: Local-only desktop architecture: Tauri shell hosts Svelte TypeScript UI and communicates with a Rust Axum command handler via Tauri invoke() over a local Unix domain socket (macOS/Linux) or named pipe (Windows). vault_service module holds in-memory decrypted vault state and owns two SQLCipher databases: vault.db for credential entries and audit.db for append-only audit log and emergency access state. Master key derived from TPM Seal/Unseal (Secure Enclave on macOS, NCrypt TPM 2.0 on Windows) or argon2id from PIN as fallback. No network calls required for core vault; team sync uses direct E2EE blob exchange.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Tauri 2.x, Svelte 4, TypeScript strict mode, Tauri plugin for biometric and OS keychain. Backend: Rust stable, Axum 0.7, SQLCipher via rusqlite with sqlcipher feature, ring crate (AES-256-GCM), argon2 crate (argon2id), x25519-dalek (team vault ECDH). Build: GitHub Actions cross-platform matrix. Packaging: tauri-bundler (macOS dmg universal, Windows NSIS, Linux AppImage and .deb). Crash reporting: Sentry Rust SDK with data scrubbing.
reference.functional_feature_map: Personal Vault: create/read/update/delete entries of type password, otp_seed, ssh_key with AES-256-GCM per-entry encryption. TOTP Generator: computes TOTP code from decrypted otp_seed with 30s countdown display; clipboard cleared after 30s. SSH Key Viewer: displays public key fingerprint; private key accessible only via copy-to-clipboard. Team Vault: share vault key to member using X25519 ECDH; member roles owner/editor/viewer enforced in Rust. Emergency Access: request access with configurable delay (24-72h); request persisted in audit.db; owner approves or denies on next app open within window. Audit Log Viewer: query audit_log_entries with hash chain integrity check.
reference.system_components: Tauri shell: window management, biometric plugin (macOS LocalAuthentication, Windows Windows Hello), OS notification. Svelte UI: VaultUnlock, EntryList, EntryDetail, TeamVaultPanel, EmergencyAccessPanel, AuditLogViewer. Rust vault_service: SQLCipher pool for vault.db and audit.db, in-memory decrypted entry cache (zeroed on lock), emergency access request evaluator running on startup. ring crate: AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt, HKDF for key derivation. argon2 crate: PIN stretching. x25519-dalek: team vault ECDH key agreement. audit_service: append-only writes with hash chain computation.
reference.workflows: Vault Unlock: Tauri invokes biometric OS dialog; on success calls CryptoKit/NCrypt to unseal master key from TPM; vault_service opens vault.db and audit.db with SQLCipher key derived from master key; verifies vault MAC; queries emergency_access_requests for pending items requiring owner action; emits OS notification if action needed. Emergency Access Request: requestor sends request with requested_at = now() stored in emergency_access_requests table (audit.db) with status=pending and approval_delay_hours; vault owner receives OS notification on next app open; if (now - requested_at) >= approval_delay_hours and owner has not denied, vault_service generates time-limited access token encrypted with requestor's public key. Team Vault Key Share: owner calls EmergencyAccessCommand::share; vault_service generates ephemeral X25519 keypair, derives shared_secret = ECDH(owner_private, member_public), encrypts vault_key with AES-256-GCM using shared_secret, stores encrypted_vault_key in access_grants.
reference.data_model: vault.db - vault_entries (id UUID PK, entry_type ENUM password/otp_seed/ssh_key, title VARCHAR, username VARCHAR, url VARCHAR, nonce BLOB 12 bytes, ciphertext BLOB, created_at DATETIME, updated_at DATETIME). otp_seeds (id UUID PK, entry_id FK, nonce BLOB, seed_encrypted BLOB, issuer VARCHAR, digits INT DEFAULT 6, period INT DEFAULT 30). ssh_key_pairs (id UUID PK, entry_id FK, nonce BLOB, private_key_encrypted BLOB, public_key TEXT, key_type ENUM ed25519/rsa4096, fingerprint CHAR 47). team_vaults (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, owner_identity VARCHAR, created_at DATETIME). access_grants (id UUID PK, vault_id FK, grantee_identity VARCHAR, role ENUM owner/editor/viewer, ecdh_wrapped_vault_key BLOB, granted_at DATETIME). vault_metadata (key VARCHAR PK, value TEXT) holding vault_mac and schema_version. audit.db - audit_log_entries (id UUID PK, action ENUM, entry_id UUID nullable, actor_identity VARCHAR, timestamp DATETIME, prev_hash CHAR 64, row_hash CHAR 64). emergency_access_requests (id UUID PK, requestor_identity VARCHAR, requestor_public_key BLOB, approval_delay_hours INT, requested_at DATETIME, status ENUM pending/approved/denied/expired, resolved_at DATETIME nullable).
reference.api_design: Tauri invoke commands - vault:unlock(pin_or_biometric) -> VaultStatus. vault:create_entry(entry_type, fields) -> EntryId. vault:read_entry(entry_id) -> DecryptedEntry (zeroed after use). vault:delete_entry(entry_id) -> void. team:share_vault(vault_id, member_identity, member_public_key, role) -> GrantId. team:revoke_member(grant_id) -> void. emergency:request_access(vault_id, requestor_public_key, delay_hours) -> RequestId. emergency:approve_access(request_id) -> EncryptedAccessToken. emergency:deny_access(request_id) -> void. audit:query_log(filters) -> AuditLogPage with chain_valid bool.
reference.security_and_compliance: NIST SP 800-132 compliance: master key derived via PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 on PIN fallback with 600,000 iterations (FIPS 140-3 guidance). TPM path: Secure Enclave CryptoKit on macOS, NCrypt TPM 2.0 on Windows. Per-entry encryption: AES-256-GCM with unique 96-bit nonce per write. Vault MAC: HMAC-SHA256 over all (entry_id, updated_at) pairs; recomputed and verified on every vault open. Audit hash chain: row_hash = SHA256(prev_hash || action || entry_id || actor_identity || timestamp); chain verified on every audit.db open. Emergency access timer: persisted in audit.db; app-restart-safe. Team vault key sharing: X25519 ECDH shared secret -> AES-256-GCM encrypted vault key; owner private key never transmitted. Clipboard cleared after 30s. Screen capture protection on sensitive views via Tauri setContentProtection(true).
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions matrix: [macos-14 (aarch64), macos-13 (x86_64)], [windows-2022], [ubuntu-22.04, ubuntu-22.04-arm64]. Steps: clippy lint, cargo test (unit + integration with in-memory SQLCipher), vitest for Svelte components, tauri build, lipo universal binary for macOS. Release tags trigger: Apple notarisation + staple (macOS), Azure Code Signing (Windows), GPG sign AppImage (Linux). Artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases. Auto-update manifest signed with Tauri updater key pair.
reference.observability: Local audit log in audit.db with hash chain provides tamper-evident access history. Sentry Rust SDK for crash reports: DSN configured in Tauri conf; data scrubbing rules strip any string matching UUID patterns from stack frames. App version and OS version sent with crashes; no vault data. Structured tracing logs via tracing crate (info level) written to local log file with 30-day rotation.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost for core vault; local-only. GitHub Actions free tier covers cross-platform build matrix. Apple Developer Program ($99/yr) required for notarisation. Windows EV certificate for Authenticode (~$300/yr). SQLCipher handles up to 10,000 entries with sub-50ms query time on consumer hardware.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): personal vault unlock via TPM/PIN, password and OTP seed CRUD with AES-256-GCM, local audit.db with hash chain, auto-lock on 15min idle, signed installers for macOS and Windows. Phase 2 (6 weeks): SSH key storage, team vault sharing with X25519 ECDH, emergency access with persistent timer, team RBAC. Phase 3 (4 weeks): browser extension autofill, SSH agent integration, FIDO2 hardware key unlock factor.
reference.development_guidelines: Rust: clippy deny(warnings), cargo test with coverage via cargo-tarpaulin targeting 85% line coverage. Secrets never in Rust String type; use ZeroizeVec from zeroize crate for plaintext buffers. Svelte: TypeScript strict, vitest for component logic. No vault plaintext in Svelte stores; fetch on-demand via IPC. PR requires security team review for any change to vault_service or audit_service modules.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TPM unavailable in VM environments; argon2id fallback with enforced PIN complexity mitigates brute-force. Risk: vault file loss without backup; mitigated by prompted portable export to encrypted USB. Tradeoff: SQLCipher over age-encrypted flat file chosen for relational query capability needed by audit log and team vault features.
