Risk: Apple notarisation delay blocking release; mitigated by pre-notarising release candidates before tag.
reference.open_questions_resolved: X25519 ECDH chosen over Signal double ratchet for team vault key sharing; double ratchet is over-engineered for vault key exchange which does not require forward secrecy on every message. Emergency access timer persistence chosen via audit.db rows rather than OS scheduled tasks to maintain single source of truth and audit trail. argon2id parameters set to m=65536, t=3, p=4 following OWASP recommendation for interactive logins.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-001 (LLM embedding in feed request path) and ISS-002 (CDN purge absent from takedown workflow). For ISS-001: pre-compute embeddings at upload-complete via Pub/Sub-triggered job, store in PostgreSQL pgvector, cache cosine similarity scores in Redis at 30s intervals, not per-request. For ISS-002: add synchronous Cloud CDN cache invalidation within AdminService.TakedownVideo gRPC handler, log purge_job_id to audit trail. For ISS-003: add gRPC interceptor to engagement-collector that reads coppa_minor claim from Firebase JWT and drops the engagement event (returns Ack without persisting).

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced Vertex AI embedding API call in feed-ranking GetFeed handler with Pub/Sub-triggered job that calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 on upload-complete event, stores 768-dim vector in PostgreSQL video_posts.content_embedding (pgvector), caches cosine similarity scores in Redis feed_cache:{viewer_id} every 30 seconds, returns ranked feed from Redis without re-querying Vertex AI
fix_report.expected_outcome: feed-ranking service completes in under 10ms; p99 <100ms for feed delivery; ISS-001 resolved

fix_report.action_taken: Added synchronous Cloud CDN cache invalidation within AdminService.TakedownVideo gRPC handler: calls Cloud CDN API PUT /v1/cache/{cache_key} with cache-control: no-cache, max-age=0 within the same gRPC transaction that updates video_post.status=taken_down; logs cdn_purge_job_id to moderation_audit_events table for DSA takedown propagation evidence
fix_report.expected_outcome: CDN purge completes within 55s of moderator action; ISS-002 resolved

parsed.title: Short-Form Video Microservices - Round 2
parsed.executive_summary: A Flutter 3.22 mobile app and Go microservices architecture on GKE Autopilot for a Gen Z short-form video platform. Each Go service is a Go 1.22 microservice with gRPC: feed-ranking pre-computes and caches 768-dim content embeddings from Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 at upload-complete, serving ranked feed within 10ms p99; upload-processor streams Cloud Run transcode jobs; social-graph stores creator and viewer relationships; engagement-collector records Cassandra-backed signals; content-moderation runs LLM moderation scoring; admin-api handles moderator actions and takedown. COPPA minor engagement signals blocked server-side; CDN purge within 60s of moderator action; DSA takedown propagation evidence via Cloud CDN cache invalidation.
parsed.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: Flutter 3.22 app (iOS and Android) communicates over HTTPS to a GKE Autopilot cluster running Go 1.22 microservices. Feed-ranking service reads Redis feed_cache:{viewer_id} for cached ranked feed (pre-computed at upload-complete via Pub/Sub-triggered embedding job) or queries Cassandra feed_rankings for live ranking. Upload-processor streams Cloud Run transcode jobs to Cloud Storage and publishes to Pub/Sub. Social-graph service reads PostgreSQL social_edges for follower/following relationships. Engagement-collector records Cassandra engagement_signals with coppa_minor JWT claim check. Content-moderation service runs LLM moderation scoring on Pub/Sub events. Admin-api handles moderator actions and takedown via gRPC. CDN serves HLS segments from Cloud Storage with Cloud CDN cache invalidation via synchronous Cloud CDN API calls within the same gRPC transaction.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Flutter 3.22, Dart, Riverpod, Impeller, Firebase Auth, Firebase App Distribution. Backend: Go 1.22, gRPC with protobuf, Pub/Sub, Cloud Run, Cloud CDN, ClickHouse (analytics). Database: PostgreSQL 16 (pgvector extension), Cassandra 4.1, ClickHouse. Cache: Redis 7 (feed_cache:{viewer_id}, trending_topics ZSET). Hosting: GKE Autopilot (us-central1, europe-west1), Cloud Build, Cloud CDN. Analytics: ClickHouse, Datadog APM, Datadog RUM, Firebase Crashlytics.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Vertical Video Feed: Flutter feed screen pre-loaded with 2 items from Redis feed_cache; on-demand GetFeed returns ranked feed from Redis or Cassandra; swipe to watch, swipe to skip; watch-time signals recorded to engagement_signals. Creator Content Management: UploadScreen uploads 30-second clips to Cloud Storage, trims to 60s max, adds caption, uploads to feed-ranking via Pub/Sub embedding job. Social Graph: Following/Followers screen, post-upload social graph update. Engagement Signals: Like, Share, Comment, Watch-Complete, Skip events recorded to engagement_signals; COPPA minor signals dropped server-side. Content Moderation: Pub/Sub event consumer runs LLM moderation scoring; flagged videos queued to moderation_queues. Admin Panel: Moderator-only bottom tab; TakedownVideo triggers CDN cache invalidation and Cloud CDN API purge; DSA takedown propagation evidence logged.
parsed.system_components: Flutter 3.22 App (iOS and Android), GKE Autopilot cluster (us-central1 primary, europe-west1 EU data residency), feed-ranking service (Go, gRPC, Redis feed_cache, Cassandra feed_rankings), upload-processor (Go, Cloud Run transcode job, Pub/Sub), social-graph service (Go, PostgreSQL social_edges), engagement-collector (Go, Cassandra engagement_signals), content-moderation service (Go, Pub/Sub, LLM moderation API), admin-api (Go, moderator-only bottom tab), Cloud CDN (Cloud Storage origin, Cloud CDN cache with synchronous invalidation), Cloud Build (Go binary container image), Cloud Run (upload-processor, content-moderation), ClickHouse (analytics cluster), Datadog APM (distributed tracing), Cloud Pub/Sub (event bus), Cloud CDN API (synchronous cache invalidation), Cloud Storage (raw video and transcoded segments), Cloud Build (container image from Go binary), Firebase App Distribution (internal Flutter test distribution).
parsed.workflows: Feed Delivery: App launches, Flutter fetches GET feed-ranking/v1/feed (gRPC) with custom claim coppa_minor=true; feed-ranking service checks coppa_minor claim in Firebase JWT, drops engagement event (returns Ack without persisting), reads Redis feed_cache:{viewer_id} for cached ranked feed (pre-computed at upload-complete via Pub/Sub-triggered embedding job), returns ranked feed within 10ms p99; if cache miss, queries Cassandra feed_rankings for live ranking, returns ranked feed within 300ms p99. CDN Purge: AdminService.TakedownVideo gRPC handler receives TakedownRequest; handler calls Cloud CDN API PUT /v1/cache/{cache_key} with cache-control: no-cache, max-age=0 within the same gRPC transaction; logs cdn_purge_job_id to moderation_audit_events table; returns TakedownAck with purge_job_id; CDN cache invalidated within 55s of purge_job_id; DSA takedown propagation evidence logged.
parsed.data_model: creator_profiles (id UUID PK, firebase_uid VARCHAR UNIQUE, username VARCHAR, display_name VARCHAR, region VARCHAR, age_verified BOOL, coppa_minor BOOL, follower_count INT, following_count INT, created_at TIMESTAMP). video_posts (id UUID PK, creator_id UUID FK, title VARCHAR, caption TEXT, hls_manifest_url VARCHAR, thumbnail_url VARCHAR, duration_seconds INT, status ENUM uploading/transcoding/active/taken_down, content_embedding vector(768) - pgvector, moderation_score FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP). feed_rankings (viewer_id UUID, segment_key TEXT, ranked_video_ids LIST<UUID>, scored_at TIMESTAMP) - Cassandra. engagement_signals (viewer_id UUID, video_id UUID, signal_type ENUM like/share/comment/watch_complete/skip, watch_time_ms INT, occurred_at TIMESTAMP) - Cassandra. social_edges (follower_id UUID, followee_id UUID, created_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL. moderation_queues (id UUID PK, video_id UUID, queue_type ENUM hash_match/llm_flagged/user_report, assigned_moderator_id UUID, status ENUM pending/reviewed/actioned, created_at TIMESTAMP). trending_topics (topic STRING, score FLOAT, updated_at TIMESTAMP) - Redis sorted set. moderation_audit_events (id UUID PK, video_id UUID, moderator_id UUID, action_type ENUM takedown/apply_label, cdn_purge_job_id UUID, occurred_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL.
parsed.api_design: gRPC proto definitions: FeedService.GetFeed(ViewerFeedRequest) -> FeedResponse (feed_cache:{viewer_id} cached ranked feed, ranked_video_ids list, feed_cache_ttl_seconds). UploadService.StartUpload(UploadRequest) -> UploadSession. EngagementService.RecordSignals(stream EngagementEvent) -> Ack. SocialService.Follow(FollowRequest) -> FollowResponse. AdminService.TakedownVideo(TakedownRequest) -> TakedownAck with cdn_purge_job_id. AdminService.ApplyLabel(ApplyLabelRequest) -> ApplyLabelResponse. NotificationService.Push(PushRequest) -> Ack. All gRPC interceptors validate JWT custom claims (age_verified, coppa_minor) before writing to Cassandra or PostgreSQL.
parsed.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth with custom claims: age_verified BOOL, coppa_minor BOOL, region STRING, moderator BOOL. Coppa minor: engagement-collector gRPC interceptor reads coppa_minor claim from Firebase JWT; if COPPA minor, drops engagement event (returns Ack without persisting); if COPPA minor and parent present, records engagement_signals with COPPA minor flag set. COPPA minor engagement signals not stored beyond session. GDPR: deletion-coordinator service receives DELETE /users/:id; fans out deletion events to all services via Pub/Sub; each service purges user data within 72 hours. DSA: transparency report endpoint GET /admin/dsa-report returns takedown counts by category and average propagation time; Cloud CDN cache invalidation logs purge_job_id to moderation_audit_events for DSA evidence. CDN purge: synchronous Cloud CDN API call within gRPC transaction; log cdn_purge_job_id to moderation_audit_events; DSA takedown propagation evidence logged.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot clusters: us-central1 (primary), europe-west1 (EU GDPR data residency). Cloud Build pipeline: go test ./..., golangci-lint, ko build (container image from Go binary), kubectl apply via Workload Identity. Flutter CI: flutter test, flutter build appbundle, Firebase App Distribution for internal testing. canary deploy: 5% traffic via Istio VirtualService weight; full rollout after 24-hour canary window. Cloud Run: upload-processor and content-moderation services; Cloud CDN origin pointing to Cloud Storage bucket. ClickHouse: separate analytics cluster. Cloud Build triggers on push to main; GitHub Actions on push to main.
parsed.observability: Datadog APM: distributed trace IDs propagated via gRPC metadata; custom metrics: feed_delivery_latency_p99 (target <100ms), upload_processing_duration_p50 (target <30s), engagement_signal_throughput (target 1000 signals/min), cdn_purge_latency (target <55s), moderation_queue_depth (alert >500). Datadog RUM: crash reporting and ANR. PagerDuty alerts: feed_delivery_p99 >100ms, cdn_purge_latency >55s, upload_failure_rate >5%.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot: ~$1200/month for 2 nodes. Cloud Run: ~$200/month for upload-processor and content-moderation. Cloud CDN: ~$50/month for HLS streaming. ClickHouse: ~$300/month for analytics. Total ~$2200/month at 500K daily active users. Scaling: GKE Autopilot auto-scales services on CPU 60%; Cloud Run scales on request count; ClickHouse scales horizontally via sharding.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Flutter 3.22 app with feed ranking, upload processor, social graph, engagement collector, basic moderation queue, admin panel with TakedownVideo, Cloud CDN with synchronous cache invalidation, COPPA minor engagement blocking, GDPR deletion coordinator, DSA takedown propagation evidence via Cloud CDN cache invalidation. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Watch-Share-Comment-Skip signals with watch-time recording, watch-complete events, trending topic ZSET, moderation queue review UI, DSA transparency report endpoint. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Live streaming via WebRTC, AR face filter integration, monetization for creators (digital gift subscriptions), creator analytics dashboard.
parsed.development_guidelines: Go services: all gRPC services must validate JWT custom claims (age_verified, coppa_minor) before writing to Cassandra or PostgreSQL. Coppa minor engagement signals must be dropped server-side; never persisted. gRPC interceptors must be idempotent. Pub/Sub events must be idempotent. PostgreSQL pgvector used for content_embedding; no external vector DB. Cloud CDN cache invalidation must be synchronous within gRPC transaction. Flutter: Firebase Auth custom claims must be validated in the Flutter app before any database write.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vertex AI embedding API latency adds 200-400ms to feed-ranking p99. Mitigation: pre-compute embeddings at upload-complete via Pub/Sub-triggered job; cache in Redis at 30s intervals; cache miss returns ranked feed within 300ms p99. Risk: Cloud CDN cache invalidation latency of 55s means DSA takedown propagation may not complete within 60s. Mitigation: log purge_job_id to moderation_audit_events for DSA evidence; monitor CDN purge latency in Datadog. Tradeoff: pgvector over external vector DB for content embeddings - pgvector avoids vendor lock-in and avoids adding a separate vector DB to the stack.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Selected pgvector over external vector DB for content embeddings to avoid vendor lock-in. Confirmed synchronous Cloud CDN cache invalidation within gRPC transaction for DSA takedown evidence. COPPA minor engagement signals dropped server-side confirmed by legal review. Confirmed Cloud CDN over Akamai for lower cost at current traffic levels.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-001 (LLM in request path) and ISS-002 (CDN purge absent). For ISS-001: embeddings are generated asynchronously by a Pub/Sub-triggered job at upload-complete time and stored in PostgreSQL pgvector; feed-ranking re-scores candidates every 30 seconds using pre-computed embeddings cached in Redis, never during a live GetFeed request. For ISS-002: AdminService.TakedownVideo handler calls Cloud CDN API synchronously within the gRPC handler before returning, logs cdn_purge_job_id to the moderation audit trail for DSA evidence. ISS-003 COPPA: engagement-collector gRPC interceptor reads coppa_minor claim and drops without persisting.

fix_report.action_taken: Moved LLM embedding computation from GetFeed request path to a Pub/Sub-triggered background job: on video_post.status=transcoded event, embedding-generator job calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5, stores 768-dim vector in video_posts.content_embedding (pgvector). Feed-ranking service re-scores top-K candidates every 30 seconds via a scheduled worker reading pgvector cosine similarity from PostgreSQL and writing ranked feed to Redis feed_cache:{viewer_id}. GetFeed handler reads only from Redis cache (<5ms), never from Vertex AI
fix_report.expected_outcome: GetFeed p99 latency drops from 200-400ms to <10ms for cached feed reads; LLM embedding freshness lag of up to 30 seconds is acceptable for feed ranking quality

fix_report.action_taken: Added synchronous Cloud CDN cache invalidation to AdminService.TakedownVideo gRPC handler: after updating video_post.status=taken_down in PostgreSQL, handler calls GCP Cloud CDN API cdn.CacheInvalidation.Create with pattern /videos/{video_id}/* before returning gRPC response; cdn_purge_job_id logged to moderation_audit_events table with timestamp; Pub/Sub event emitted post-purge for notification service to alert affected creator
fix_report.expected_outcome: CDN cache invalidation initiated within 1-2 seconds of moderator action; propagation completes in 10-30 seconds on Cloud CDN; DSA 60-second SLA met with buffer; cdn_purge_job_id provides audit evidence for DSA transparency reports

reference.title: Gen Z Short-Form Video Platform - Microservices Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A COPPA/GDPR/DSA-compliant Flutter 3.22 mobile app backed by Go 1.22 gRPC microservices on GKE Autopilot, decomposed from a scaling-bottlenecked monolith. The feed-ranking service delivers personalized vertical video feeds at p99 <100ms by reading pre-scored Redis caches refreshed every 30 seconds using Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 content embeddings computed asynchronously at upload time. Content moderation combines hash fingerprinting and LLM scoring; moderator takedowns synchronously invoke Cloud CDN cache invalidation within the gRPC handler for DSA 60-second propagation compliance. COPPA minor users have engagement signal collection blocked at the gRPC interceptor layer before any Cassandra write.
reference.architecture_overview: Flutter client connects via gRPC-Dart to a GKE Ingress routing to seven Go microservices: feed-ranking, upload-processor, social-graph, engagement-collector, content-moderation, admin-api, and notification. Feed delivery critical path: GetFeed reads Redis feed_cache:{viewer_id} (pre-scored every 30 seconds by a background feed-scorer worker) for sub-10ms response, bypassing Vertex AI entirely at request time. LLM embedding pipeline: upload-processor publishes upload_complete to Pub/Sub; embedding-generator service calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 embedding API, stores 768-dim vector in video_posts.content_embedding (PostgreSQL pgvector); feed-scorer worker runs cosine similarity queries against cached viewer interest embeddings every 30 seconds and writes results to Redis. CDN takedown path: TakedownVideo gRPC handler updates PostgreSQL video_post status, calls Cloud CDN CacheInvalidation API synchronously, logs cdn_purge_job_id to audit trail. Dual-region GKE (us-central1, europe-west1) with Cloud CDN fronting Cloud Storage video segments globally.
reference.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.22, Dart, Riverpod, gRPC-Dart, video_player package. Backend: Go 1.22, gRPC with protobuf, GCP Pub/Sub for async events, Firebase Admin SDK for JWT validation. Data: PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension (creator_profiles, video_posts, social_edges), Cassandra 4.1 (engagement_signals, feed_rankings), Redis 7 (feed_cache, trending_topics). LLM: Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 embedding API (768-dim). Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot (us-central1, europe-west1), Cloud Build, Cloud CDN, Cloud Storage, Cloud Run for transcoding jobs. Observability: Datadog APM + RUM.
reference.functional_feature_map: Vertical Feed: Flutter PageView rendering HLS video posts pre-loaded from Redis feed cache, refreshed every 30s by LLM-assisted relevance scoring. Upload Pipeline: creator submits clip via gRPC UploadService, upload-processor stores raw video in Cloud Storage, triggers Cloud Run FFmpeg transcode job, publishes upload_complete event to Pub/Sub which triggers embedding generation. Social Graph: SocialService.Follow/Unfollow writes PostgreSQL social_edges; follower counts maintained with PostgreSQL counter tables. Engagement Collection: streaming gRPC EngagementService.RecordSignals batches like/share/watch_time events to Cassandra; COPPA interceptor drops events for coppa_minor users. Content Moderation: content-moderation service checks PhotoDNA hash + LLM moderation score at upload_complete, routes flagged content to moderation_queues. Moderator Admin Panel: Flutter admin tab (moderator role only) calls AdminService.TakedownVideo which purges CDN within 60 seconds. Trending Topics: Redis sorted set updated hourly by trending-scorer worker based on engagement velocity.
reference.system_components: Flutter app (iOS/Android): Riverpod providers, gRPC-Dart stubs, video_player with HLS. GKE Ingress (gRPC HTTP/2 passthrough). Go microservices: feed-ranking (Redis read + 30s scorer worker), upload-processor (gRPC + Pub/Sub publisher + Cloud Run job launcher), social-graph (PostgreSQL), engagement-collector (Cassandra + coppa_minor interceptor), content-moderation (Pub/Sub consumer, hash + LLM scoring), admin-api (PostgreSQL + Cloud CDN API caller), notification (Pub/Sub consumer + FCM). embedding-generator service (Pub/Sub consumer, Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 API, pgvector write). feed-scorer worker (30s cron, PostgreSQL pgvector cosine similarity, Redis write). PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector. Cassandra 4.1. Redis 7. Cloud Storage (raw video + HLS segments). Cloud CDN. Datadog APM agents on all services.
reference.workflows: Feed Delivery (Critical Path): Flutter calls FeedService.GetFeed via gRPC. feed-ranking handler reads Redis LIST feed_cache:{viewer_id} (pre-populated by feed-scorer worker). Returns top 20 video_post_ids with HLS manifest URLs signed for Cloud CDN. p99 < 10ms from Redis. Background Feed Scoring (every 30s): feed-scorer worker queries Cassandra engagement_signals for each active viewer (last 7 days), fetches top-500 candidate videos, runs pgvector cosine similarity query: SELECT video_id FROM video_posts ORDER BY content_embedding <=> $viewer_interest_embedding LIMIT 500, applies collaborative filter boost from Cassandra follow-graph signal, writes re-ranked list to Redis feed_cache:{viewer_id} with 60s TTL. Upload and Embedding Pipeline: Creator uploads clip via UploadService.StartUpload. upload-processor stores raw file in Cloud Storage, launches Cloud Run FFmpeg transcode job (HLS at 720p/1080p). On transcode complete, publishes {video_id, hls_manifest_url} to upload_complete Pub/Sub topic. embedding-generator consumes event, calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 embedding API with video thumbnail + caption text, stores 768-dim vector in video_posts.content_embedding via pgvector. content-moderation consumes same event, checks hash, runs LLM moderation score, routes to moderation_queues if score > 0.7. Moderator Takedown (DSA SLA): Moderator clicks takedown in Flutter admin panel. Flutter calls AdminService.TakedownVideo(video_id, reason). admin-api gRPC handler: UPDATE video_posts SET status='taken_down'; calls GCP Cloud CDN CacheInvalidation.Create API with url_map pattern /videos/{video_id}/*; receives cdn_purge_job_id; INSERTs moderation_audit_event row with cdn_purge_job_id and initiated_at timestamp; returns TakedownAck with cdn_purge_job_id to Flutter. Flutter shows confirmation with purge job ID. CDN propagation completes in 10-30s; total takedown propagation under 60s. Pub/Sub notification event emitted to notify creator.
reference.data_model: creator_profiles (id UUID PK, firebase_uid VARCHAR, username VARCHAR UNIQUE, region VARCHAR, age_verified BOOL DEFAULT false, coppa_minor BOOL DEFAULT false, follower_count INT DEFAULT 0, following_count INT DEFAULT 0, created_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL. video_posts (id UUID PK, creator_id UUID FK, caption TEXT, hls_manifest_url TEXT, thumbnail_url TEXT, duration_seconds FLOAT, status TEXT CHECK IN ('uploading','transcoding','active','taken_down'), content_embedding vector(768), moderation_score FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL with pgvector. social_edges (follower_id UUID, followee_id UUID, created_at TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY (follower_id, followee_id)) - PostgreSQL. engagement_signals (viewer_id UUID, video_id UUID, signal_type TEXT, watch_time_ms INT, occurred_at TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY (viewer_id, occurred_at)) - Cassandra MV partition. feed_rankings (viewer_id UUID, bucket_key TEXT, ranked_video_ids LIST<UUID>, scored_at TIMESTAMP) - Cassandra. moderation_queues (id UUID PK, video_id UUID FK, queue_type TEXT, assigned_moderator_id UUID, status TEXT, cdn_purge_job_id TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL. trending_topics stored in Redis ZSET trending:topics with score = engagement_velocity_score.
reference.api_design: gRPC services (proto): FeedService.GetFeed(ViewerFeedRequest{viewer_id, page_token}) returns FeedResponse{video_posts[], next_page_token}. UploadService.StartUpload(UploadRequest{creator_id, duration_seconds, caption}) returns UploadSession{upload_url, session_id}. EngagementService.RecordSignals(stream EngagementEvent{viewer_id, video_id, signal_type, watch_time_ms}) returns Ack. SocialService.Follow(FollowRequest{follower_id, followee_id}) returns FollowAck. AdminService.TakedownVideo(TakedownRequest{video_id, moderator_id, reason}) returns TakedownAck{cdn_purge_job_id, initiated_at}. AdminService.GetModerationQueue(QueueRequest) returns ModerationQueueResponse. REST (admin panel DSA reporting): GET /admin/dsa-report?period=monthly (returns takedown counts and avg propagation times for DSA transparency report).
reference.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth custom claims: coppa_minor (set on DOB < 13 at registration), age_verified (parental consent received), moderator (granted by admin). gRPC interceptors on all services: JWT validation, and on engagement-collector: coppa_minor=true drops event without Cassandra write. COPPA: no behavioral signal collection for minors; watch-time not persisted; no ad targeting. GDPR: deletion-coordinator receives DELETE /users/:id, publishes user_delete_request to Pub/Sub; each service purges user rows within 72 hours; PostgreSQL social_edges and video_posts cascade on creator_profiles delete. DSA: TakedownVideo handler calls Cloud CDN CacheInvalidation synchronously; cdn_purge_job_id and timing logged to moderation_audit_events for quarterly DSA transparency report; GET /admin/dsa-report endpoint for regulatory submissions. Content hash fingerprinting: PhotoDNA hash checked at upload_complete before video becomes active.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Dual-region GKE Autopilot: us-central1 (global traffic default), europe-west1 (EU user affinity via Cloud Load Balancing geo-routing for GDPR residency). Each region deploys all Go microservices via Cloud Build. HPA on CPU + custom Datadog metrics: feed-ranking scales on feed_request_queue_depth, upload-processor scales on upload_queue_depth. Cloud Build pipeline per service: go test ./..., golangci-lint, ko build, kubectl apply with Workload Identity. Flutter CI: flutter test, flutter build appbundle --release, Firebase App Distribution for beta. Canary: Istio VirtualService 5% traffic split before full rollout. Rollback: kubectl rollout undo per service.
reference.observability: Datadog APM gRPC interceptor on all Go services with distributed trace propagation via gRPC metadata. Key traces: GetFeed (Redis read latency), TakedownVideo (CDN purge latency), embedding-generator (Vertex AI call duration). Custom Datadog metrics: feed_cache_hit_rate (target >99%), embedding_generation_lag_seconds (p95 < 120s post-upload), cdn_purge_latency_seconds (p99 < 55s for DSA SLA), engagement_signal_drop_rate_coppa (count of COPPA-dropped signals for audit). PagerDuty alerts: feed_cache_hit_rate <95%, cdn_purge_p99 >55s, upload_failure_rate >3%. Datadog RUM in Flutter for crash rate and video player error rate.
reference.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot scales to zero for low-traffic services; estimated $2,400/month at 500K daily active users: GKE compute ($900), Cloud CDN egress for 5PB/month video ($1,100), PostgreSQL CloudSQL ($250), Cassandra (self-managed GKE StatefulSet, $150). Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 embedding API: ~$0.0001 per 1K tokens, negligible for upload volume. Redis cluster ($120/month). Datadog ($200/month at this scale). Feed-scorer worker cost negligible (Go goroutine pool, <1 CPU continuous).
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (12 weeks): Decompose monolith feed-ranking and upload-processor services; Flutter gRPC client with Riverpod; Cassandra engagement_signals; Redis feed cache with 30s scorer; Vertex AI embedding pipeline (async); Cloud CDN with synchronous TakedownVideo purge; COPPA engagement interceptor; GDPR deletion coordinator; Datadog APM. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Social graph service with follower/following feed boost; trending topics Redis ZSET scorer; push notifications via FCM; content-moderation LLM scoring with PhotoDNA; DSA transparency report endpoint. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Ad placement service (excluding COPPA minors); creator analytics dashboard; live streaming architecture; AR face filters via TensorFlow Lite on-device.
reference.development_guidelines: Proto-first API design: all service interfaces defined in .proto files in the shared proto repository before implementation. Go: golangci-lint, unit tests for all business logic (80% coverage), gRPC interceptor tests with mock JWT tokens including coppa_minor=true test cases. Flutter: flutter_test for widget tests, integration tests for feed load and upload flow. DSA audit trail: every TakedownVideo must include cdn_purge_job_id in response - tested in admin-api integration tests. Each service deployed independently; no cross-service database writes (services own their data stores). Datadog trace annotations on all gRPC handlers with feed_cache_hit span tag.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: 30-second feed re-scoring lag means newly uploaded viral content appears in feeds up to 30 seconds after embedding generation. Mitigation: hot-content detector publishes forced re-score event when video engagement velocity exceeds threshold. Risk: PostgreSQL pgvector cosine similarity query over 10M video_posts at 30s interval becoming a bottleneck. Mitigation: partition pgvector queries by content category tag; pre-filter candidates by creator follow graph before similarity scan. Tradeoff: synchronous CDN purge in TakedownVideo vs. async job - chosen synchronous to meet DSA 60s SLA guarantee; adds ~2s to moderator API response time which is acceptable. Risk: Cassandra fan-out read for feed-scorer on viral videos followed by millions: Mitigation: cap fan-out at top-500 followed creators per viewer; use Cassandra materialized view for pre-aggregated engagement counts.
reference.open_questions_resolved: LLM embedding computed asynchronously at upload time, not at request time; GetFeed reads only Redis cache. CDN purge called synchronously in TakedownVideo gRPC handler with cdn_purge_job_id logged for DSA evidence. COPPA engagement blocking enforced via gRPC interceptor in engagement-collector service. pgvector in PostgreSQL chosen over Pinecone/Weaviate to reduce external service dependencies at current scale.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 fixes the shared Ticker rate limiter (ISS-001) with per-goroutine golang.org/x/time/rate.Limiter and adds YAML startup credential scan (ISS-002). The flawed plan_quality introduces an intentional inconsistency: the workflows section describes a web dashboard contradicting the cli_tool project class.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced shared time.Ticker with per-goroutine rate.NewLimiter(rate.Every(time.Second/workerEPS), 1) where workerEPS=totalEPS/numWorkers. Each goroutine calls limiter.Wait(ctx) before producing, yielding accurate per-goroutine rate control. Dropped-message counter now only increments on Kafka produce error or context deadline.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Accurate EPS delivery at target rates up to 50000 events/second; dropped-message count reflects actual Kafka failures not rate limiter artifacts

fix_report.action_taken: Added validateConfigCredentials(rawYAML []byte) function called at startup before viper parsing. Scans raw YAML bytes for '-----BEGIN', 'password:', 'sasl_password:', 'auth_token:'. Any match triggers log.Fatal with matched line number.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Startup scan prevents engineers running benchmarks with credential-containing YAML configs

reference.title: Sports Feed Pipeline Benchmark CLI v2
reference.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 CLI tool enabling sports data platform engineers to benchmark Kafka ingestion pipelines by simulating burst traffic at configurable events-per-second rates. Per-goroutine rate limiters using golang.org/x/time/rate deliver accurate EPS targeting, while an HDRHistogram records p50/p95/p99 latency. Results persist to a local DuckDB database for historical comparison and optionally push to Prometheus pushgateway. Startup YAML validation scans for credential patterns and aborts with a fatal error on detection.
reference.architecture_overview: cobra CLI with run, compare, and config validate subcommands. BenchmarkRunner spawns N producer goroutines using a semaphore-bounded pool; each goroutine holds an independent golang.org/x/time/rate.Limiter initialized at totalEPS/numWorkers, calling limiter.Wait(ctx) before each produce call. An HDRHistogram recorder goroutine receives latency samples via unbuffered channel. DuckDB via CGo stores run results. No network server; pure CLI binary.
reference.technology_stack: Language: Go 1.22. CLI: cobra and viper. Rate limiting: golang.org/x/time/rate per-goroutine Limiter. Kafka: confluent-kafka-go with librdkafka. Histograms: hdrhistogram-go. Result storage: DuckDB via marcboeker/go-duckdb. Logging: zerolog JSON. CI: GitHub Actions with goreleaser for multi-platform releases. pprof profiling endpoint exposed via net/http on --pprof-port flag for heap and goroutine dump capture during high-throughput benchmark runs. go test -benchmem tracks allocations per message to detect GC pressure regressions in hot-path serialisation code.
reference.functional_feature_map: sports-bench run: load and validate YAML config, scan for credential patterns, spawn N rate-limited producer goroutines, collect HDR latency samples, print p50/p95/p99 report, write JSON result, append to DuckDB. sports-bench compare: SQL query DuckDB for two run IDs, render side-by-side diff table. sports-bench config validate: parse YAML, apply schema and credential scan, print resolved config or error. --dry-run: validate config without connecting to cluster.
reference.system_components: BenchmarkRunner (goroutine pool orchestrator), ProducerGoroutine (per-worker Limiter and confluent-kafka-go producer), LatencyRecorder (HDRHistogram via channel), DropCounter (atomic counter), ResultWriter (DuckDB append and JSON file), ConfigLoader (viper YAML and validateConfigCredentials scan), PrometheusExporter (push to pushgateway on completion).
reference.workflows: Benchmark Execution: Engineer runs sports-bench run --config match_day_feed.yaml --eps 5000 --duration 60 --workers 20; ConfigLoader reads YAML and scans for credential patterns (fatal on match); BenchmarkRunner initializes 20 ProducerGoroutines each with rate.NewLimiter(rate.Every(time.Second/250), 1); goroutines call limiter.Wait(ctx) then produce to Kafka; LatencyRecorder collects percentiles; after 60 seconds SIGALRM triggers graceful drain; HDRHistogram printed and JSON written. INTENTIONAL FLAW: A web dashboard is available at http://localhost:8080 after running sports-bench serve to view benchmark history — this contradicts the cli_tool project class and local_only exposure.
reference.data_model: benchmark_runs (run_id VARCHAR PK, config_file_hash, target_type, eps_target INT, worker_count INT, duration_seconds INT, started_at TIMESTAMP, total_messages_sent BIGINT, dropped_messages BIGINT, p50_latency_us DOUBLE, p95_latency_us DOUBLE, p99_latency_us DOUBLE, max_latency_us DOUBLE, throughput_msg_per_sec DOUBLE). feed_configurations (config_hash VARCHAR PK, config_yaml TEXT, parsed_at TIMESTAMP). throughput_samples (run_id VARCHAR, sample_second INT, messages_in_window BIGINT). latency_histograms (run_id VARCHAR, percentile_bucket DOUBLE, count BIGINT). All in ~/.sports-bench/results.duckdb.
reference.api_design: CLI interface only. sports-bench run [flags]: --config, --target kafka/redis-streams, --eps, --duration, --workers, --dry-run, --push-gateway, --verbose. sports-bench compare --run-id (repeatable). sports-bench config validate --config. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 config error, 2 cluster connection error, 3 credential detection error.
reference.security_and_compliance: Kafka credentials via KAFKA_BROKERS, KAFKA_TLS_CERT_PATH, KAFKA_SASL_USERNAME, KAFKA_SASL_PASSWORD env vars only. Redis AUTH via REDIS_AUTH. validateConfigCredentials scans for '-----BEGIN', 'password:', 'sasl_password:', 'auth_token:' — fatal on match with line number. Kafka TLS via security.protocol SASL_SSL. Prometheus pushgateway URL validated for localhost or RFC1918 range.
reference.deployment_and_operations: goreleaser: linux/amd64, darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64, windows/amd64. CGo enabled; librdkafka pre-installed in GitHub Actions matrix. PR CI: go vet, staticcheck, golangci-lint, go test -race. Release on git tag: goreleaser release --clean, GitHub Release with checksums. go install supported via module proxy. GitHub Actions matrix builds across linux/amd64, darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64, windows/amd64 with CGO_ENABLED=1 and pre-installed librdkafka via apt/brew/choco. Release checksums verified against SLSA provenance attestation generated by goreleaser's --provenance flag for supply-chain integrity.
reference.observability: zerolog JSON to stderr on --verbose with run_id, worker_id, eps_target, total_sent, dropped, p50_us, p95_us, p99_us. Prometheus metrics on --push-gateway completion: sports_bench_messages_total, sports_bench_dropped_total, sports_bench_p99_latency_us, sports_bench_throughput_mps labeled by run_id. DuckDB result file includes a run_metadata table (run_id, kafka_broker, topic, target_eps, duration_s, cli_version) for longitudinal regression analysis across benchmark versions using a simple SELECT with JOIN.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost — local binary. GitHub Actions free tier. goreleaser OSS tier. DuckDB file grows at ~1KB per benchmark run. DuckDB file stored on local NVMe for maximum write throughput during high-frequency benchmark runs; results exported to CSV for sharing. No cloud egress costs; self-hosted Kafka required — tool assumes engineer provides their own broker.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (v1, complete): Kafka target, shared Ticker (known-flawed), DuckDB, JSON output. Phase 2 (v2, current): Per-goroutine rate.Limiter, validateConfigCredentials, --dry-run, goreleaser multi-platform release. Phase 3: Redis Streams adapter, HTML report from DuckDB, GitHub Actions step summary mode.
reference.development_guidelines: Go modules, no global state. gosec in CI with zero high-severity findings. Rate limiter accuracy regression test: assert p99 EPS error <5% at 10000 EPS with 50 workers. Credential scan unit test: assert validateConfigCredentials returns error on YAML containing 'password: secret123'.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: confluent-kafka-go CGo complicates cross-compilation — mitigated by platform-native GitHub Actions runners. Risk: HDRHistogram memory grows with very long runs — max_duration_seconds: 3600 cap. Tradeoff: DuckDB CGo over pure-Go SQLite — DuckDB chosen for SQL aggregation power on histogram data.
reference.open_questions_resolved: golang.org/x/time/rate chosen over custom token bucket — standard library, well-tested. DuckDB confirmed over pure-Go SQLite for compare subcommand aggregation. HDRHistogram channel-based recorder avoids contention on hot produce path.

raw:
{
 "thinking_summary": "This is a static bakery site with no server-side runtime. The key architectural decisions are: Eleventy for zero-JavaScript rendering, YAML data files for daily availability updates by the baker, and Netlify Forms for wholesale inquiries. The image optimization constraint drives the eleventy-img plugin for WebP generation at build time. The baker-friendly edit workflow is the primary architectural concern — the YAML data file is the single source of truth for all product and availability data.",
 "fix_report": [],
 "title": "Artisan Bakery Online Presence — Architecture",
 "executive_summary": "A static Eleventy 3.x website deployed on Netlify for a small artisan bakery, built with YAML-driven daily product availability and a wholesale contact form. No server-side r

parsed.thinking_summary: This is a static bakery site with no server-side runtime. The key architectural decisions are: Eleventy for zero-JavaScript rendering, YAML data files for daily availability updates by the baker, and Netlify Forms for wholesale inquiries. The image optimization constraint drives the eleventy-img plugin for WebP generation at build time. The baker-friendly edit workflow is the primary architectural concern — the YAML data file is the single source of truth for all product and availability data.
parsed.title: Artisan Bakery Online Presence — Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: A static Eleventy 3.x website deployed on Netlify for a small artisan bakery, built with YAML-driven daily product availability and a wholesale contact form. No server-side runtime, no database, no authentication. Baker updates availability by editing a single YAML file in GitHub; images are optimized at build time. Netlify Forms handles wholesale inquiries without a backend. Plausible Analytics provides privacy-respecting page view tracking.
parsed.architecture_overview: Fully static architecture: Eleventy 3.x generates HTML pages from Nunjucks templates and CSS custom properties at build time. No runtime server processes. GitHub repository stores all content as YAML data files and image assets. Netlify CDN serves the static output. Netlify Forms handles wholesale inquiry submissions to the baker's email address. Plausible Analytics provides cookieless page view tracking. No cookies set by the site itself.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Eleventy 3.x, Nunjucks, eleventy-img for WebP image optimization, CSS custom properties for brand colors. Gallery: CSS grid with lazy-loaded images via loading=lazy. Analytics: Plausible Analytics (cookieless). Forms: Netlify Forms (spam-filtered). Hosting: Netlify (free tier). CI: GitHub Actions (YAML lint, image validation).
parsed.functional_feature_map: Home page: hero banner with bakery story and daily availability summary, bread category cards, gallery grid with lazy loading. Availability page: full daily product listing from YAML, sortable by category and price. Baker story page: biography, years of baking, favorite recipes. Gallery page: full baked goods photo grid with category tags. Wholesale page: inquiry form with name, business name, email, and inquiry text. Contact page: email address and phone number.
parsed.system_components: GitHub repository (YAML data files, image assets, Nunjucks templates). Netlify project (static output, custom domain, auto-provisioned TLS). Netlify Forms (wholesale inquiry submissions). Netlify deploy previews (PR branch review). GitHub Actions (CI pipeline). Plausible Analytics (cookieless page view tracking).
parsed.workflows: Daily Availability Update: Baker opens GitHub repository, edits availability.yaml adding or changing a product line, commits the file. GitHub Actions YAML lint step validates the YAML syntax. Netlify build runs, eleventy-img processes all gallery images generating WebP at 300x300, 800x800, 1200x1200 breakpoints with responsive srcset. Netlify deploys the static output to CDN. Baker visits artisanbakery.com and sees the updated availability reflected in the daily product listing. Wholesale Inquiry: Retail customer fills the wholesale inquiry form on the /wholesale page. Netlify Forms validates the form with a honeypot field. The form data is submitted to the baker's email address via Netlify Forms API. The baker receives an email notification and can respond from their email client.
parsed.data_model: product_listings (YAML: name, category ENUM bread/pastry/cake/seasonal, price_display, available_qty, notes). daily_availability (YAML: date, items[]). gallery_images (YAML: filename, alt_text, category). baker_profiles (YAML: name, bio, years_baking, photo_filename). wholesale_inquiry_forms (Netlify Forms submissions: contact_name, business_name, email, inquiry_text).
parsed.api_design: No server-side API. Netlify Forms API: POST /api/forms/ submissions/{id} to baker's email address. Netlify Forms honeypot field: hidden input with value=true. No cookies set by the site itself.
parsed.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Netlify for all requests. Basic Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://plausible.io https://netlify.com; img-src 'self' data: https://*.netlify.com. Netlify Forms honeypot field blocks bot submissions. Akismet spam filtering enabled on Netlify Forms. No cookies set by the site. Netlify enforces auto-provisioned TLS for custom domain. No personal data stored on self-managed infrastructure.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI pipeline: (1) YAML lint step validates availability.yaml and product data files against YAML schema. (2) Image validation step checks no source image exceeds 3MB. (3) Eleventy build runs npx @11ty/eleventy with --dry-run flag to confirm no template errors. (4) On main branch push, Netlify build runs and deploys to CDN. On PR branch push, Netlify deploy preview with unique URL is available for baker review. Netlify auto-provisions TLS certificate for custom domain.
parsed.observability: Plausible Analytics: cookieless page view tracking (page title, referrer, unique visitors), referral source, top pages. No error tracking or custom metrics needed for a static site. GitHub Actions build log shows build success or failure. Netlify deploy preview URL shared with baker for review.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Netlify free tier: $0/month for custom domain, CDN, and 300 build minutes/month. Netlify Forms: free for up to 100 submissions/month. Plausible Analytics: free for up to 1000 page views/month. Total cost: $0. No scaling concerns for a single-site static architecture.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (1 sprint): Home page with hero banner and daily availability summary, availability page with YAML data, baker story page, gallery page with lazy-loaded images, wholesale page with Netlify Forms, Netlify deploy preview, GitHub Actions CI pipeline. Phase 2 (post-launch): Seasonal menu archive, Instagram feed embed, pre-order form for weekly bread subscription.
parsed.development_guidelines: YAML data files must be committed to the repository and never edited via GitHub web interface. No inline JavaScript in Nunjucks templates — all interactivity via Netlify Forms or Plausible Analytics script tags. GitHub Actions YAML lint step must pass before any merge. Image assets must be named with descriptive filenames (e.g., sourdough-loaf-20260315.jpg) and stored in the _assets/images/ directory. Netlify Forms honeypot field must be present on every wholesale page.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: baker commits incorrect YAML syntax to availability.yaml, causing a build failure. Mitigation: GitHub Actions YAML lint step with error reporting. Risk: Netlify free tier build minute limit (300/month) is reached during a large image optimization run. Mitigation: eleventy-img generates WebP at 300x300 by default; baker can override to 800x800 for larger images. Tradeoff: Netlify Forms over a custom backend for simplicity at the cost of a third-party service dependency.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Selected Netlify over Vercel for free tier compatibility with the custom domain. Chose YAML data files over a CMS for the baker-friendly edit workflow. Selected Plausible over Google Analytics for GDPR compliance. Confirmed lazy loading via loading=lazy for mobile performance.

reference.thinking_summary: This is a first-pass plan for a minimalist artisan bakery static site. The critical design decisions are: Eleventy with YAML data files to meet the self-service baker update constraint, image optimization via eleventy-img to meet the 2-second mobile load constraint on a gallery-heavy site, and Netlify Forms to eliminate all server-side form infrastructure. The biggest technical risk is unoptimized imagery; the plan must address this explicitly.
reference.title: Artisan Bakery Static Website Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A zero-server static website for a boutique artisan bakery built with Eleventy 3.x and deployed to Netlify free tier. The baker updates daily product availability by editing a YAML file in GitHub, triggering an automated site rebuild. The photo gallery is optimized at build time for sub-2-second mobile loads. Wholesale inquiry submissions route via Netlify Forms directly to the baker's inbox with no backend infrastructure required.
reference.architecture_overview: Fully static Eleventy site: HTML generated at build time from Nunjucks templates and YAML data sources. No client-side JavaScript except a deferred Plausible Analytics snippet. Daily availability data lives in _data/availability.yaml, edited by the baker in GitHub and triggering a Netlify redeploy via webhook. The photo gallery is processed at build time via eleventy-img, generating WebP variants at three responsive breakpoints. Netlify serves the static output globally via its CDN with automatic HTTPS via Let's Encrypt.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Eleventy 3.x, Nunjucks templating, custom CSS with CSS custom properties. Image optimization: eleventy-img plugin (WebP output, responsive srcset). Forms: Netlify Forms with Akismet spam filtering. Analytics: Plausible Analytics (cookieless, GDPR-compliant). Hosting: Netlify free tier with custom domain. CI: GitHub Actions (YAML lint, image size validation, Eleventy build check). Version control: GitHub.
reference.functional_feature_map: Daily Availability Page: renders product_listings from _data/availability.yaml sorted by category (bread, pastry, cake, seasonal); each item shows name, quantity available, and price display. Baker Story: static HTML/CSS page with baker biography, years of craft, and portrait photo. Photo Gallery: responsive CSS grid of baked goods images processed via eleventy-img with lazy loading; filtered by category. Wholesale Inquiry: Netlify Forms-powered contact form capturing business name, contact name, email, and inquiry text, submitted to baker inbox with spam filtering. Home Page: hero with daily availability summary showing today's featured items plus gallery preview grid.
reference.system_components: Netlify CDN (global edge delivery of static HTML, CSS, and optimized images). GitHub repository (source of truth for all content, templates, and YAML data). eleventy-img (build-time image processing pipeline). Netlify Forms (form submission handler with Akismet spam filtering, email notification to baker). Plausible Analytics (cookieless page view tracking hosted on Plausible's CDN). GitHub Actions (pre-merge validation: YAML lint, image size check, Eleventy build smoke test).
reference.workflows: Daily Availability Update: Baker opens _data/availability.yaml in GitHub web editor, updates product quantities and availability notes, commits to main branch → Netlify detects push, triggers Eleventy build (approx 20-30 seconds) → updated availability page live globally via Netlify CDN. Wholesale Inquiry Flow: Prospective wholesale buyer fills /wholesale form → Netlify Forms validates honeypot field and Akismet check → submission forwarded to baker's email with full form contents → baker replies directly; no data persists beyond Netlify Forms dashboard (baker clears periodically). Photo Gallery Update: Baker (or developer) adds optimized source images to src/assets/gallery/, commits to main → eleventy-img processes new images at build time into WebP variants → gallery page updated on next deploy.
reference.data_model: product_listings (YAML: name STRING, category ENUM bread/pastry/cake/seasonal, available_qty INTEGER, price_display STRING, notes STRING optional). daily_availability (YAML: published_date DATE, items ARRAY of product_listing references). gallery_images (filesystem metadata: filename, alt_text STRING, category ENUM bread/pastry/cake/seasonal, display_order INTEGER). baker_profiles (YAML: baker_name, bio_text, years_baking INTEGER, portrait_filename). wholesale_inquiry_submissions (Netlify Forms: contact_name, business_name, email, product_interest, inquiry_text — stored in Netlify dashboard only, not in repository or self-managed storage).
reference.api_design: No self-hosted API. External integrations only: Netlify Forms POST https://www.netlify.com/form-handler (browser-native form action, no JavaScript required). Plausible Analytics script GET https://plausible.io/js/script.js (deferred). Netlify Build Webhook: Netlify listens on main branch push events from GitHub to trigger rebuilds. GitHub Actions invokes Eleventy CLI (npx @11ty/eleventy --dry-run) for build validation.
reference.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Netlify with auto-renewed Let's Encrypt certificate. CSP header via netlify.toml: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://plausible.io; img-src 'self' data:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; frame-ancestors 'none'. Netlify Forms spam filtering via Akismet integration plus HTML honeypot field. Plausible is cookieless — no GDPR consent banner required. No cookies set by the site. YAML data files contain no personal information — only product names and availability counts.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Netlify build command: npx @11ty/eleventy. Publish directory: _site. Build triggered on push to main branch and on Netlify Forms integration events. Deploy previews automatically generated for all open pull requests with a unique URL shared in PR comments. Baker workflow: edit availability.yaml via GitHub web UI → commit to main → site rebuilds in ~25 seconds → live. Rollback: Netlify one-click rollback to any previous successful deploy from the Netlify deploy history dashboard. Custom domain: configured via Netlify DNS or external DNS CNAME to Netlify's load balancer.
reference.observability: Plausible Analytics dashboard showing daily unique visitors, top pages (availability, gallery, wholesale), and referral sources (Instagram, Google, direct). Netlify deploy notifications via email on build failure. GitHub Actions status checks visible in PR view. No APM or error tracking required for a zero-JavaScript static site. Netlify Forms dashboard shows submission count and allows baker to review and delete stored submissions.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Total monthly cost: $0. Netlify free tier includes 100GB bandwidth, 300 build minutes, 100 form submissions/month — all sufficient for a local artisan bakery's expected traffic. Plausible Analytics: $9/month for the paid plan if the baker wants more than 30-day data retention (optional upgrade). Custom domain registration ~$15/year via Namecheap or Google Domains. If traffic grows beyond Netlify free tier limits, Netlify Pro ($19/month) provides 400GB bandwidth.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Launch (1 sprint, 5 days): Eleventy project setup with Nunjucks layouts, YAML data files for availability and baker profile, gallery with eleventy-img optimization, wholesale inquiry form via Netlify Forms, Plausible Analytics integration, CSP headers via netlify.toml, GitHub Actions CI, deploy to Netlify with custom domain. No phase 2 required for MVP — the full feature set is the initial delivery. Post-launch enhancements (if requested): Instagram embed, pre-order YAML form, seasonal menu archive page — all additive static content changes.
reference.development_guidelines: All product and availability data in YAML files — no hardcoded content in Nunjucks templates. Image optimization required via eleventy-img; raw img tags forbidden. CSS custom properties for bakery brand colors defined in :root to enable easy rebranding. GitHub branch protection on main: require PR review from developer before baker-initiated commits trigger production deploy. YAML lint (yamllint) enforced in GitHub Actions. No npm packages with node_modules committed to repository.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Baker commits invalid YAML syntax causing a failed build. Mitigation: GitHub Actions YAML lint step catches errors before merge; Netlify atomic deploys mean the previous version stays live on build failure. Risk: Unoptimized source images committed to repository bloating clone size. Mitigation: GitHub Actions checks that no source image in src/assets/ exceeds 3MB; eleventy-img handles all output optimization. Tradeoff: YAML-in-GitHub update workflow requires baker to have basic GitHub familiarity. Accepted because alternative (CMS) adds cost and complexity that is not justified for this scope.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected Eleventy over Hugo for more familiar Nunjucks templating for the developer. Chose Plausible over GA4 to avoid GDPR cookie consent banner complexity on a simple bakery site. Confirmed Netlify Forms over Formspree for tighter Netlify hosting integration. No CMS needed — YAML data files are sufficient for this content volume.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for the ZTNA desktop agent. Two high-severity issues: EDR detection must be specified per platform (WMI Win32_Service on Windows, launchctl on macOS) and WireGuard integration must be decided (boringtun userspace vs kernel module). Both are implementation-critical and must be resolved in this revision. The agent architecture is sound — the plan quality is weak per manifest because the plan quality is intentionally weak per manifest.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified EDR detection per platform: Windows via WMI Win32_Service query filtering for known EDR service names (CsSensorService, CSFalconService, SentinelOne, CylanceSvc); macOS via launchctl list filtered against configurable EDR process name list. Added EDR detection check to posture_evaluator module in Rust agent daemon.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Agent can detect EDR on Windows and macOS; EDR detection is now a concrete implementation detail in the plan

fix_report.action_taken: Chose wireguard-rs (boringtun crate) userspace implementation for cross-platform MVP. Added wireguard-rs crate dependency to dependencies section and specified userspace approach in tunnel_manager module.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Agent supports both Windows and macOS MVP without kernel module dependency; acceptable <5% throughput overhead

parsed.title: ZTNA Desktop Agent — Round 2
parsed.executive_summary: A Rust 1.78 stable agent daemon for ZTNA that replaces aging VPN clients. Tauri 2.0 React 18 tray UI communicates via localhost Axum IPC to a Rust agent daemon that evaluates device posture, signs a posture report with the device private key, attests to the on-premise control plane via mTLS, and manages WireGuard userspace tunnels. SQLCipher AES-256 encrypts all local data. EDR detection is specified per platform (WMI Win32_Service on Windows, launchctl on macOS). WireGuard integration is userspace boringtun for cross-platform MVP.
parsed.architecture_overview: Tauri 2.0 React 18 WebView communicates with a Rust 1.78 stable agent daemon via localhost Axum HTTP server (127.0.0.1:7777). The agent daemon runs as a privileged process on the device, evaluating device posture via platform-specific modules: WMI Win32_Service query on Windows filtering for EDR service names (CsSensorService, CSFalconService, SentinelOne, CylanceSvc); launchctl list filtered against EDR process name list on macOS. The agent signs the posture report with the device private key using an OS-native CNG API on Windows and a Security framework API on macOS. The agent attests to the on-premise control plane via mTLS using the control plane ECDSA key. On policy token receipt, the agent establishes a WireGuard userspace tunnel via boringtun. On posture failure, the agent tears down the tunnel and writes an access_log entry with the failing check name and remediation step. All posture data and access logs are stored in a SQLCipher AES-256 encrypted SQLite file at %APPDATA%/ztna-agent/agent.db.
parsed.technology_stack: Runtime: Rust 1.78 stable. Web: Tauri 2.0, React 18, TypeScript. Tray: Tauri system-tray plugin. IPC: Axum 0.7 for local HTTP server. Posture: WMI on Windows via windows-certificate-store crate, SystemConfiguration on macOS via security-framework crate, boringtun userspace for WireGuard on both platforms. Database: rusqlite with SQLCipher feature for AES-256 encryption. Certificate: windows-certificate-store and security-framework crates for OS-native certificate access. Control plane: mTLS via OpenSSL crate, SPKI hash for pin storage. Packaging: Tauri bundler for macOS .dmg and Windows .msi. CI: GitHub Actions with cargo clippy, cargo test, cargo audit. Observability: Prometheus on-premise, Grafana on-premise.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Device Posture Evaluation: WMI Win32_Service query on Windows filtering for EDR service names (CsSensorService, CSFalconService, SentinelOne, CylanceSvc); launchctl list on macOS filtered against EDR process name list. EDR Detection: EDR service name detection on Windows; launchctl process name detection on macOS. Policy Token Issuance: control plane ECDSA signature on posture report; policy token lifetime 4 hours. Tunnel Management: WireGuard userspace tunnel lifecycle (connect, disconnect, re-attestation). Tray Icon State: connected (green), checking (amber), remediating (red-pulse), disconnected (grey). Remediation Notifications: Tauri system notification on posture failure mid-session. Offline Posture Mode: all posture checks disabled; agent enters offline-posture mode using last-known posture from SQLite.
parsed.system_components: Tauri 2.0 Application (React 18 WebView, system-tray plugin, notification API). localhost Axum HTTP Server (127.0.0.1:7777, loopback only, Tauri IPC). Rust Agent Daemon (privileged process, Rust 1.78 stable, WMI/SystemConfiguration/boringtun/SQLCipher/SPKI/Tauri IPC). WMI Posture Evaluator (Windows only, WMI Win32_Service query filtering EDR service names). macOS Posture Evaluator (macOS only, launchctl list EDR process name filtering). Policy Engine (evaluates posture_check_rules against posture report, issues policy token). Tunnel Manager (boringtun userspace, WireGuard peer config generation). Tray Icon State Manager (Tauri system-tray plugin, React state). Access Log Writer (rusqlite write, 90-day auto-purge). Certificate Identities Manager (SQLCipher key derivation from device certificate thumbprint).
parsed.workflows: Posture Evaluation and Attestation: IT administrator installs agent on managed device. Agent daemon starts. On first boot, agent queries WMI Win32_Service on Windows for EDR service names (CsSensorService, CSFalconService, SentinelOne, CylanceSvc); on macOS, agent queries launchctl list for EDR process names. EDR detection result stored in device_posture_reports. On next posture check, agent signs posture report with device private key using CNG API on Windows and Security framework API on macOS, stores signed report in SQLCipher SQLite. Agent sends POST /v1/attestation to control plane with signed report and policy token. Control plane verifies ECDSA signature, validates policy token, and returns policy token. Agent stores policy token in certificate_identities table. Tunnel Management: IT admin receives policy token via MDM or Intune. Agent establishes WireGuard userspace tunnel via boringtun: agent calls boringtun::Connection::connect(peer_config); tunnel established; agent writes tunnel_state record in SQLite. On posture failure mid-session, agent tears down tunnel, writes access_log entry with failing check name and remediation step, fires Tauri system notification. On policy expiry or posture change, agent re-attestation: agent calls POST /v1/attestation with new signed posture report; control plane re-attestation required; if re-attestation fails, agent enters offline-posture mode.
parsed.data_model: device_posture_reports (id INTEGER PK, check_type TEXT CHECK(IN('pass','fail','unknown')), detail TEXT, evaluated_at INTEGER, signed_report_b64 TEXT, EDR_detected BOOLEAN). posture_check_rules (id INTEGER PK, check_name TEXT UNIQUE, check_type TEXT CHECK(IN('windows','macos','all')), edr_service_names TEXT[], pass_condition TEXT, severity TEXT CHECK(IN('blocking','warning'))). access_logs (id INTEGER PK, event_type TEXT, network_segment TEXT, policy_token_id TEXT, event_ts INTEGER, detail TEXT). certificate_identities (id INTEGER PK, cert_thumbprint TEXT UNIQUE, subject_cn TEXT, not_before INTEGER, not_after INTEGER, is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1). All in SQLCipher AES-256 encrypted SQLite at %APPDATA%/ztna-agent/agent.db.
parsed.api_design: Local Axum HTTP server (127.0.0.1:7777, loopback only): GET /status (tray icon state table), GET /posture?check_type=windows (WMI EDR check result), GET /posture?check_type=macos (launchctl EDR check result), POST /connect (initiate WireGuard tunnel), POST /disconnect (tear down tunnel), GET /logs?limit=100 (recent access_logs), POST /logs?limit=100&after= (paginated access_logs), POST /attestation (submit signed posture report, receive policy token), POST /cert/renew (request new certificate). Control plane REST: POST /v1/attestation (submit signed posture report), POST /v1/tunnel/config (receive WireGuard peer config), POST /v1/cert/renew (issue new certificate). All endpoints require mTLS client certificate from device.
parsed.security_and_compliance: mTLS: control plane ECDSA key stored at %APPDATA%/ztna-agent/agent.key; agent pin stored at %APPDATA%/ztna-agent/agent.pin; agent verifies control plane certificate via SPKI hash before each attestation; certificate rotation requires MDM-distributed binary update. Posture report signed with device private key using OS-native CNG API on Windows and Security framework API on macOS; private key never exported from hardware-protected store. SQLCipher AES-256 key derived from device certificate thumbprint via HKDF; key never stored in plaintext. Certificate thumbprint stored in certificate_identities table; not_after field prevents certificate expiry before control plane is reachable. NIST SP 800-207 controls: device identity verification before access, continuous posture evaluation, EDR detection, policy token issuance, tunnel integrity, offline-posture mode. Cross-border data transfer: posture data and access logs transmitted only within corporate network; no cross-border transmission.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: on PR — cargo clippy (deny warnings), cargo test (posture module unit tests with mock WMI/SystemConfiguration/boringtun), cargo audit. macOS runner: macOS 14 test with macOS-specific WMI and SystemConfiguration tests. Windows runner: Windows 11 test with WMI and SystemConfiguration tests. Tauri build: macOS .dmg (Apple Developer ID required) and Windows .msi (EV code signing cert required) produced by Tauri bundler. MDM deployment: JAMF Pro deploys macOS .dmg; Intune deploys Windows .msi. IT controls: rollout via JAMF deployment policy; agent auto-update disabled by default; IT can enable via JAMF API. Control plane: on-premise Kubernetes (existing infrastructure); Prometheus scrapes /metrics; Grafana dashboards on-premise.
parsed.observability: Prometheus metrics from agent: agent_attestation_success_total (label: policy_token_issued), agent_attestation_failure_total (label: failure_reason), policy_token_issued_total, cert_renewal_failure_total, active_tunnel_count (label: tunnel_type), offline_posture_mode_duration_seconds. On-premise Grafana dashboards: agent status (connected/checking/remediating), tunnel health (active/disconnected), posture check results (pass/fail/unknown), EDR detection status (EDR_detected: true/false), policy token expiry countdown. Alert: Prometheus rule fires when min(not_after - now()) < 30 days.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost — agent runs on existing device hardware. Control plane: existing on-premise Kubernetes. Prometheus and Grafana: existing on-premise Grafana deployment. GitHub Actions: free tier. Total cost: zero. Scaling: single-process Rust daemon scales to any device without architecture changes; control plane scales horizontally behind on-premise load balancer.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Windows 11 and macOS 14 agent with WMI EDR detection, SQLCipher local database, mTLS attestation to control plane, WireGuard userspace tunnel via boringtun, Tauri React 18 tray UI, GitHub Actions CI, JAMF and Intune MDM deployment. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Linux (Ubuntu 22.04) agent support via Ubuntu 22.04 WMI and SystemConfiguration, additional EDR service name detection on Windows, launchctl EDR detection on macOS, browser extension for per-app network policy. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Per-app network policy via browser extension, offline posture mode, certificate rotation workflow, Grafana observability dashboards.
parsed.development_guidelines: Rust: clippy deny warnings enforced in CI; cargo audit in CI. Posture module unit tests use mock WMI/SystemConfiguration/boringtun/SQLCipher crates. All EDR detection logic in posture_evaluator module must be unit-testable with mock EDR service names. WireGuard integration must be unit-testable with mock boringtun and boringctl. No static hardcoded certificate thumbprints — all certificate thumbprints derived at runtime from device certificate via HKDF. Tauri: Tauri build for macOS .dmg and Windows .msi in CI; no native code in Tauri binary.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: EDR vendor service names on Windows vary between EDR vendors; agent must detect all EDR vendors. Mitigation: WMI Win32_Service query with configurable EDR service name list; EDR detection result stored in device_posture_reports. Risk: WireGuard kernel module dependency on Windows causes installer failure. Mitigation: userspace boringtun for MVP; kernel module added in Phase 2 if Windows EDR detection requires it. Tradeoff: userspace boringtun over kernel module for MVP — acceptable <5% throughput overhead; kernel module added in Phase 2 if throughput degrades. Risk: SQLCipher key compromise leaves all posture data and access logs unencrypted. Mitigation: SQLCipher AES-256 key derived from device certificate thumbprint via HKDF; key never stored in plaintext.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Chose wireguard-rs (boringtun) userspace implementation for cross-platform MVP. Chose SQLCipher AES-256 over AES-128 for posture report signing to prevent key compromise. Chose HKDF over static key derivation for certificate thumbprint derivation to prevent key exposure in keystore files. Chose WMI Win32_Service over Process Explorer for EDR detection on Windows to avoid requiring elevated privileges.

reference.thinking_summary: Resolving two blocking implementation gaps: EDR detection is now specified per platform using WMI Win32_Service query on Windows for known EDR service names (CsSensorService for CrowdStrike Falcon, CSFalconService, SentinelOne, CylanceSvc) and launchctl list filtered against an EDR process name list on macOS. WireGuard uses boringtun (wireguard-rs) userspace implementation, avoiding kernel module dependency on both Windows and macOS with <5% throughput overhead. Two-phase certificate rotation and SQLCipher key derivation carry forward unchanged.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified EDR posture check per platform: Windows posture_evaluator calls WMI Win32_Service query for known EDR service names stored in posture_check_rules.edr_service_names column; macOS calls launchctl list and filters against configured EDR process name list. Result is PASS if any known EDR service is in running state.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Rust posture_evaluator module has unambiguous implementation spec for both platforms; EDR check testable with mock WMI responses in unit tests

fix_report.action_taken: Chose wireguard-rs userspace (boringtun crate) over kernel WireGuard; documented rationale and integration approach in system_components, workflows, and technology_stack.
fix_report.expected_outcome: tunnel_manager module can be implemented without kernel extension requirements; macOS and Windows supported identically with userspace WireGuard

reference.title: ZTNA Desktop Agent — Zero Trust Network Access
reference.executive_summary: A cross-platform desktop application built with Tauri 2.0 and Rust replacing a legacy VPN client with a zero-trust network access agent. The Rust daemon evaluates device posture (OS patch level, disk encryption, EDR presence via platform-specific detection, firewall state), signs the posture report with the device private key from the OS certificate store, and attests to an on-premise control plane via mTLS. On successful attestation, the agent establishes a WireGuard userspace (boringtun) tunnel to permitted network segments. The Tauri React tray UI shows real-time connection state and fires remediation notifications on posture failures. Revision 2 resolves EDR detection platform specifics and confirms boringtun userspace WireGuard.
reference.architecture_overview: Tauri 2.0 application hosts a React 18 WebView rendering the tray status panel. All privileged operations (posture evaluation, certificate access, tunnel management) execute in the Rust daemon process. Tauri WebView communicates with the Rust daemon via a loopback-only Axum HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:7777 — WebView CSP restricts external network access entirely. The Rust posture_evaluator uses platform-specific APIs under #[cfg(target_os)] conditional compilation: WMI Win32_QuickFixEngineering for OS patch level and Win32_Service for EDR detection on Windows; CSPropertyList and launchctl for patch level and EDR on macOS. Posture results are assembled into a JSON report, SHA-256 hashed, and signed using the OS-native signing API (CNG on Windows, Security framework on macOS) — device private key never leaves hardware-protected storage. The signed report is transmitted to the on-premise control plane via mTLS POST /v1/attestation. The control plane validates the client certificate against the corporate PKI CA, verifies the posture report signature, evaluates posture against policy, and returns a signed ECDSA policy token. The Rust tunnel_manager establishes a WireGuard interface using boringtun (wireguard-rs) configured with the peer config from POST /v1/tunnel/config. Mid-session, posture_evaluator re-runs every 30 seconds; on any blocking check failing, the tunnel is torn down and a Tauri remediation notification fires.
reference.technology_stack: Desktop: Tauri 2.0 with React 18 TypeScript for tray UI. Rust 1.78 stable for agent daemon. Axum 0.7 for local IPC HTTP server. boringtun (wireguard-rs) for userspace WireGuard tunnel. windows-certificate-store crate for Windows CNG certificate access. security-framework crate for macOS Keychain access. wmi crate for Windows WMI queries. rusqlite with SQLCipher feature for encrypted local database. tokio for async runtime and scheduled tasks. Control plane: on-premise Kubernetes (existing). Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus on-premise. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with macOS and Windows runners, Tauri bundler, JAMF API and Intune Graph API for MDM distribution.
reference.functional_feature_map: Device Posture Evaluation: four checks run in parallel via tokio::join! — (1) OS patch level: Windows WMI Win32_QuickFixEngineering last installed date vs configurable max_age_days; macOS softwareupdate --list. (2) Disk encryption: Windows WMI Win32_EncryptableVolume ProtectionStatus for BitLocker; macOS fdesetup status. (3) EDR presence: Windows WMI Win32_Service query for service names in posture_check_rules.edr_service_names (CsSensorService, CSFalconService, SentinelOne, CylanceSvc, SEPMasterService); macOS launchctl list filtered against EDR process pattern list in posture_check_rules. (4) Firewall: Windows netsh advfirewall show allprofiles; macOS socketfilterfw --getglobalstate. mTLS Attestation: posture report signed and POSTed to control plane; policy token received. WireGuard Tunnel: boringtun interface created with peer config; traffic to permitted segments routed through tunnel. Tray: four states driven by Axum /status endpoint polled by Tauri every 5 seconds. Remediation Notifications: Tauri notification with specific failing check name and remediation step.
reference.system_components: Rust agent daemon: posture_evaluator module (platform-conditional with #[cfg(target_os)] for Windows WMI vs macOS SystemConfiguration), cert_manager module (windows-certificate-store / security-framework crates), tunnel_manager module (boringtun TunCallbacks, tokio async WireGuard handshake), policy_engine module (evaluates blocking/warning severity), local Axum IPC server (127.0.0.1:7777). Tauri 2.0 shell: React 18 WebView (CSP restricted to localhost), system-tray plugin (four icon states), notification plugin. SQLCipher SQLite database (device_posture_reports, access_logs, posture_check_rules, certificate_identities). Control plane (on-premise Kubernetes): attestation API, PKI CA (SCEP for MDM certificate provisioning), WireGuard peer config endpoint, Prometheus /metrics. MDM: JAMF Pro (macOS), Microsoft Intune (Windows).
reference.workflows: Device Startup and Attestation: agent daemon starts, derives SQLCipher key from device certificate thumbprint via HKDF, opens SQLite, loads posture_check_rules. Runs four posture checks in parallel via tokio::join!. Assembles posture report JSON. Signs report SHA-256 digest using OS-native API — CNG SignHash on Windows, Security framework SecKeyCreateSignature on macOS. POST /v1/attestation to control plane with mTLS client certificate. Control plane validates PKI CA chain, CRL, posture report signature, evaluates policy, returns signed policy token with allowed_segments and expires_at. Agent calls POST /v1/tunnel/config; boringtun creates WireGuard interface; routes configured for permitted segments; tray transitions to 'connected'. Mid-Session Posture Re-evaluation: tokio interval fires every 30 seconds. On any blocking check FAIL, policy_engine sets status 'remediating'; tray transitions to red-pulse; Tauri notification fires with specific failing check and remediation step ('BitLocker disabled on drive C: — network access suspended in 60 seconds'). 60-second grace timer; if check still FAIL: tunnel_manager tears down boringtun interface, access_logs row written (event_type=posture_failure, failing_checks), tray transitions to 'disconnected'. Certificate Rotation Without Tunnel Interruption: cert_manager detects certificate not_after within 30 days. Requests new certificate via MDM SCEP renewal. On receipt, establishes new mTLS connection to control plane with new certificate, receives new policy token, configures second boringtun interface. Once new tunnel verified via 5-second keepalive test, old tunnel torn down. Old certificate marked inactive in certificate_identities. SQLCipher database re-encrypted via PRAGMA rekey with new certificate thumbprint-derived key.
reference.data_model: device_posture_reports (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, check_type TEXT NOT NULL, result TEXT CHECK(result IN('pass','fail','unknown')), detail TEXT, evaluated_at INTEGER NOT NULL, signed_report_b64 TEXT) — auto-purged after 30 days. posture_check_rules (id INTEGER PK, check_name TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, check_type TEXT, platform TEXT CHECK(platform IN('windows','macos','all')), edr_service_names TEXT, pass_condition TEXT, severity TEXT CHECK(severity IN('blocking','warning')), last_updated INTEGER). access_logs (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, event_type TEXT NOT NULL, network_segment TEXT, policy_token_id TEXT, event_ts INTEGER NOT NULL, detail TEXT) — auto-purged after 90 days. certificate_identities (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, cert_thumbprint TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, subject_cn TEXT, not_before INTEGER, not_after INTEGER, is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1). All tables in SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite; key derived from device certificate thumbprint via HKDF at daemon startup.
