reference.api_design: Local Axum IPC (127.0.0.1:7777): GET /status returns {state:'connected'|'checking'|'remediating'|'disconnected', policy_token_expires_at:ISO8601, active_segments:[String], last_posture_check:ISO8601}. GET /posture returns [{check_type,result,detail,evaluated_at}]. GET /logs?limit=50 returns recent access_logs. POST /connect triggers manual attestation cycle. POST /disconnect tears down WireGuard tunnel. Control plane REST (mTLS): POST /v1/attestation {posture_report_json,signed_report_b64,cert_thumbprint} returns {policy_token,expires_at,allowed_segments}. POST /v1/tunnel/config {policy_token} returns {wireguard_peer_config}. GET /v1/posture-rules returns updated posture_check_rules.
reference.security_and_compliance: NIST SP 800-207 Zero Trust controls: device identity verification via mTLS client certificate; continuous posture evaluation every 30 seconds with automatic tunnel teardown on blocking posture failure; per-session policy token scoped to minimum required segments. Device private key protected in OS hardware store (Windows CNG TPM-backed, macOS Secure Enclave) — never exported. Posture report signed before transmission. SQLCipher AES-256 with key derived from device certificate thumbprint via HKDF — database inaccessible if device certificate changes. Control plane validates posture report signature on every attestation. mTLS SPKI pin in agent binary; pin rotation requires MDM-distributed binary update. CRL checked on every control plane connection. Certificate expiry alert 30 days before renewal via Prometheus rule. JAMF/Intune MDM profiles enforce agent always running (LaunchDaemon on macOS, Windows Service).
reference.deployment_and_operations: MDM deployment: JAMF Pro deploys macOS .dmg (Apple Developer ID signed); macOS LaunchDaemon plist ensures agent runs as root daemon at boot. Intune deploys Windows .msi (EV code signing cert); Windows Service runs as SYSTEM. GitHub Actions CI: on PR — cargo clippy --deny warnings, cargo test (mock WMI and launchctl via trait injection, coverage >80%), cargo audit; macOS runner for macOS tests, Windows runner for WMI tests; Tauri build both platforms. On merge — Tauri bundler produces signed .dmg and .msi; artifacts uploaded to internal S3; JAMF Smart Group updated via JAMF API; Intune Win32App updated via Graph API. Control plane Kubernetes on-premise (existing, not managed by this project). Certificate rotation handled autonomously by cert_manager module per two-phase runbook.
reference.observability: Control plane Prometheus metrics: agent_attestation_total (result=success/failure, failure_reason=posture/cert/signature), policy_token_issued_total, active_tunnel_count, posture_check_duration_seconds (histogram per check_type), cert_expiry_days (gauge, alert <30). Grafana dashboard: fleet posture compliance by check type (% of devices passing each check), attestation success rate, active tunnel count, certificate expiry timeline. Agent local SQLite access_logs provide per-device diagnostic history accessible to IT via GET /logs during helpdesk investigation.
reference.cost_and_scaling: No cloud infrastructure costs — control plane on-premise. Agent distribution via existing JAMF Pro and Intune MDM licences. GitHub Actions macOS and Windows runners ~$150/month for CI builds. EV code signing certificate ~$300/year. Apple Developer ID $99/year. Total incremental ~$200/month. boringtun userspace WireGuard scales linearly — each active tunnel is a tokio task; tested to 500 concurrent tunnels per control plane node on 8-core hardware.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Windows 11 and macOS 14 agent, four posture checks (patch level, disk encryption, EDR detection with WMI Win32_Service on Windows and launchctl on macOS, firewall state), mTLS attestation, boringtun WireGuard tunnel for one network segment, SQLCipher local database, tray icon four states, remediation notifications, MDM distribution via JAMF and Intune, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Linux Ubuntu 22.04 agent (systemctl for EDR detection), second network segment policy, CrowdStrike Falcon real-time EDR posture signals via Falcon Sensor local API, mid-session posture grace period UI with countdown timer. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Browser extension for per-app network policy, automated certificate rotation, SIEM integration forwarding access_logs, IT admin web console posture compliance dashboard.
reference.development_guidelines: Rust: cargo clippy --deny warnings; no unwrap() in production code. Platform-specific code behind #[cfg(target_os)] with shared PostureChecker trait interface for testability. WMI queries and launchctl calls abstracted behind trait injection to enable mock responses in unit tests. SQLCipher key derivation tested with known certificate thumbprint fixtures. Tauri WebView CSP reviewed in every PR modifying frontend. ADR documents for boringtun choice over kernel WireGuard, SQLCipher key derivation approach, and two-phase certificate rotation design.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: boringtun userspace WireGuard performance overhead under high-throughput workloads. Mitigation: <5% overhead acceptable for enterprise traffic; benchmark in Phase 1; kernel WireGuard migration path documented if needed. Risk: WMI Win32_Service queries can return stale results during EDR service restart. Mitigation: posture check retries twice with 1-second delay before returning FAIL to reduce false-positive tunnel teardowns. Risk: macOS Gatekeeper notarisation required for .dmg distribution outside App Store. Mitigation: GitHub Actions notarisation step with Apple ID credentials in GitHub Secrets. Risk: SQLCipher key derived from certificate thumbprint — if renewed certificate has different thumbprint, database is inaccessible until PRAGMA rekey completes. Mitigation: cert_manager re-encrypts SQLite with new key using PRAGMA rekey before completing certificate rotation. Tradeoff: Tauri over Electron — Rust + Tauri binary ~8MB vs Electron ~100MB, critical for MDM fleet distribution speed.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed boringtun (wireguard-rs) userspace WireGuard — no kernel module dependency for macOS and Windows MVP. EDR detection: Windows WMI Win32_Service query against configurable edr_service_names list in posture_check_rules; macOS launchctl list filtered against configurable process name patterns. Linux EDR detection via systemctl deferred to Phase 2. Two-phase certificate rotation confirmed with PRAGMA rekey for SQLCipher database re-encryption.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision addressing two blocking issues: ISS-001 (sync conflict resolution) and ISS-002 (partial sync retry behavior). For ISS-001: checklist_completion records are unique per (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) — collisions indicate duplicate submissions and the server returns the existing record with a conflict_id. For ISS-002: failed records stay in WatermelonDB with status=pending_retry; next sync attempt re-includes them with exponential backoff; server idempotency key prevents duplicate inserts. Both fixes are targeted at the sync layer and the mobile client retry queue.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified checklist_completion records are unique per (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) — collisions indicate duplicate submissions. Server returns 409 Conflict with conflict_id on duplicate client UUIDs. Mobile client displays conflict dialog with option to overwrite or resolve.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Duplicate checklist submissions are detected and resolved server-side before any data is persisted; mobile client displays clear conflict resolution guidance

fix_report.action_taken: Specified the mobile retry queue: failed records stay in WatermelonDB with status=pending_retry; next sync attempt re-includes them with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s); server idempotency key prevents duplicate inserts. Mobile client shows retry count in sync status screen.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Partial sync failures are handled gracefully; mobile client shows retry progress without requiring manual re-submission

parsed.title: Food Safety Checklist Mobile App — Round 3 Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: An offline-first React Native mobile app for kitchen staff to complete HACCP-compliant food safety checklists with automatic sync to a FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate. WatermelonDB on Expo device stores completed records locally, with conflict resolution on duplicate submissions and retry queue on partial sync failures. Cognito MFA enforced for supervisors and managers; tamper-evident audit trail with SHA-256 content hashing protects compliance records. Health authority export formats are versioned via a server-side template registry.
parsed.architecture_overview: React Native Expo app with WatermelonDB offline SQLite store (local schema pre-loaded with all active checklist templates and location config at login). Sync is initiated via Expo TaskManager background task that POSTs completed records to a FastAPI sync endpoint. The backend validates records against a server-side idempotency key and returns a 409 Conflict if a duplicate client UUID is detected. Failed records are queued in WatermelonDB with status=pending_retry and re-included on the next sync attempt with exponential backoff. A second sync endpoint handles incremental delta since the last_sync_timestamp. Compliance managers can export reports in PDF format via a GET /compliance/export/{format} endpoint. Cognito MFA enforced for supervisors and managers; staff use PIN-only flow. All records include a SHA-256 content hash chained to a location-scoped audit_trail_events table.
parsed.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native 0.71, Expo SDK 51, WatermelonDB 0.10, Expo SecureStore, Expo TaskManager, Expo NetInfo. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.111, async SQLAlchemy 2.0, Pydantic v2, httpx for sync. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, Alembic for migrations. Auth: Cognito user pools with MFA, custom auth challenge for PIN-only staff flow. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, S3 for compliance report PDFs, Buildkite for CI/CD, Datadog APM.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Offline Checklist Completion: staff open checklist, complete CCP items (temperature, allergen, equipment), save to WatermelonDB. Sync: Expo TaskManager background task POSTs completed records to /sync/push; server validates idempotency key, returns 409 Conflict on duplicate UUIDs, re-queues failed records with exponential backoff. Conflict Resolution: mobile displays conflict dialog with overwrite or resolve options; server returns existing record with conflict_id. Sync Queue: sync status screen shows pending_retry count and last_sync_timestamp. Compliance Dashboard: manager view of overdue checklists by location, temperature exceedances, and allergen rate. Health Authority Export: GET /compliance/export/pdf/{format} returns PDF report with checklist data and temperature readings.
parsed.system_components: Expo Router (auth, app) route groups, WatermelonDB provider wrapping app shell, Expo TaskManager (sync background task), FastAPI sync endpoint (POST /sync/push, idempotency key validation), FastAPI compliance export endpoint (GET /compliance/export/pdf/{format}), Cognito user pools (MFA), ALB with WAF (rate limiting 100 req/min per user), RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (server records), S3 bucket (compliance report PDFs), Buildkite (CI/CD), Datadog APM (sync latency and sync queue depth metrics).
parsed.workflows: Sync Workflow: Expo TaskManager fires on completion of checklist; POSTs to /sync/push with {checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date, completed_at}; FastAPI validates idempotency key (UUID + checklist_id + shift_date), returns 200 with existing record if conflict detected; returns 201 with new record if conflict not found; re-queues failed records with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s); sync status screen updates with pending_retry count. Conflict Resolution: mobile displays conflict dialog with overwrite or resolve options; on resolve, server returns 200 with existing record and conflict_id; on overwrite, server returns 200 with new record and conflict_id; on conflict, server returns 409 with conflict_id and conflict_type (duplicate_client_uuid). Compliance Export: manager calls GET /compliance/export/pdf/json; FastAPI validates Cognito JWT, returns 401 if missing; returns 200 with PDF stream; compliance manager saves to S3 and opens in browser or PDF reader.
parsed.data_model: safety_checklists (id UUID PK, location_id UUID FK, checklist_type ENUM opening/closing/hourly/custom, template_version INT, active BOOL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). checklist_completions (id UUID PK, checklist_id UUID FK, staff_id UUID FK, location_id UUID FK, shift_date DATE, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completion_hash SHA-256, conflict_id UUID nullable, resolved_at nullable). temperature_records (id UUID PK, completion_id UUID FK, probe_point VARCHAR, celsius_value NUMERIC, within_range BOOL, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ). allergen_declarations (id UUID PK, completion_id UUID FK, allergen_type VARCHAR, confirmed_by_staff_id UUID FK, confirmed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). equipment_sanitization_logs (id UUID PK, completion_id UUID FK, equipment_id UUID FK, sanitizer_type VARCHAR, contact_time_seconds INT, pass BOOL). audit_trail_events (id UUID PK, location_id UUID FK, sequence_num INT, action VARCHAR, actor_id UUID FK, record_id UUID FK, content_hash SHA-256, chained_hash SHA-256, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
parsed.api_design: POST /sync/push {checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date, completed_at} — idempotency key: UUID + checklist_id + shift_date, returns 200 with existing record or 201 with new record, returns 409 Conflict with conflict_id on duplicate UUIDs. GET /sync/pull {last_sync_timestamp} — returns delta since last_sync_timestamp, returns 404 if no records since last_sync. GET /compliance/export/pdf/{format} — returns PDF stream, returns 401 if missing Cognito JWT. GET /compliance/export/json/{format} — returns JSON array, returns 401 if missing Cognito JWT. GET /users/{id}/profile — returns user profile with Cognito attributes. POST /users/{id}/update — updates Cognito attributes.
parsed.security_and_compliance: Cognito MFA enforced for supervisors and managers; staff use PIN-only flow. Expo SecureStore stores JWT and Cognito access token. TLS 1.3 on ALB. RDS encryption at rest. FSMA compliance: checklist_completions and temperature_records retained 3 years per FSMA; staff accounts anonymized 90 days post-termination; audit_trail_events append-only; CloudTrail for API key and admin actions. Health authority export: PDF reports include checklist data and temperature readings; format versions stored in compliance_export_templates table with version_num and format_name.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster with API service (2-6 tasks, auto-scale on CPU 60%), RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ, ALB with WAF (rate limiting 100 req/min per user). Buildkite pipeline: unit tests (FastAPI integration tests, WatermelonDB reactive queries), Expo build for iOS (TestFlight) and Android (Play Store internal track), smoke tests against staging API. Environments: dev (single-AZ RDS, shared ALB), staging (multi-AZ RDS, load-balanced ALB), production (multi-AZ RDS, auto-scaling ALB). Alembic migrations via Flyway for schema changes. Rollback: ECS rolling update with 50% minimum healthy; previous task definition retained 1-click.
parsed.observability: Datadog APM on FastAPI: sync endpoint p99 latency, sync queue depth (pending_retry count), conflict resolution rate (duplicate UUIDs detected per sync). Expo TaskManager sync events: sync_success, sync_conflict, sync_partial_failure. CloudWatch alarm: sync queue depth > 50 records triggers PagerDuty alert. Sentry for Expo crash reporting via Expo SDK. Compliance dashboard: overdue checklist count by location and type, temperature exceedance rate, allergen declaration rate.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate API service: ~$150/month for 2-6 tasks. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.t3.medium: ~$180/month. ALB with WAF: ~$50/month. S3 for compliance reports: ~$5/month. Total: ~$480/month. Scaling: ECS auto-scales on CPU 60%; RDS scales to db.t3.large if concurrent sync traffic exceeds 500 req/min.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Expo Router with auth and offline store, WatermelonDB offline schema, checklist completion with CCP items, sync /sync/push and /sync/pull endpoints, conflict resolution with 409 Conflict on duplicate UUIDs, Cognito MFA for supervisors and managers, FSMA 3-year retention, compliance dashboard with overdue checklist list, Datadog APM. Phase 2 (6 weeks): IoT temperature probe Bluetooth integration, automated health department submission API, compliance export in PDF and JSON formats, compliance manager dashboard with temperature exceedance heatmap. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Predictive non-conformance alerts (temperature trend exceeding 10°C over 24 hours), automated health authority submission API for jurisdiction-specific formats, compliance export template versioning.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. FastAPI dependency injection for sync endpoints. WatermelonDB reactive queries for offline store queries — no raw SQL. 409 Conflict returned on duplicate UUIDs; never overwrite server-side records. Exponential backoff for sync retry queue (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s). Cognito JWT validation in FastAPI middleware. Alembic migrations for schema changes; no raw SQL in production. Sentry for Expo SDK errors; no console.log in production.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WatermelonDB offline store corruption on device power loss. Mitigation: sync on next connectivity event; device can resume from last_sync_timestamp. Risk: Cognito MFA outage blocks supervisor and manager access. Mitigation: PIN-only staff flow for non-MFA staff; supervisors can request MFA reset via admin panel. Tradeoff: WatermelonDB over SQLite for offline store — adds ~100KB binary overhead but provides reactive query performance for offline store queries. Tradeoff: server-side conflict resolution over client-side — server-side prevents duplicate record creation but requires idempotency key validation.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed WatermelonDB over expo-sqlite for offline store — reactive queries provide offline store query performance. Confirmed server-side conflict resolution over client-side — server-side prevents duplicate record creation. Confirmed Cognito MFA for supervisors and managers — PIN-only flow for non-MFA staff. Confirmed PDF export over CSV — PDF preserves checklist data format for health authority submission.

reference.thinking_summary: The key gap in rounds 1-2 was underspecified sync semantics. ISS-001 needs a concrete unique key on checklist_completion records and explicit server adjudication rules for duplicate client UUIDs. ISS-002 needs a WatermelonDB-level retry queue with sync_status field and exponential backoff. The health authority export template versioning (ISS-003) was resolved in round 2 and just needs to be carried forward correctly. HACCP compliance demands that these sync details are precise — ambiguity in conflict resolution could produce duplicate or missing regulatory records.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified that checklist_completion records are unique on composite key (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date); server treats client-generated UUID as idempotency key — duplicate UUID returns 200 with existing record; composite key collision returns 409 with existing record payload so client can reconcile; documented in api_design and data_model sections
fix_report.expected_outcome: Concurrent submissions from multiple staff on the same checklist type and shift date are deterministically handled without duplicate compliance records

fix_report.action_taken: Defined WatermelonDB sync_status field on local checklist_completion model (values: pending, synced, failed, retrying); POST /sync/push returns per-record status array; mobile client iterates response and marks failed records as sync_status=failed; a Zustand-managed retry scheduler re-queues failed records on next connectivity event with exponential backoff capped at 4 retries before escalating to supervisor alert; described in workflows and deployment sections
fix_report.expected_outcome: Partial sync failures are tracked at the record level in WatermelonDB and retried automatically without data loss or silent drops

reference.title: Food Safety Checklist Mobile Platform — Round 3 Architecture
reference.executive_summary: An offline-first React Native mobile application enabling kitchen staff to complete HACCP-required food safety checklists — including temperature logs, allergen handling confirmations, and equipment sanitization records — with up to 8-hour offline capability via WatermelonDB SQLite storage and deterministic conflict-resolved sync to a FastAPI backend on AWS ECS Fargate. Compliance managers access a cross-location audit dashboard and can export tamper-evident audit trails in health authority-accepted formats.
reference.architecture_overview: React Native Expo SDK 51 app with WatermelonDB as the offline-first reactive data layer, syncing to a Python 3.12 FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate via two purpose-built sync endpoints. Checklist completions carry client-generated UUIDs as idempotency keys; the server enforces a composite unique constraint on (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) to prevent duplicate regulatory records. Authentication flows through Cognito with MFA mandatory for supervisors and managers. All completed records are hashed with SHA-256 and chained into a per-location append-only audit_trail_events sequence, making retroactive tampering detectable. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ stores all server-side compliance data; a Redis ElastiCache instance handles sync session state and rate limiting.
reference.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native, Expo SDK 51, Expo Router, WatermelonDB 0.27, Zustand, Expo NetInfo, Expo TaskManager, Expo SecureStore. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy 2.0, Alembic, Pydantic v2. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, SQLite (WatermelonDB on device), Redis 7 ElastiCache. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, RDS Multi-AZ us-east-1, S3 for compliance PDF exports. CI/CD: Buildkite with iOS and Android lanes, TestFlight and Play Store internal track. Monitoring: Datadog APM, Sentry for mobile crash reporting, CloudWatch for sync metrics.
reference.functional_feature_map: Offline Checklist Completion: staff select shift type and work through CCP rows, entering temperature probe readings, allergen confirmation checkboxes, and sanitization log entries entirely offline with no network dependency. Sync Engine: WatermelonDB push/pull sync to FastAPI with idempotency, composite key conflict detection, and per-record retry queue with exponential backoff. Compliance Audit Trail: server-side SHA-256 hash chaining of all submitted records, detectable chain breaks trigger manager alerts. Health Authority Export: server-side export template registry supporting jurisdiction-specific PDF and CSV formats keyed by format_version param, no app release required for new format additions. Admin Compliance Dashboard: multi-location managers view overdue checklist alerts, temperature exceedance events, completion rate heatmaps, and initiate audit exports.
reference.system_components: React Native Expo app (WatermelonDB local SQLite, Zustand UI state, Expo NetInfo connectivity detection, Expo TaskManager background sync), FastAPI sync service (POST /sync/push with per-record idempotency, GET /sync/pull delta by last_sync_at), FastAPI compliance service (audit trail query, export template rendering), Cognito user pool (MFA enforced for supervisor/manager roles), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (checklist_completions, temperature_records, audit_trail_events), Redis ElastiCache (sync session locks, rate limit counters), S3 (exported compliance PDFs with server-side encryption), ALB with WAF (rate limiting, OWASP rule set).
reference.workflows: Offline Checklist Completion and Sync: Staff log in (Cognito, cached JWT in Expo SecureStore), app pre-fetches active checklist templates and location config into WatermelonDB on login. During shift, staff complete opening temperature checks, allergen station confirmations, and sanitization logs — all persisted locally with sync_status=pending. When network restored, Expo TaskManager triggers POST /sync/push with all pending records as a batch; server processes each record, enforces (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) unique constraint, returns per-record status array. Mobile client iterates response: synced records marked sync_status=synced, failed records marked sync_status=failed and scheduled for retry with exponential backoff (30s, 2m, 8m, 30m); after 4 failures supervisor alert is surfaced. Compliance Audit Trail Generation: After sync, server writes each accepted completion to audit_trail_events with SHA-256 hash of the completion payload chained to the previous event hash for the location. Compliance managers request an export via GET /compliance/export/{format}?location_id=X&from_date=Y, server renders the records using the registered jurisdiction template and returns a signed S3 PDF URL valid for 1 hour.
reference.data_model: safety_checklists (id UUID PK, location_id FK, checklist_type ENUM opening/closing/hourly/custom, template_version INT, active BOOL, created_at). checklist_completions (id UUID PK client-generated idempotency key, checklist_id FK, staff_id FK, location_id FK, shift_date DATE, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completion_hash VARCHAR, sync_status ENUM pending/synced/failed/retrying, UNIQUE(checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date)). temperature_records (id UUID PK, completion_id FK, probe_point VARCHAR, celsius_value DECIMAL, within_range BOOL, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). allergen_declarations (id UUID PK, completion_id FK, allergen_type VARCHAR, confirmed_by_staff_id FK, confirmed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). equipment_sanitization_logs (id UUID PK, completion_id FK, equipment_id FK, sanitizer_type VARCHAR, contact_time_seconds INT, pass BOOL). audit_trail_events (id UUID PK, location_id FK, sequence_num BIGINT, action VARCHAR, actor_id FK, record_id UUID, content_hash VARCHAR, chained_hash VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE(location_id, sequence_num)). location_profiles (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, jurisdiction_code VARCHAR, export_format_version VARCHAR, timezone VARCHAR).
reference.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. POST /sync/push body: {records: [{id, checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date, items: [...], submitted_at}]} returns {results: [{id, status: synced|conflict|error, existing_record_id?}]}. GET /sync/pull?location_id=X&since=ISO8601 returns delta of records modified since timestamp. GET /compliance/export/{format}?location_id=X&from_date=Y&to_date=Z returns {download_url, expires_at}. GET /checklists?location_id=X&active=true returns checklist templates for pre-fetch. PATCH /checklists/{id}/completions/{cid}/approve for supervisor sign-off. Auth: Cognito JWT bearer on all endpoints; location_id scope enforced server-side.
reference.security_and_compliance: FSMA compliance: all checklist_completion and temperature_record data retained 3 years with automated RDS snapshot lifecycle policy. HACCP audit integrity: SHA-256 content hash per completion record, chained to per-location sequence in audit_trail_events; hash chain verification endpoint available for health inspector review. Cognito MFA mandatory for supervisor and manager roles; staff use PIN-based custom auth challenge. Expo SecureStore for JWT token storage on device (hardware-backed keystore on Android). TLS 1.3 on all API traffic; WAF OWASP rule set on ALB. RBAC enforced in FastAPI dependency injection: staff scope to assigned location_id, managers have read-all. Allergen declaration records are immutable post-submission — PATCH forbidden on allergen_declarations.
reference.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1: API service (min 2 tasks, max 6, CPU 60% scale-out), RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ primary in us-east-1a, standby in us-east-1b, automated failover <60s. Redis ElastiCache single-node for sync session state (non-critical). ALB with WAF in front of ECS tasks. Buildkite CI pipeline: Python unit tests (pytest, 85% coverage gate), FastAPI integration tests against Dockerized PostgreSQL, Expo EAS Build for iOS (TestFlight distribution) and Android (Play Store internal track), Lighthouse-equivalent API smoke tests against staging. Alembic migrations run as ECS one-off task before new API deployment. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. OTA updates for non-native JS changes via Expo EAS Update, bypassing app store review cycle.
reference.observability: Datadog APM on FastAPI service: trace sync push/pull endpoints, alert on p99 latency >1.5s or error rate >2%. CloudWatch custom metrics: sync_queue_depth per location, conflict_rate (409 responses / total sync requests), retry_escalation_count (records reaching 4th retry). Sentry for React Native: crash reports tagged by device OS version and app version. Grafana dashboard (Datadog source): sync health per location, overdue checklist count by location, temperature exceedance rate by facility. PagerDuty alert on: API error rate >2% for 5 minutes, hash chain integrity check failure for any location, sync queue depth >500 records for >30 minutes.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $800/month at 50 locations with 200 daily checklist completions. ECS Fargate auto-scales from 2 to 6 tasks on CPU threshold; peak sync load at shift-change handled by Redis-based sync session concurrency control. RDS Multi-AZ ~$350/month; Fargate tasks ~$250/month at baseline. S3 export storage minimal (<5GB/year). EAS Build credits managed under Expo plan. Scaling to 500 locations requires read replica for compliance dashboard queries and potential move to Aurora Serverless for cost efficiency at variable load.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Cognito auth with MFA, opening and closing checklist templates for 10-location pilot, offline WatermelonDB storage, POST /sync/push and GET /sync/pull with idempotency and conflict resolution, basic admin dashboard showing completion rates, Buildkite CI with TestFlight distribution. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Temperature probe integration (manual entry and future Bluetooth), allergen declaration workflow, equipment sanitization logs, health authority export templates for 3 target jurisdictions, audit trail hash chain verification UI. Phase 3 (8 weeks): IoT Bluetooth temperature probe auto-capture via Expo BLE, automated health department submission API integration, predictive non-conformance alerts based on historical temperature trend analysis, 500-location scalability hardening with Aurora Serverless migration.
reference.development_guidelines: Python: black + isort + mypy strict in CI. FastAPI endpoints typed with Pydantic v2 models; no raw dict responses. 85% pytest coverage required for sync and compliance endpoints. React Native: ESLint + Prettier, TypeScript strict mode. WatermelonDB model changes require migration files in the models/migrations directory — no schema changes without a migration. All sync endpoint changes require integration test coverage against a Dockerized PostgreSQL instance in CI. FSMA retention policies encoded as Alembic data migrations, not application-layer logic, to ensure they survive code refactors. ADR documents required for any change to the conflict resolution or audit chain strategy.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WatermelonDB sync protocol complexity — custom push/pull adds implementation burden vs. using a managed sync service. Mitigation: strict integration test suite for sync semantics; conflict resolution encapsulated in a single SyncService class. Risk: Expo OTA update delivery fails on devices with poor connectivity — compliance checklist templates could be stale. Mitigation: version-check at launch; if template_version mismatch exceeds 1 minor version, force full sync before allowing checklist access. Tradeoff: SHA-256 hash chaining adds per-record compute on server but is required for tamper-evident audit trail; acceptable overhead at expected record volume. Risk: iOS background sync limitations — Expo TaskManager background fetch is throttled by iOS. Mitigation: primary sync triggered on foreground resume; background fetch as secondary mechanism only. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL over DynamoDB for relational integrity of checklist-completion-temperature record hierarchy; cost higher but correctness critical for compliance data.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Composite unique key (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) confirmed as the conflict detection boundary — a completion is per-staff per shift, not shared. Export template versioning uses server-side registry keyed by jurisdiction_code avoiding app store release dependency. WatermelonDB chosen over direct expo-sqlite for its built-in sync primitives and reactive query subscriptions. EAS Update (OTA) confirmed for JS-layer changes; native module changes still require app store submission. Buildkite selected over GitHub Actions for superior iOS simulator support and parallelism.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for a telemedicine urgent care platform. Two high-severity issues from round 1 must be resolved: replace Next.js 14 Pages Router with Next.js 15 App Router throughout, and remove the custom JWT service and replace it with Cognito user pools and MSAL. The PCI-DSS SAQ A requirement is addressed by specifying Stripe.js hosted card fields so raw card numbers never reach the backend. The plan quality is weak per manifest: the technology_stack and security_and_compliance sections are thin and the data_model section is incomplete.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced all Next.js 14 Pages Router references with Next.js 15 App Router patterns throughout the frontend sections. Updated technology_stack and frontend sections to reflect Next.js 15 App Router and TypeScript.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Technology stack now fully aligned with the contract Next.js 15 App Router requirement.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced custom JWT service with Cognito user pools and MSAL. Updated security_and_compliance and api_design sections to reflect Cognito JWT issuance and MSAL for physician role claims.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Auth provider now specified as Cognito user pools with MSAL for physician role claims, resolving the custom JWT conflict.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Stripe.js Payment Element specification with confirm that backend only receives PaymentIntent IDs, never raw card numbers. Updated payment_processing_section and security_and_compliance to reflect Stripe.js SAQ A scope.
fix_report.expected_outcome: PCI-DSS SAQ A scope achieved by using Stripe.js hosted card fields; raw card numbers never reach the backend.

parsed.title: Telemedicine Urgent Care Platform - Round 2 Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: A fullstack telemedicine platform for urgent care virtual visits enabling patients to queue for on-demand WebRTC video consultations with on-call physicians. Built with Next.js 15 App Router on Vercel and Node.js 22 Express on ECS Fargate. Real-time wait time estimates and physician encounter dashboards manage concurrent virtual visits. Payments are processed via Stripe with Stripe.js hosted card fields for PCI-DSS SAQ A scope reduction. Cognito user pools handle patient and physician authentication with MFA for physicians. Twilio Video provides WebRTC session infrastructure behind the Express auth wall.
parsed.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: Next.js 15 App Router frontend on Vercel (HTTPS, TLS 1.3) communicates over HTTPS to a Node.js 22 Express backend on ECS Fargate (HTTPS, TLS 1.3) behind an ALB. The Express backend manages queue state (Redis sorted set), Twilio Video room creation, encounter note completion, and Stripe payment processing. Cognito user pools on AWS Cognito handle patient and physician authentication with MFA for physicians. Redis 7 on ElastiCache stores the virtual queue sorted set and SSE-encrypted patient wait time data. Stripe handles payment processing with Stripe.js hosted card fields for PCI-DSS SAQ A scope reduction. Datadog APM sidecar on ECS Fargate provides corporate-standard observability.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 15 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Zustand, React Query, Twilio Video React SDK, @aws-amplify/auth (Cognito), MSAL for physician role claims. Backend: Node.js 22, Express, TypeScript, Twilio Video SDK, Stripe Node SDK, Redis client, @aws-sdk/s3 for PHI storage. Database: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 with KMS, ElastiCache Redis 7. Auth: Cognito user pools, MSAL for physician role claims. Hosting: Vercel Pro (Next.js), ECS Fargate (Express), ALB, Cognito BAA. Observability: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Patient Queue Entry: chief complaint form with insurance/payment info, Stripe Payment Element for copay, SSE-encrypted wait time update, Twilio Video room auto-launch on queue position change. Physician Encounter Dashboard: active virtual visit cards with video join, queue depth indicator, encounter note form (chief complaint, assessment, plan, diagnosis codes), prescription routing. Structured Encounter Note Completion: note form with structured fields (chief complaint, assessment, plan, diagnosis codes ICD10[], status) with required fields. After-Visit Summary: patient receives summary with encounter note and prescription status. Stripe Payment Processing: payment intent creation on Stripe.js hosted card fields, webhook handler for payment_succeeded and payment_failed events. Encounter Export: physician can export encounter note as PDF for EHR integration.
parsed.system_components: Vercel Pro (Next.js 15 App Router, HTTPS, TLS 1.3), ALB (HTTPS, TLS 1.3), ECS Fargate (Express backend, 3-10 tasks, 10-minute auto-scaling), ElastiCache Redis 7 (virtual queue sorted set, SSE-encrypted patient wait time data), Aurora PostgreSQL 16 with KMS (patients, virtual_queue_entries, encounter_notes, payment_transactions), Cognito user pools (patient and physician clients, Cognito JWTs), Twilio Video (WebRTC rooms, Twilio Programmable Video SDK), Stripe (payment processing, Stripe.js hosted card fields), Datadog APM sidecar (ECS Fargate), PagerDuty (alerts), S3 (PHI storage for encounter notes), AWS Cognito BAA (HIPAA BAA for Aurora and ECS), AWS SNS (payment webhook notifications).
parsed.workflows: Patient Queue Entry: Patient opens queue page, completes chief complaint and insurance/payment info, selects Stripe Payment Element (Stripe.js hosted card fields only, backend never sees raw card numbers), Vercel validates payment, Express creates virtual_queue_entry with status=waiting, Twilio Video creates room with access token, Express sends SSE-encrypted SSE-128-AES-256 key to patient via WebSocket, patient receives SSE stream update on every queue position change, Twilio Video room auto-launched on queue position change. Physician Encounter: Physician opens encounter card, sees queue depth indicator, clicks join video session, Express creates encounter_note with status=draft, physician completes structured note form with required fields (chief complaint, assessment, plan, diagnosis codes ICD10[]), physician saves note, Express updates encounter_note.status=finalized, Twilio Video room ends. Payment Processing: Patient completes Stripe Payment Element, Stripe creates PaymentIntent with Stripe.js hosted card fields, Stripe.js sends to backend, Express creates payment_transaction with status=pending, Stripe webhook handler POSTs payment_succeeded or payment_failed to Express, Express updates payment_transaction.status=succeeded or payment_transaction.status=failed, patient receives confirmation email.
parsed.data_model: patients (id UUID PK, cognito_sub VARCHAR UNIQUE, first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR, dob_encrypted BYTEA, insurance_member_id_encrypted BYTEA, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). virtual_queue_entries (id UUID PK, patient_id UUID FK, chief_complaint TEXT, estimated_wait_minutes INT, status ENUM waiting/in_session/completed/abandoned, joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ). video_sessions (id UUID PK, queue_entry_id UUID FK, physician_id UUID FK, twilio_room_sid VARCHAR, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ, duration_seconds INT). encounter_notes (id UUID PK, video_session_id UUID FK, physician_id UUID FK, patient_id UUID FK, chief_complaint_text TEXT, assessment TEXT, plan TEXT, diagnosis_codes UUID[] REFERENCES diagnosis_codes, status ENUM draft/finalized). payment_transactions (id UUID PK, queue_entry_id UUID FK, stripe_payment_intent_id VARCHAR, amount_cents INT, currency VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/succeeded/failed, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). diagnosis_codes (id UUID PK, code VARCHAR, name VARCHAR, system VARCHAR).
parsed.api_design: POST /auth/token (Cognito JWT issuance, MSAL for physician role claims). GET /patients/queue (patient own queue, SSE-encrypted wait time data). POST /patients/queue (add patient to queue, returns queue_entry_id). GET /patients/queue/{id} (patient queue detail, SSE-encrypted). POST /encounters/create (physician create encounter, returns encounter_note_id). GET /encounters/{id}/note (physician view encounter note, SSE-encrypted). PATCH /encounters/{id}/note (physician finalize note, returns note_id). POST /payments/charge (physician initiate payment, returns PaymentIntent ID). GET /payments/charge/{intentId} (physician payment status). POST /payments/charge/confirm (physician confirm payment, returns payment_transaction_id). GET /encounters/{id}/export (physician export PDF of encounter note).
parsed.security_and_compliance: HIPAA controls: Cognito user pools with patient and physician clients, Cognito JWTs verified in Express middleware on all routes, Cognito JWTs expire in 1 hour, Cognito MFA enforced for physician client, Cognito BAA covers Aurora and ECS. PCI-DSS SAQ A: Stripe.js Payment Element with Stripe.js hosted card fields; backend never receives raw card numbers; Stripe.js handles all payment processing; Stripe webhook handler processes payment_succeeded and payment_failed events. TLS 1.3 on all Vercel routes and ALB. Aurora RDS KMS encryption for PHI tables. Twilio Video: WebRTC negotiation failure fallback to Twilio Programmable Video retry with exponential backoff. HIPAA BAA with AWS for Aurora and ECS. CloudTrail for all AWS API calls. Custom audit table encounter_access_log for all PHI read events with physician_id, patient_id, encounter_id, and timestamp.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI/CD: eslint + tsc, jest unit tests for queue and note handlers, docker build and push to ECR, ECS deploy with rolling update, Vercel deploy triggered by Vercel GitHub integration. Environments: dev (ECS single task, Aurora single AZ, Vercel dev), staging (ECS 2 tasks, Aurora multi-AZ, Vercel staging), production (ECS 3-10 tasks, Aurora multi-AZ with read replica, Vercel Pro, HIPAA BAA). Environments use separate Cognito clients and IAM roles. Environments use different Stripe test keys. Environments use different S3 bucket prefixes for PHI storage. Environments use different AWS region endpoints.
parsed.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on ECS Fargate: Express request traces with patient_id and encounter_id tags, Twilio Video SDK call traces, Stripe webhook handler traces. Custom Datadog metrics: queue_depth (gauge), video_session_success_rate (gauge), payment_processing_latency_p99 (histogram), encounter_note_completion_time (histogram), queue_abandonment_rate (gauge). PagerDuty alerts: queue_depth >100 (critical), video_success_rate <95% (high), payment_processing_latency_p99 >30s (high), encounter_note_completion_time >60s (high).
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2,800/month at launch: ECS Fargate 3 tasks ~$180, Aurora PostgreSQL multi-AZ ~$400, ElastiCache Redis r6g.large ~$120, Vercel Pro ~$200, Stripe ~$100, Twilio Video ~$50, Datadog ~$200, S3 ~$50, Cognito ~$50. Scaling: ECS auto-scales to 3-10 tasks on CPU 60% target; Aurora multi-AZ handles 500 concurrent video sessions at launch. Redis r6g.large sufficient for queue state at this scale.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Patient queue entry with SSE wait time, Stripe Payment Element with Stripe.js hosted card fields, Twilio Video room auto-launch, Cognito patient and physician clients, Cognito MFA for physicians, encounter note completion with structured fields, Stripe payment processing, PagerDuty alerts, GitHub Actions CI/CD. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Physician encounter dashboard with queue depth indicator, after-visit summary email, prescription routing, diagnosis code export, EHR integration for encounter note export, HIPAA BAA with AWS. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Scheduled telehealth appointments, specialty virtual care routing, CMS telehealth coverage verification, Twilio Video room recording for audit trail.
parsed.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for all Next.js 15 components. ESLint + tsc in CI. Jest unit tests for queue and note handlers. No raw card numbers in Express request bodies or logs. Stripe.js hosted card fields only; backend never receives raw card numbers. Cognito JWTs must be validated in Express middleware before any route. Custom audit table encounter_access_log must be included in all PHI read events. Twilio Video SDK WebRTC negotiation failure fallback must be tested in staging.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Twilio Video room creation failure causes patient to remain in waiting state. Mitigation: Twilio Programmable Video retry with exponential backoff; fallback to Twilio Network Traversal Service for fallback rooms. Risk: Cognito BAA with AWS is a single point of failure for PHI. Mitigation: CloudTrail for all AWS API calls; custom audit table for all PHI read events. Tradeoff: Next.js 15 App Router over Next.js 14 Pages Router adds 10-20% frontend build time but eliminates the Pages Router pattern conflict. Tradeoff: Stripe.js hosted card fields over Stripe Connect for SAQ A scope reduction adds Stripe.js SDK complexity but eliminates raw card number exposure.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Selected Next.js 15 App Router over Next.js 14 Pages Router to resolve technology stack conflict. Selected Stripe.js hosted card fields over Stripe Connect for SAQ A scope reduction. Selected Cognito user pools over custom JWT service to resolve auth provider conflict. Selected Twilio Video over Zoom for WebRTC to avoid Zoom's data processing agreements.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision with three contract violations to fix: ISS-001 (Next.js 14 vs 15), ISS-002 (custom JWT vs Cognito), and ISS-003 (PCI scope). The plan quality is intentionally flawed - while I am addressing the focus issues in the fix_report, the revised plan will still contain a subtle internal inconsistency: the deployment_and_operations section will reference the Vercel frontend as requiring no HIPAA BAA while the contract security_baseline explicitly requires a Vercel Pro HIPAA BAA, creating a compliance gap that an auditor should flag.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced all Next.js 14 Pages Router references with Next.js 15 App Router patterns throughout technology_stack, functional_feature_map, and workflows sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Plan technology stack matches contract frontend_stack specification enabling correct implementation.

fix_report.action_taken: Removed custom JWT issuance service; replaced with AWS Cognito user pools using patient and physician app clients with Cognito JWT verification in Express middleware via aws-jwt-verify library.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Auth implementation matches contract access_model Cognito specification and eliminates custom key management overhead.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified Stripe.js Payment Element hosted card fields so raw card numbers never reach the backend; backend only creates PaymentIntent and receives PaymentIntent ID from frontend, achieving PCI SAQ A scope.
fix_report.expected_outcome: PCI-DSS scope reduced to SAQ A; no card data handled in backend code or stored in Aurora.

reference.title: Telemedicine Urgent Care Virtual Visit Platform Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A Next.js 15 App Router and Node.js Express full-stack telemedicine platform on AWS ECS Fargate enabling patients to join on-demand urgent care video queues, pay copays via Stripe, and complete WebRTC video encounters with on-call physicians. Cognito user pools authenticate both patient and physician roles. Aurora PostgreSQL stores encounter notes and PHI with KMS encryption. Redis sorted sets manage the real-time virtual queue. Twilio Video handles WebRTC room creation and TURN server fallback.
reference.architecture_overview: Next.js 15 App Router deployed on Vercel communicates over HTTPS to an Express API on ECS Fargate. Patients enter the virtual queue via the Next.js patient flow; the backend maintains queue position in a Redis sorted set and pushes wait time updates via SSE. When a physician accepts the next patient, the backend creates a Twilio Video room and returns access tokens to both participants. Cognito user pools issue JWTs verified by aws-jwt-verify in Express middleware. Stripe.js Payment Element collects copay card data client-side; the backend creates a PaymentIntent and receives only the ID. Aurora PostgreSQL with KMS CMK stores encounter notes, diagnoses, and patient PHI. Note: Vercel deployment does not have a HIPAA BAA configured, which creates a compliance gap for any PHI in Next.js server actions or edge functions.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 15 with TypeScript, App Router, Tailwind CSS, Zustand for queue state, React Query, @twilio/video React hooks, Stripe.js Payment Element. Backend: Node.js 22, Express with TypeScript, aws-jwt-verify for Cognito JWT validation, Twilio Video Node SDK, Stripe Node SDK. Database: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 with KMS CMK, ElastiCache Redis 7. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, ECR, Cognito user pools, CloudWatch Logs, Datadog APM sidecar.
reference.functional_feature_map: Patient Queue Entry: Next.js 15 App Router page with chief complaint multi-select, insurance lookup, and Stripe Payment Element for copay pre-authorization. Virtual Waiting Room: SSE-driven real-time wait time counter with queue position. WebRTC Video Session: Twilio Video room with camera/mic controls, participant tiles, and in-session chat for non-PHI messages. Physician Encounter Dashboard: active encounter cards showing patient chief complaint, queue depth badge, one-click video join. Encounter Note Completion: structured SOAP note form with ICD-10 autocomplete, plan text, and prescription routing dropdown. After-Visit Summary: automated generation on note finalization with patient email delivery.
reference.system_components: Next.js 15 App Router on Vercel Pro (HIPAA BAA status unclear in this revision). Express API on ECS Fargate (3 min, 10 max instances) behind ALB with WAF. Cognito user pools (patient and physician app clients, physician MFA TOTP enforced). ElastiCache Redis r6g.large for virtual queue sorted set and session tokens. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ with KMS CMK. Twilio Video rooms for WebRTC. Stripe for copay payment processing with hosted card fields. Datadog APM sidecars on ECS tasks.
reference.workflows: Patient Queue Workflow: Patient completes chief complaint form, Stripe Payment Element pre-authorizes copay, POST /queue-entries creates queue entry in Redis sorted set with score=joined_at_unix; SSE stream /queue/wait-time pushes updated estimates every 30 seconds; physician accepts next patient triggering Twilio Video room creation via POST /video-sessions; Cognito access tokens exchanged for Twilio Video access tokens; video session auto-launches in browser. Encounter Completion Workflow: Physician completes SOAP note with ICD-10 codes and plan, POST /encounter-notes with finalized=true triggers after-visit summary generation, Stripe captures pre-authorized payment, note status updated to finalized.
reference.data_model: patients (id UUID PK, cognito_sub TEXT, first_name, last_name, dob_encrypted BYTEA, insurance_member_id_encrypted BYTEA, created_at). virtual_queue_entries (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, chief_complaint TEXT[], status ENUM waiting/in_session/completed/abandoned, payment_intent_id TEXT, joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ). video_sessions (id UUID PK, queue_entry_id FK, physician_id FK, twilio_room_sid TEXT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ). encounter_notes (id UUID PK, video_session_id FK, physician_id FK, patient_id FK, subjective TEXT, objective TEXT, assessment TEXT, plan TEXT, diagnosis_codes TEXT[], status ENUM draft/finalized, finalized_at TIMESTAMPTZ). payment_transactions (id UUID PK, queue_entry_id FK, stripe_payment_intent_id TEXT, amount_cents INT, status ENUM pending/captured/failed).
reference.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. POST /queue-entries (patient joins queue; body: chief_complaint[], payment_intent_id). GET /queue/wait-time (SSE stream; physician_count, estimated_wait_minutes). POST /video-sessions (backend creates Twilio room; returns twilio_access_token for patient and physician). POST /encounter-notes (physician creates note). PATCH /encounter-notes/{id}/finalize (triggers after-visit summary and Stripe capture). GET /physician/queue (active queue for physician dashboard). All routes require Cognito JWT in Authorization header.
reference.security_and_compliance: Cognito user pools with physician app client enforcing MFA TOTP; patient app client with optional MFA. aws-jwt-verify library validates Cognito JWTs in Express middleware. Stripe.js Payment Element hosted card fields: backend creates PaymentIntent via Stripe API, returns client_secret to frontend, Stripe.js confirms payment client-side - raw card numbers never reach Express API, achieving PCI SAQ A. Aurora KMS CMK encrypts PHI at rest. TLS 1.3 on ALB. WAF rules blocking SQLi and XSS on ALB. HIPAA BAA with AWS covers Aurora and ECS. Note: Vercel HIPAA BAA not confirmed for this deployment - Next.js server actions or middleware touching PHI would fall outside HIPAA BAA coverage.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: eslint + tsc + jest, docker build and push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy to staging, integration smoke tests, manual gate, ECS rolling deploy to production. Vercel deploys triggered by GitHub push via Vercel GitHub integration with no HIPAA configuration applied. ECS Fargate autoscale on ALB RequestCountPerTarget >500 per task. Aurora multi-AZ with automated backups. ElastiCache Redis cluster mode disabled (single node r6g.large) for simplicity. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day Stripe webhook secret rotation.
reference.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on each ECS task with distributed traces. Custom metrics: virtual_queue_depth (alert >100), video_session_success_rate (alert <95%), payment_capture_success_rate (alert <99%), encounter_note_completion_time_p90. CloudWatch Logs for ECS stdout. PagerDuty escalation for all production alerts.
reference.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate 3 min tasks at $0.04048/vCPU/hr: ~$200/month baseline. Aurora db.r6g.large multi-AZ: ~$400/month. ElastiCache r6g.large: ~$150/month. Twilio Video: ~$400/month at 500 daily sessions (0.004/min * 10 min avg * 500). Stripe fees pass-through to encounter billing. Vercel Pro: ~$20/month. Total ~$1300/month well under $3000 budget.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Patient queue entry with chief complaint and Stripe copay pre-auth, physician dashboard with queue view, Twilio Video session launch, basic SOAP note, Cognito auth. Phase 2 (4 weeks): ICD-10 autocomplete, after-visit summary email, Stripe capture on note finalization, encounter history. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Prescription routing integration, payer claim submission, physician performance analytics dashboard.
reference.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode on frontend and backend. ESLint with @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any enforced in CI. Encounter note content must not appear in Express request logs; use log scrubbing middleware on /encounter-notes routes. Stripe webhook signature verification required in payment handler. Cognito JWT claims must be the sole source of role authorization; no role stored in DB. PHI fields in Aurora must use encrypted column types.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vercel deployment without HIPAA BAA exposes PHI if Next.js server actions or edge middleware process encounter data. Mitigation incomplete in this revision - PHI access in Next.js server components must be audited. Risk: Twilio Video TURN server dependency for patients on restrictive networks; mitigated by Twilio Network Traversal Service automatic fallback. Tradeoff: ECS Fargate over Lambda for WebSocket connection persistence; Lambda cold starts incompatible with sub-second queue update requirements.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Twilio Video over Amazon Chime per existing Twilio vendor relationship. Confirmed Stripe SAQ A eligibility via Stripe.js hosted fields - backend never handles raw card data. Auth provider corrected to Cognito per contract. Next.js version corrected to 15 App Router per contract.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 3 must resolve ISS-002 (step lock timeout) and ISS-003 (CIM import idempotency). For ISS-002: add explicit 10-minute Redis TTL with PagerDuty alert on auto-release, and StepLockManager with 10-minute timeout. For ISS-003: add ON CONFLICT upsert and import_batch_id rollback in CIM import pipeline. Both fixes are targeted and specific to the round 3 plan scope.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit 10-minute Redis TTL to StepLockManager with PagerDuty alert on auto-release. Added StepLockManager class with acquire_lock(isolation_point_id) and release_lock(isolation_point_id) methods. StepLockManager acquires lock before step execution, releases after 10 minutes with PagerDuty alert on auto-release.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Step lock timeout prevents concurrent field crew operations on the same isolation_point within 10 minutes; PagerDuty alert ensures on-call team is notified of automatic release

fix_report.action_taken: Added ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE to CIM import pipeline. Each import batch assigned batch_id. On conflict, upsert cim_mrid with batch_id. On import_batch_id not found, DELETE FROM topology_nodes WHERE import_batch_id = :id to revert bad import.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CIM topology_nodes are idempotent; re-imports safely append new elements without duplicating existing ones

parsed.title: Distribution Grid Switching Order Management Platform v3
parsed.executive_summary: A Vue 3 + FastAPI web application on GKE Autopilot for medium-voltage distribution grid switching order management, enabling control room dispatchers to create and approve switching orders, field crew step execution with real-time SVG topology visualization, and topology rule validation before approval. Round 3 resolves step lock timeout and CIM import idempotency. Keycloak OIDC with 15-min JWT, Istio mTLS STRICT, PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL, and Redis 7 Memorystore.
parsed.architecture_overview: Three-service GKE Autopilot deployment: topology-api (WebSocket broadcast, CIM import), switching-order-api (REST + WebSocket), integration-api (GIS/SCADA data ingest). All services behind Cloud Load Balancing with Istio service mesh enforcing mTLS STRICT. Vue 3 SPA served via Cloud CDN with Keycloak OIDC silent-check on each route. Topology state changes from SCADA integration validated against isolation_points.current_state before write. StepLockManager acquires exclusive Redis lock on isolation_point before step execution, releasing after 10 minutes with PagerDuty alert on auto-release. CIM import uses ON CONFLICT upsert with batch_id for idempotency. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with automated failover. Redis 7 Memorystore for topology pub/sub and step_lock entries.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Vue Router 4, D3.js force-layout for SVG topology, Keycloak OIDC, Vue3 Router 4 guard. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0, pandas_profiling for CIM validation, Redis 7 for pub/sub and step locks. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with automated failover. Cache: Redis 7 Memorystore. Auth: Keycloak OIDC with LDAP federation. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot, Istio service mesh, Cloud Load Balancing, Cloud SQL, Memorystore Redis, Artifact Registry, GitLab CI, Grafana, Prometheus, Google Cloud Logging.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Topology Viewer: SVG single-line diagram with D3.js force-layout showing all active isolation_points, breaker/recloser/switch/fuse status, and topology_nodes. Topology Rule Engine: validates switching sequence against network_segments connectivity graph before approval; returns list of topology_rule_violations. Switching Order Management: create order with step authoring, dispatcher approval workflow, field crew step execution with photo evidence upload. Step Execution Tracker: field crew step list with status, crew assignment, and photo evidence. CIM Import Pipeline: GIS/SCADA data ingestion endpoint with idempotency via ON CONFLICT and batch_id rollback. Audit Log Viewer: structured JSON audit trail for all write operations.
parsed.system_components: Cloud Load Balancing (GKE Autopilot, VPN-only ingress, mTLS STRICT), Istio sidecar injection (mTLS STRICT on all services), topology-api (FastAPI, 2-6 pods, WebSocket connection count metric), switching-order-api (FastAPI, 2-4 pods, REST + WebSocket), integration-api (FastAPI, 1-2 pods, GIS/SCADA data ingest), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (HA, us-central1), Memorystore Redis 7 (pub/sub and step locks), Cloud CDN (Vue SPA), Keycloak (OIDC, LDAP federation), Prometheus (switching_orders_created_total, step_locks_acquired_total, step_lock_timeout_total), Grafana (topology change rate, lock timeout rate), Google Cloud Logging (structured JSON audit trail), PagerDuty (step lock auto-release alert, CIM import validation failure alert).
parsed.workflows: Switching Order Approval Workflow: Dispatcher creates switching order via switching-order-api POST /switching-orders with step authoring. Vue frontend calls GET /topology/network-model with current order_id; topology-api validates order against network_segments connectivity graph and calls TopologyRuleEngine.engine.validate_sequence(order_id). On validation success, order transitions to approved status and StepLockManager acquires exclusive Redis lock on first isolation_point of order. Field crew executes steps in order via step-execution-api POST /switching-orders/:id/steps/:step_id/execute. Vue frontend calls GET /topology/network-model with current order_id; topology-api validates step against isolation_points.current_state and network_segments.connectivity_graph. On validation success, step transitions to completed status. StepLockManager releases lock after 10 minutes. On auto-release, PagerDuty alert fires. CIM Import Workflow: Integration-api receives GIS/SCADA data via POST /integration/cim-import with multipart CIM XML. CIM parser uses pandas_profiling to validate CIM element types and schema. ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE upserts cim_mrid with batch_id. On import_batch_id not found, DELETE FROM topology_nodes WHERE import_batch_id = :id. On validation failure, CIM parser returns validation_report with CIM element IDs causing parse errors. Vue frontend displays validation_report in step execution form.
parsed.data_model: switching_orders (id UUID PK, title VARCHAR, region_code VARCHAR, outage_ticket_id UUID FK, created_by keycloak_sub, approved_by keycloak_sub, status ENUM draft/pending_approval/approved/in_execution/completed/cancelled, planned_start TIMESTAMPTZ, planned_end TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). switching_steps (id UUID PK, order_id UUID FK, step_number INT, isolation_point_id UUID FK, action ENUM open/close/tag/remove_tag, assigned_crew_id UUID FK, status ENUM pending/locked/completed/failed, completed_by keycloak_sub, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, photo_evidence_url VARCHAR). isolation_points (id UUID PK, cim_mrid VARCHAR UNIQUE, name VARCHAR, network_segment_id UUID FK, device_type ENUM breaker/recloser/switch/fuse, current_state ENUM open/closed/unknown, last_state_changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). network_segments (id UUID PK, cim_mrid VARCHAR UNIQUE, name VARCHAR, voltage_kv DECIMAL, upstream_node_id UUID FK, downstream_nodes JSONB, segment_type ENUM feeder/lateral/tie). topology_nodes (id UUID PK, cim_mrid VARCHAR UNIQUE, substation_id UUID FK, bus_voltage_kv DECIMAL, connected_segments JSONB, import_batch_id UUID, status ENUM pending/imported/imported_with_errors). topology_rule_violations (id UUID PK, order_id UUID FK, violation_type VARCHAR, cim_element_id VARCHAR, violation_description TEXT, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ). crew_assignments (id UUID PK, switching_order_id UUID FK, crew_leader_id keycloak_sub, crew_members JSONB, assigned_steps JSONB). audit_logs (id UUID PK, keycloak_sub VARCHAR, display_name VARCHAR, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, changed_fields JSONB, ip_address INET, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ).
parsed.api_design: REST: POST /switching-orders (create order), GET /switching-orders/:id (detail), PATCH /switching-orders/:id/approve (approve), POST /switching-orders/:id/steps/:step_id/execute (field crew step execution), GET /topology/network-model?order_id= (current order SVG), GET /topology/network-model?order_id= (step status and photo evidence), POST /topology/cim-import (multipart CIM XML, idempotency via ON CONFLICT), GET /topology/cim-import/validation-report?order_id= (CIM validation report), GET /audit-log?order_id= (audit log for order), PATCH /admin/users (admin user management). WebSocket: ws://topology-api/ws/topology-updates (broadcasts device_state_changed events to all connected clients). POST /topology/cim-import (multipart CIM XML, idempotency via ON CONFLICT and batch_id rollback).
parsed.security_and_compliance: Keycloak OIDC: JWT 15-minute expiry, silent-check-sso, HTTPOnly cookie, X-Frame-Options DENY. Istio mTLS STRICT: all three services enforce mTLS on all internal service-to-service traffic. NERC CIP-002: audit_logs table captures keycloak_sub, display_name, action, resource_type, resource_id, changed_fields JSONB, ip_address, timestamp; shipped to Google Cloud Logging and immutable audit bucket. CIM import idempotency: ON CONFLICT upsert with batch_id; DELETE FROM topology_nodes WHERE import_batch_id = :id on import_batch_id not found; batch_id assigned to each import batch. Topology rule validation: TopologyRuleEngine.validate_sequence(order_id) returns list of topology_rule_violations before order approval; violations displayed in step execution form. VPN-only: Cloud Load Balancing enforces GKE NetworkPolicy denying ingress from non-VPN CIDR ranges.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot cluster (us-central1) with Istio service mesh. Three deployments: topology-api (2-6 pods, HPA on WebSocket connection count), switching-order-api (2-4 pods), integration-api (1-2 pods). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 HA with automated failover. Memorystore Redis 7 Memorystore for pub/sub and step locks. Cloud Load Balancing with Istio sidecar injection for mTLS STRICT. GitLab CI: ruff lint, mypy strict, pytest (90% coverage gate), Vitest (85% gate), docker buildx, Artifact Registry push, Helm chart update in GitOps repo, ArgoCD sync. Staging auto-deploy on merge to main; production requires control room supervisor approval in GitLab. Environments: dev (single-AZ), staging (multi-AZ), production (multi-AZ, Istio mesh, Cloud SQL HA). Rollback: ArgoCD automated rollback on failed readiness probe.
parsed.observability: Prometheus: switching_orders_created_total (counter), step_locks_acquired_total (gauge), step_lock_timeout_total (gauge), topology_rule_violations_blocked_total (gauge), websocket_connections_active (gauge). Grafana dashboard: switching order pipeline state, active locks, topology change rate, lock timeout rate. Google Cloud Logging: structured JSON audit trail for all write operations; 20-year retention in Cloud Logging bucket. PagerDuty: step lock auto-release alert (10-minute TTL), CIM import validation failure alert, topology rule violation alert, PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion alert.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot: ~$1,200/month for three pods at steady state. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL HA: ~$300/month. Memorystore Redis 7: ~$150/month. Cloud Load Balancing: ~$50/month. Cloud CDN: ~$50/month. Total ~$2,100/month at steady state. Scaling: GKE Autopilot scales to zero pods; Cloud SQL HA handles burst; Memorystore Redis scales horizontally via Memorystore cluster mode.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Topology viewer with SVG force-layout, switching order creation with dispatcher approval, step execution with photo evidence upload, StepLockManager with 10-minute Redis TTL and PagerDuty alert, CIM import with ON CONFLICT idempotency, audit log, Keycloak OIDC with LDAP federation, GitLab CI pipeline, ArgoCD sync. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Topology rule engine with connectivity graph validation, CIM import batch management UI, crew assignment management, TopologyRuleEngine.validate_sequence() validation before approval, Grafana topology change rate dashboard, PagerDuty alert escalation. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Mobile step execution form for field crew, predictive outage scheduling based on network_segments.connectivity_graph, SCADA direct integration via SCADA control system API, GIS import from GIS data warehouse.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 strict type annotations enforced by mypy. ruff for linting. pytest with 90% coverage gate and Vitest 85% gate in CI. Vue 3 TypeScript strict mode. CIM import must validate cim_mrid against existing topology_nodes before upsert. TopologyRuleEngine.validate_sequence() must return list of violations before order approval. StepLockManager must acquire lock before any step execution. CIM import batch_id must be assigned to each batch for idempotency. All PostgreSQL queries must use parameterized SQLAlchemy; no f-string interpolation in ORM queries.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CIM import re-import risk if CIM schema changes in GIS/SCADA. Mitigation: ON CONFLICT upsert with batch_id and DELETE on batch_id not found; CIM parser returns validation_report with CIM element IDs causing parse errors. Risk: StepLockManager auto-release on lock timeout causes PagerDuty alert. Mitigation: 10-minute TTL with PagerDuty alert on auto-release; lock timeout value configurable in StepLockManager constructor. Tradeoff: Redis pub/sub for topology WebSocket broadcast over direct WebSocket for simpler Redis-only deployment. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL 16 over InfluxDB for audit log storage given 20-year retention requirement.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Redis pub/sub over direct WebSocket for topology broadcast to avoid WebSocket connection overhead. Confirmed ON CONFLICT upsert with batch_id for CIM idempotency. Confirmed 10-minute StepLockManager TTL with PagerDuty alert on auto-release. Confirmed PostgreSQL 16 over InfluxDB for audit log storage given 20-year retention requirement.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision targeting ISS-002 (step lock timeout) and ISS-003 (CIM import idempotency). Adding explicit 10-minute Redis TTL with SET NX EX 600 for step_lock atomic acquisition and PagerDuty alert on auto-release. Adding ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE to CIM import pipeline with import_batch_id for rollback. ISS-001 (Istio mTLS) was resolved in round 2 and carried forward. All three issues now resolved - plan should converge in this round.

fix_report.action_taken: StepLockManager now uses Redis SET NX EX 600 (10-minute TTL) for atomic lock acquisition. Added lock_timeout Prometheus counter. PagerDuty alert fires when step_lock_timeout_total increments, notifying dispatcher that a field crew step lock expired and the step is unlocked. switching_steps.status transitions back to pending on auto-release.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Step locks automatically release after 10 minutes with dispatcher notification, preventing permanently locked isolation points from blocking switching order completion

fix_report.action_taken: CIM XML import pipeline now uses PostgreSQL ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE SET ... for topology_nodes and isolation_points, making re-imports safe and idempotent. Each import assigned a UUID import_batch_id. Added DELETE /topology/cim-import/:batch_id rollback endpoint that removes all topology_nodes and isolation_points with matching import_batch_id using a database transaction. Added import idempotency_key validation to reject duplicate concurrent imports.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Corrected CIM XML files can be safely re-imported without duplicating topology nodes; bad imports can be atomically rolled back by batch_id

reference.title: Distribution Grid Switching Order Management Platform v3
reference.executive_summary: Vue 3 + FastAPI web application for control room dispatchers and field crews to manage switching orders on medium-voltage distribution networks. Deployed on GKE Autopilot with Istio mTLS service mesh and Keycloak OIDC authentication. Features real-time SVG single-line diagram with WebSocket topology updates, switching order approval workflow with step-level field crew execution tracking, and TopologyRuleEngine pre-validation. Round 3 resolves all three tracked issues: Istio mTLS (ISS-001 round 2), step lock 10-minute timeout (ISS-002), and CIM import idempotency (ISS-003).
reference.architecture_overview: Three FastAPI services on GKE Autopilot with Istio sidecar mTLS STRICT: topology-api (CIM import, device state, WebSocket broadcast to Vue clients via Redis pub/sub), switching-order-api (order CRUD, SwitchingOrderStateMachine, StepLockManager with 10-minute Redis TTL, TopologyRuleEngine), and integration-api (GIS/SCADA ingest). Vue 3 SPA connects to topology-api WebSocket for live single-line diagram updates, patching Pinia topology store on device_state_changed events. Keycloak OIDC with 15-minute JWT and silent refresh governs all route access. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL HA persists switching orders, topology, and audit trail. Redis Memorystore backs step locks (SET NX EX 600) and topology pub/sub channel.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Vue Router 4, Vue Query, keycloak-js, D3.js v7 (SVG single-line diagram rendering), Vitest. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, alembic, pandapower (CIM XML parsing), redis-py, websockets. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot, Istio 1.21 (mTLS STRICT), Keycloak 24, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16, Memorystore Redis 6 GB, Cloud Load Balancing with IAP. CI/CD: GitLab CI, ArgoCD, Helm, Artifact Registry. Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, Google Cloud Logging, PagerDuty.
reference.functional_feature_map: Single-Line Diagram Viewer: SVG topology rendering with isolation_points color-coded by device state (closed/open/unknown) and network_segments highlighted during active switching orders. Switching Order Lifecycle: dispatcher creates order with step sequence, TopologyRuleEngine validates sequence against connectivity graph and returns topology_rule_violations list, dispatcher approves, field crews execute step-by-step with StepLockManager preventing concurrent operations on the same isolation_point. Step Execution: field crew acquires lock via PATCH /steps/:id/execute, completes action, uploads optional photo evidence, marks complete. CIM Network Model Import: POST multipart CIM XML triggers pandapower parse, idempotent upsert on cim_mrid, import_batch_id assigned for rollback capability. Audit Trail: all topology-modifying actions written to audit_logs with keycloak_sub, display_name, changed_fields JSONB.
reference.system_components: topology-api pod (FastAPI, pandapower CIM parser, WebSocket manager broadcasting to Redis topology_updates channel, 2-6 pods HPA on WebSocket connection count), switching-order-api pod (FastAPI, SwitchingOrderStateMachine, StepLockManager with Redis SET NX EX 600, TopologyRuleEngine with materialized view query, 2-4 pods), integration-api pod (FastAPI, SCADA/GIS ingest handlers, 1-2 pods), Istio sidecar proxies (mTLS STRICT between all pods), PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL HA (switching_orders, switching_steps, isolation_points, network_segments, topology_nodes, outage_tickets, crew_assignments, audit_logs), Redis Memorystore 6 GB (step_lock entries with 600s TTL, topology_updates pub/sub), Keycloak 24 (LDAP-federated, GKE Deployment), Cloud Load Balancing with IAP (VPN enforcement).
reference.workflows: Switching Order Creation and Approval: dispatcher creates switching order with title, region_code, planned window, and ordered list of steps (each specifying isolation_point_id and action open/close/tag). switching-order-api calls TopologyRuleEngine which queries topology_rule_violations materialized view to verify the step sequence does not create an unintended island or violate N-1 connectivity. If violations exist, order returns to draft with violation list. Dispatcher resolves and resubmits. On clean validation, order status transitions to pending_approval; second dispatcher approves (PATCH .../approve), status transitions to approved. Step Execution with Lock Management: field crew opens Step Execution Form for their assigned step. Frontend POSTs PATCH /switching-orders/:id/steps/:step_id/execute. StepLockManager calls Redis SET NX EX 600 keyed by isolation_point_id. If lock acquired, step.status transitions to locked; crew performs physical switching action, confirms in UI, optionally uploads photo. PATCH .../complete transitions step.status to completed, Redis lock deleted. If crew does not complete within 10 minutes, Redis TTL expires, step.status reverts to pending, Prometheus step_lock_timeout_total increments, PagerDuty alert fires to dispatcher. CIM XML Import: operator uploads CIM XML via POST /topology/cim-import. pandapower parses CIM elements. PostgreSQL transaction executes INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE for topology_nodes and isolation_points. import_batch_id UUID assigned to all inserted/updated records. On parse failure, transaction rolls back atomically. DELETE /topology/cim-import/:batch_id removes all records with matching import_batch_id.
