parsed.title: Open-Source SAML 2.0 and OIDC Library Documentation Site
parsed.executive_summary: A static documentation site for an open-source SAML 2.0 and OIDC library, built with Astro 4.x and MDX for content pages and TypeDoc for API reference. Deployed to Vercel Edge Network with automatic preview deployments on GitHub PRs. Features an interactive provider comparison table, client-side search via Pagefind, and Vercel Analytics for content prioritisation. No backend runtime, no database, no authentication.
parsed.architecture_overview: Astro 4.x project with MDX content collections for guides, API reference, integrations, and changelog. TypeDoc generates MDX API documentation from class definitions, written into the content directory before Astro build. Pagefind static search index is generated at build time and shipped as static assets. The interactive provider comparison table is an Astro island component that ships zero-JS to the browser. Vercel Edge Network serves all static assets globally with automatic HTTPS. GitHub Actions CI runs markdown-link-check and cspell on PRs before any build. Vercel integration handles production and preview deployments automatically.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4.x, MDX, Tailwind CSS, Astro content collections, Astro islands. Content: MDX files with frontmatter. API reference: TypeDoc MDX, Astro content collection. Search: Pagefind static search. Analytics: Vercel Analytics cookieless. Hosting: Vercel Pro with automatic preview deployments. CI: GitHub Actions with cspell and markdown-link-check. Build: Astro build worker on Vercel.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Homepage: library pitch, quick-start snippet, links to SAML and OIDC guides. SAML 2.0 Guides: /guides/saml-2.0-overview, /guides/saml-2.0-federation-patterns, /guides/saml-2.0-attribute-mapping. OIDC Guides: /guides/oidc-flows, /guides/oidc-federation-patterns. API Reference: /api-reference/[class] pages auto-generated from TypeDoc MDX. Integrations: /integrations/okta-saml, /integrations/auth0-oidc, /integrations/azure-ad-saml, /integrations/google-oidc. Provider Comparison: /comparison — interactive Astro island with filter by protocol and feature. Changelog: /changelog from MDX files tagged by version. Search: Pagefind client-side search on any page.
parsed.system_components: Astro 4.x project (content collections, islands, content directory). TypeDoc (MDX API reference generator). Pagefind (static search index generator). Vercel Edge Network (global CDN, automatic HTTPS). Vercel Pro (hosting, preview deployments, analytics). GitHub Actions (CI with cspell and markdown-link-check). Vercel integration (deployment webhook). Vercel Analytics (cookieless page tracking).
parsed.workflows: Content Update Workflow: Maintainer opens PR with updated MDX file. GitHub Actions runs cspell against custom SAML/OIDC/federation dictionary and markdown-link-check against all .mdx files. If both pass, TypeDoc runs to generate API reference MDX. Vercel integration triggers Astro build on Vercel build worker. Preview URL generated and shared with maintainers for review. Production deployment on merge to main. Search Workflow: Engineer visits /search?q=saml+2.0+attribute+mapping. Pagefind reads all MDX pages matching the query, renders a search result list, and loads the top-matching page. No backend API call required.
parsed.data_model: Content entities: doc_pages (MDX files in src/content/docs/ with frontmatter: title, slug, description, tags). API reference entities: api_reference_entries (TypeDoc MDX files in src/content/docs/api-reference/ with class/method/property names). Provider comparison entities: provider_comparisons (structured frontmatter: protocol, provider_name, supported_features, supported_protocols, supported_versions, notes). Changelog entities: changelog_entries (MDX files in src/content/docs/changelog/ with version, release_notes, breaking_changes). No database, no schema.
parsed.api_design: No server-side API. Client-side only: Pagefind search API (fetch /search?q= from Astro client). Vercel Analytics API (fetch /api/analytics/pageviews from Vercel Edge). No HTTP endpoints in the site code.
parsed.security_and_compliance: CSP headers via vercel.json: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://cdn.jsdelivr.net; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https:; connect-src 'self' https://www.google-analytics.com. CSP blocks inline script execution. No cookies, no user input forms, no API endpoints. Vercel environment variables for analytics token. GitHub Actions encrypted secrets for build pipeline secrets. No GDPR retention obligations since no personal data collected.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub repository (main branch) → Vercel integration webhook → Astro build on Vercel build worker → static assets deployed to Vercel Edge Network globally. PR branches → preview deployment at unique Vercel URL (e.g., https://library-docs.vercel.app/comparison). Vercel Pro plan covers unlimited preview deployments. GitHub Actions CI: cspell check, markdown-link-check, TypeDoc dry-run, Vercel integration build trigger. Vercel integration handles production and preview deployments automatically. No manual server configuration required.
parsed.observability: Vercel Analytics dashboard: page views by URL, top referrers, geography breakdown, device type distribution. No custom metrics required for static delivery. Vercel Analytics cookieless: no PII, no GDPR consent required. GitHub Actions build logs show Astro build success or error. Vercel deployment logs show build worker status.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro ~$20/month covers hosting, preview deployments, and analytics. TypeDoc runs in GitHub Actions free tier. Pagefind static search index is under 2MB at typical documentation site scale. No scaling concerns for static content delivery.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (2 weeks): Core architecture, Astro project scaffolding, MDX content for SAML 2.0 overview, OIDC flows, SAML 2.0 attribute mapping guide, TypeDoc API reference for v1.0, Vercel deployment with preview URLs, GitHub Actions CI with cspell and markdown-link-check. Phase 2 Content Expansion (4 weeks): SAML 2.0 federation patterns, OIDC federation patterns, provider comparison table as Astro island, provider comparison table for Okta, Auth0, Azure AD, Google OIDC, Vercel Analytics dashboard setup, search functionality. Phase 3 Community Integration (2 weeks): Community forum link integration, interactive SAML 2.0 flow diagram, versioned changelog per library release.
parsed.development_guidelines: Astro content collections for all MDX files — no hardcoded paths in HTML. TypeDoc MDX files must be in src/content/docs/api-reference/ with class/method/property names. All Astro islands must be pure HTML — no client-side JS unless explicitly required. cspell custom dictionary required for all PRs. markdown-link-check must pass on every PR before any build. Vercel Analytics must be enabled on production branch.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TypeDoc API reference generation produces incorrect or incomplete documentation if class/method names change between library versions. Mitigation: TypeDoc dry-run step in CI verifies API doc build before any production deployment. Risk: Pagefind search index size grows beyond 2MB at large documentation site scale. Mitigation: Pagefind index is lazy-loaded on search interaction and can be manually updated by maintainers. Tradeoff: Astro islands for comparison table over a dedicated search component — islands keep the comparison table zero-JS and maintainable.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Selected Astro 4.x over Next.js for zero-JS default and better content collection API. Chose Pagefind over Algolia for zero-backend search and sub-2MB index size. Confirmed Vercel Analytics over Google Analytics for cookieless tracking. Decided Astro islands over a dedicated search component for zero-JS delivery.

reference.thinking_summary: Static documentation site for a SAML/OIDC library — key decisions are Astro content collections for MDX organisation, Pagefind for client-side search (satisfies no-backend constraint), TypeDoc integration for API reference generation, and Vercel Edge for zero-config hosting with PR preview deployments. No server runtime, no database, no authentication. CSP headers via vercel.json complete the minimal security surface.
reference.title: SAML 2.0 / OIDC Federation Library Documentation Site
reference.executive_summary: A fully static Astro 4.x documentation site for an open-source identity federation library implementing SAML 2.0 and OIDC. Hosted on Vercel Edge Network with automatic preview deployments on contributor PRs. Content is authored in MDX and organised into Astro content collections covering architecture guides, TypeDoc-generated API reference, per-provider integration guides, and a provider comparison table. Client-side Pagefind search requires no backend. Vercel Analytics provides cookieless page popularity tracking to guide documentation priorities.
reference.architecture_overview: Pure static architecture: Astro 4.x builds MDX content collections into plain HTML with minimal JavaScript islands at build time. TypeDoc runs as a pre-build step in GitHub Actions generating API reference MDX from library source JSDoc annotations before Astro processes them. Built static assets deploy to Vercel Edge Network globally; no origin server exists. Pagefind index is built during the same Astro build step and shipped as static JSON fragments loaded lazily when a visitor activates search. CSP headers delivered on every edge response via vercel.json. No cookies, no session state, no server-side runtime.
reference.technology_stack: Astro 4.x as static site generator with MDX integration and Tailwind CSS typography plugin. Pagefind for client-side search index (generated at build, loaded lazily). TypeDoc for API reference MDX generation from library JSDoc. Vercel Edge Network for static hosting, automatic HTTPS, preview deployments. Vercel Analytics (cookieless). GitHub Actions for PR link-checking (markdown-link-check) and spellcheck (cspell with SAML/OIDC custom dictionary).
reference.functional_feature_map: Architecture Guides: /guides/saml-2.0-overview (SP-initiated and IdP-initiated flows), /guides/oidc-flows (authorisation code with PKCE, device flow), /guides/federation-patterns (hub-and-spoke vs mesh federation). API Reference: /api-reference/[class] pages TypeDoc-generated covering SamlClient, OidcProvider, FederationManager, and TokenValidator classes. Integration Guides: /integrations/okta-saml, /integrations/auth0-oidc, /integrations/azure-ad-saml, /integrations/google-workspace-saml. Provider Comparison: /comparison — Astro island table filtered by protocol and features (MFA support, SLO, attribute mapping, PKCE). Changelog: /changelog per-version MDX entries tagged by semver.
reference.system_components: Astro 4.x build process (runs on Vercel build worker): content collection loader reads MDX from src/content/{guides,api-reference,integrations,changelog}; TypeDoc pre-step generates api-reference MDX; Pagefind post-build step indexes all HTML output. Vercel Edge Network: delivers static HTML/CSS/JS from 30+ regions; handles TLS termination and HTTP/3. Vercel Analytics edge script: cookieless page view capture. GitHub Actions CI: markdown-link-check and cspell lint on PR. No origin server, no database, no cache layer.
reference.workflows: Documentation Contribution: contributor opens PR with edited MDX files. GitHub Actions runs markdown-link-check on all modified .mdx files (dead links block merge) and cspell against custom dictionary file containing SAML, OIDC, PKCE, SP-initiated, and federation terms. Vercel PR integration creates preview deployment at unique URL. Maintainer reviews rendered output at preview URL and approves PR. On merge to main, Vercel triggers production build: TypeDoc generates API reference MDX, Astro builds all content collections, Pagefind builds search index, static assets deploy to edge in under 2 minutes. Visitor Search: visitor activates search box; Pagefind JS lazily loads the index (under 2MB for current content); search runs entirely in-browser against the index; results rendered with page title, section heading, and content excerpt. No network request to any backend.
reference.data_model: Content collections (MDX files in Git, not database tables): doc_pages (src/content/guides/*.mdx — frontmatter: title, description, protocol ENUM saml_2_0/oidc/both, order INT, last_updated DATE). api_references (src/content/api-reference/*.mdx — TypeDoc-generated: class_name, module, since_version). integration_guides (src/content/integrations/*.mdx — frontmatter: provider_name, protocol ENUM saml_2_0/oidc, tested_library_version). changelog_entries (src/content/changelog/*.mdx — frontmatter: version SEMVER, release_date, breaking_changes BOOL). provider_comparisons (src/content/comparison/providers.json — array of {provider,protocol,mfa_support,slo_support,attribute_mapping,pkce_support,library_example_link}).
reference.api_design: No backend API. Static content delivery only. Client-side Pagefind search: Pagefind.search(query) returns {results:[{url,meta:{title,section},excerpt,sub_results}]} — runs in browser with no network calls beyond initial index load. Vercel Analytics Web API (browser-side, automatic): cookieless, no PII. GitHub Actions outputs: markdown-link-check exits non-zero on dead links (blocks PR merge); cspell exits non-zero on unknown terms (blocks PR merge).
reference.security_and_compliance: Vercel-managed TLS with HSTS on all responses. CSP configured in vercel.json: Content-Security-Policy 'default-src self; script-src self; style-src self unsafe-inline; img-src self data:; frame-ancestors none; form-action none'. No cookies set anywhere. X-Frame-Options DENY and X-Content-Type-Options nosniff headers in vercel.json. Dependabot configured for Astro, Tailwind, and TypeDoc package updates; critical CVEs auto-create PRs. No secrets in repository; Vercel environment variables hold only public analytics token.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Vercel integration: push to main triggers production build (Astro build + TypeDoc pre-step + Pagefind index). Build time under 3 minutes. PR branches trigger preview deployments automatically. Zero downtime deploys via atomic Vercel edge asset swap. GitHub Actions on PR: markdown-link-check (npm run check:links), cspell (npx cspell '**/*.mdx' --config .cspell.json), TypeDoc dry-run to confirm API docs build without errors. Rollback via Vercel dashboard instant revert to previous deployment. No infrastructure to manage.
reference.observability: Vercel Analytics dashboard: daily and weekly page views by URL, top referral sources (GitHub, Hacker News, search engines), geographic distribution, device type breakdown. Used monthly by documentation maintainers to identify which integration guides drive most traffic and which API reference pages have high bounce rates. Vercel provides edge network uptime SLA. GitHub Actions run history provides build success/failure visibility per PR.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro: $20/month flat. GitHub Actions: free tier sufficient (PR checks under 2 minutes per run). Total ~$20/month. Scales to unlimited traffic via Vercel Edge CDN — no origin server to scale. Bandwidth costs included in Vercel Pro up to 1TB/month.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (3 weeks): Astro project scaffolding with content collections, Tailwind typography setup, homepage, SAML 2.0 and OIDC architecture guides, API reference for v1.x (SamlClient, OidcProvider, FederationManager, TokenValidator), Okta and Auth0 integration guides, provider comparison table island, Pagefind search, Vercel deployment, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Azure AD and Google Workspace integration guides, changelog section, versioned documentation navigation, interactive SAML/OIDC flow diagrams as Astro island components. Phase 3 (ongoing): Community contributions, additional provider guides, library v2.x documentation.
reference.development_guidelines: MDX frontmatter schema enforced via Astro content collection Zod schemas — build fails on schema violation. All protocol-specific terms added to .cspell.json custom dictionary before use. TypeDoc must build without warnings; --strict flag in CI dry-run. No inline JavaScript in MDX files — interactive elements must be Astro island components. Comparison table data maintained in providers.json structured data not prose — enables programmatic validation.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TypeDoc API reference generation breaks when library adds new public API without JSDoc. Mitigation: TypeDoc --strict flag in CI blocks merge on missing JSDoc. Risk: Pagefind search index grows large with content volume — performance degrades above 5000 pages. Mitigation: monitor index size in CI; implement content chunking if index exceeds 5MB. Tradeoff: Astro over Docusaurus — chosen for zero-JS default; Docusaurus would add React runtime overhead for a docs-only site. Tradeoff: Pagefind over Algolia — satisfies no-backend-search constraint with acceptable quality for technical documentation.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Pagefind over Algolia DocSearch — no external service dependency and satisfies constraint. TypeDoc integration confirmed generating MDX output format natively in TypeDoc 0.26. Versioned documentation deferred to Phase 2 — Phase 1 covers v1.x only. Community forum integration deferred to Phase 3.

{
 "thinking_summary": "This is a university workload governance platform — the microservices decomposition is the key architectural decision. Each service should have its own PostgreSQL schema to avoid cross-service coupling. The scheduling service depends on SIS enrollment projections, so a daily batch job is safer than event-driven. SOC 2 Type II requires append-only audit logging and Azure Key Vault secrets. The Workday HR sync must handle rate limits with exponential backoff.",
 "fix_report": [],
 "title": "University Faculty Workload Governance Platform Architecture",
 "executive_summary": "A Java 21 Spring Boot microservices architecture on Azure Kubernetes Service that automates faculty workload contracting, course section assignments, and tenure milestone tracking across multiple

parsed.thinking_summary: This is a university workload governance platform — the microservices decomposition is the key architectural decision. Each service should have its own PostgreSQL schema to avoid cross-service coupling. The scheduling service depends on SIS enrollment projections, so a daily batch job is safer than event-driven. SOC 2 Type II requires append-only audit logging and Azure Key Vault secrets. The Workday HR sync must handle rate limits with exponential backoff.
parsed.title: University Faculty Workload Governance Platform Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: A Java 21 Spring Boot microservices architecture on Azure Kubernetes Service that automates faculty workload contracting, course section assignments, and tenure milestone tracking across multiple university campuses. Vue 3 frontend with Pinia state management provides a role-scoped governance dashboard for faculty, department chairs, deans, and provosts. Each microservice owns its PostgreSQL schema and has its own append-only audit_log table for SOC 2 compliance. A daily batch job ingests SIS enrollment projections and generates workload contracts, while a nightly HRSyncService reconciles Workday faculty records.
parsed.architecture_overview: Four Spring Boot microservices deployed on AKS: ContractsService (faculty workload contract authoring), SchedulingService (daily batch contract generation from SIS enrollment projections), HRSyncService (Workday HR record reconciliation), and ReportingService (department allocation and tenure milestone analytics). Each service has its own PostgreSQL 16 schema and REST API. Azure API Management enforces Azure AD OIDC JWT validation at the gateway. Azure Key Vault stores all secrets; no credentials in AKS pod environment variables. Vue 3 SPA served via Azure CDN communicates with the microservices via Feign client with circuit breaker. Azure Container Registry holds Docker images. Azure DevOps Pipelines manages CI/CD with Helm chart deployment to AKS.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, PrimeVue, Vue Query, optimistic updates. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3, Spring Security OIDC, Feign, Spring Data JPA, Flyway. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with separate schemas per microservice. Cache: Redis 7 for distributed locking. Auth: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC with Spring Security OIDC. Secrets: Azure Key Vault. Hosting: AKS with 3-node general-purpose and 1-node high-memory node pool. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Observability: Azure Monitor, Grafana, PagerDuty. Container Registry: Azure Container Registry.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Faculty Contract Authoring: department chair submits workload contract draft; system transitions to department_approved state; dean reviews and approves; provost approves and executes contract. Course Section Assignment: department chair assigns course sections to faculty members; system creates workload contracts for each section. Tenure Milestone Tracking: system records tenure milestones (e.g., probation, tenure-track, tenure-eligible) with due dates. HR Sync: nightly HRSyncService reconciles Workday faculty records with department records. Department Allocation Dashboard: department chair views workload distribution across faculty members. Batch Report Generation: ReportingService generates tenure-eligible faculty lists and workload distribution reports.
parsed.system_components: Azure API Management (JWT validation, rate limiting), AKS (ContractsService, SchedulingService, HRSyncService, ReportingService), Azure Container Registry (Docker images), Azure DevOps Pipelines (CI/CD), Azure Key Vault (secrets), Azure Blob Storage (record archival), Azure Monitor (cluster metrics), Grafana (contract throughput and sync lag dashboards), PagerDuty (batch job failure alerts), Azure Container Registry (Docker images).
parsed.workflows: Workload Contract Authoring: Department chair opens Contract Authoring Wizard, selects workload type (teaching, research, service), enters workload hours and course sections, submits draft; system transitions to department_approved state; chair receives email notification; dean reviews and approves; provost approves and executes contract; contract record created in PostgreSQL. Course Section Assignment: Department chair selects course section from grid, clicks Assign; system creates workload contract for each section, updates faculty_members.workload_contracts table, notifies faculty member via email. Tenure Milestone Tracking: Tenure Officer records probation, tenure-track, tenure-eligible milestones with due dates; system creates audit_log entry; milestone due date triggers email reminder to faculty member. HR Sync: HRSyncService polls Workday REST API every 15 minutes; on each record, checks Workday rate limit (100 req/min), inserts hr_sync_records record, updates Workday record if different.
parsed.data_model: faculty_members (id UUID PK, name, title, department_id, campus_id, work_email, work_phone). workload_contracts (id UUID PK, faculty_member_id FK, workload_type ENUM teaching/research/service, total_hours NUMERIC, course_section_ids UUID[], created_at, updated_at). course_sections (id UUID PK, course_id FK, section_number, course_title, credit_hours NUMERIC, faculty_member_id FK). tenure_milestones (id UUID PK, faculty_member_id FK, milestone_type ENUM probation/tenure_track/tenure_eligible, due_date DATE, status ENUM pending/due/completed, notes TEXT). department_allocations (id UUID PK, department_id FK, faculty_member_id FK, section_id FK, workload_hours NUMERIC, assigned_at). hr_sync_records (id UUID PK, faculty_member_id FK, workday_record_id, status ENUM pending/synced/failed, error_message TEXT). audit_logs (id UUID PK, microservice_name, action, resource_id, correlation_id, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ).
parsed.api_design: REST at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.0. ContractsService: GET /contracts?faculty_id=&status= (list contracts), POST /contracts (create draft), PATCH /contracts/:id (update), DELETE /contracts/:id (delete). SchedulingService: GET /contracts?status=draft (pending contracts), POST /contracts/:id/generate (generate draft workload contract), GET /enrollments?year= (SIS enrollment projections). HRSyncService: GET /hr/sync?faculty_id= (sync status), POST /hr/sync/:id (trigger sync). ReportingService: GET /reports/tenure-eligible?year= (list tenure-eligible faculty), GET /reports/allocation?year= (department allocation summary). All endpoints require Azure AD JWT; RBAC enforced at API gateway.
parsed.security_and_compliance: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC with Spring Security OIDC integration; JWT validated at Azure API Management; RBAC enforced at API gateway and repeated at service layer via Spring Security annotations. TLS 1.3 in transit; AES-256 at rest in Azure Key Vault. Append-only audit_log table per microservice; correlation_id propagated via HTTP header for cross-service trace reconstruction. Azure Key Vault for all secrets; no credentials in AKS pod environment variables. SOC 2 Type II controls: append-only audit_log, Azure Key Vault secrets, RBAC, Workday rate limit handling. FERPA: cross-reference to student course enrollment prohibited; audit log reviewed for any cross-reference. Azure Blob archival for records exceeding 7-year active retention.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: AKS with 3-node general-purpose node pool (2 vCPU, 4GB) and 1-node high-memory node pool (8 vCPU, 32GB) for ReportingService batch jobs. Azure Container Registry for Docker images. Azure DevOps Pipelines: Maven build, JUnit tests, SonarQube quality gate (80% line coverage), Docker build, ACR push, Helm chart deploy to AKS with 3-min rolling update. Environments: dev (single-AZ, shared AKS node pool), staging (multi-AZ, shared node pool), production (multi-AZ, dedicated node pool). Flyway migrations run as Kubernetes Job pre-deploy hook. Rollback: Helm rollback on failed health checks; database migration versioned with Flyway.
parsed.observability: Azure Monitor cluster metrics: pod CPU/memory utilization, container count, disk usage. Grafana dashboards: contract throughput (contracts/day), Workday sync lag (records/min), HRSync error rate. PagerDuty alerts: batch job failure (ContractsService, SchedulingService, HRSyncService), API gateway error rate above 1%, AKS node pool CPU above 80%. Structured application logging to Azure Application Insights with correlation_id header for cross-service trace reconstruction.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: AKS node pool: $1200/month for 3-node general-purpose and 1-node high-memory. Azure API Management: $200/month. Azure Container Registry: $50/month. Azure DevOps Pipelines: $150/month. Azure Blob archival: $50/month. Total: $2000/month within $8000 budget. Scaling: AKS auto-scales node pool on CPU 60% target; ReportingService scales to 2 nodes during annual tenure review season.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Pilot (12 months): ContractsService, SchedulingService, HRSyncService, Azure AD OIDC auth, RBAC, audit_log, Azure DevOps CI/CD, dev and staging environments. Phase 2 Full Rollout (12 months): ReportingService, department allocation dashboard, tenure milestone tracking, department chair approval workflow, multi-campus deployment. Phase 3 (18 months): Faculty performance review integration, cross-campus reporting analytics, mobile self-service app for faculty workload management.
parsed.development_guidelines: Java 21 with Spotless for linting and SonarQube for quality gate. Spring Security OIDC integration for JWT validation. Flyway migrations for schema changes. Unit tests for all microservices with 80% line coverage. Integration tests via Testcontainers for PostgreSQL and Redis. ADR required for any change to microservice ownership or database schema. ADRs stored in Azure DevOps Wiki.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SIS enrollment projections are inaccurate, causing contract over-estimation. Mitigation: contract generation uses enrollment projections as input, but contract values are capped at 1200 hours per year. Risk: Workday rate limit prevents nightly HRSyncService from syncing all records. Mitigation: exponential backoff with 10-second delay; failed records logged to hr_sync_records table. Tradeoff: daily batch job over event-driven for scheduling — daily batch is safer for a university system where SIS data is typically batch-updated overnight. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL per microservice over a shared schema — reduces cross-service coupling but requires Flyway migrations for schema changes.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Workday sync frequency: daily batch job confirmed. Contract generation frequency: daily batch job confirmed. Campus data isolation: each microservice owns its PostgreSQL schema. RBAC enforcement: Azure API Management and Spring Security repeated at service layer.

reference.thinking_summary: This is a flawed first-pass row — the plan intentionally contains a contract violation: the backend_stack field in the frozen_requirement_contract correctly specifies Java 21 with Spring Boot, but the technology_stack section of the plan mentions Node.js as the backend runtime, creating an internal inconsistency. Additionally, the compliance_context field is present but the security_and_compliance section omits SOC 2 audit controls, which are required given the confidential data sensitivity and high risk level.
reference.title: Faculty Workload and Tenure Governance Platform — Initial Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A fullstack academic governance platform for a multi-campus state university system managing faculty workload contracts, course section assignments, and tenure milestone tracking. Built with Vue 3 on a microservices backend using Java 21 Spring Boot services on Azure Kubernetes Service, with PostgreSQL 16 per-service schema isolation, Azure AD SSO, and full audit logging. Integrates with the university Workday HR system via nightly batch sync. NOTE: this plan contains intentional flaws for training purposes — technology_stack section incorrectly references Node.js backend.
reference.architecture_overview: Vue 3 SPA authenticates via Azure AD Entra ID OIDC and calls four Spring Boot microservices through Azure API Management: ContractsService, SchedulingService, HRSyncService, and ReportingService. Each microservice owns its PostgreSQL 16 schema. Redis 7 provides distributed locking for concurrent contract edits. Daily batch jobs in SchedulingService ingest SIS enrollment projections to generate draft workload contracts. Tenure milestone tracking and multi-level approval workflows are orchestrated by ContractsService with role-gated endpoints for department_chair, college_dean, and provost reviewers.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, PrimeVue, Vue Router. Backend: Node.js 20 with Express (intentional contract violation — contract specifies Java 21 Spring Boot). Database: PostgreSQL 16 per-microservice schema. Cache: Redis 7. Auth: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC. Infrastructure: AKS, Azure Container Registry, Azure API Management. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Monitoring: Azure Monitor, Grafana.
reference.functional_feature_map: Contract Authoring Wizard: faculty or department chair drafts workload contract with course assignment grid and credit hour calculation. Multi-Level Approval Workflow: state machine routing contracts through department_chair > college_dean > provost reviewers with email notifications at each stage. Tenure Milestone Timeline: faculty view of tenure review schedule with document upload for dossier submission. Department Allocation Dashboard: chair view of total workload hours across all faculty in department. Batch Contract Generation: SchedulingService automatically generates draft contracts from SIS enrollment projections each semester. HR Sync Status: admin panel showing Workday sync last run, records updated, and error counts.
reference.system_components: Azure API Management (JWT validation, RBAC routing), Vue 3 SPA (Azure Blob Static Sites), ContractsService (Spring Boot, PostgreSQL contracts schema), SchedulingService (Spring Boot, PostgreSQL scheduling schema, batch job), HRSyncService (Spring Boot, Workday REST API integration), ReportingService (Spring Boot, ClickHouse read-only reporting replica), Redis 7 (session cache, distributed locking), Azure Key Vault (secrets management), Azure Monitor (cluster and application metrics).
reference.workflows: Semester Contract Generation: SchedulingService batch job runs at 02:00 on the Monday before the semester opens; fetches course_sections with enrollment projections from SIS via REST; generates draft workload_contracts for each faculty_member with assigned sections; notifies department chairs via email. Tenure Review Initiation: the milestones service detects faculty members within 60 days of their tenure_review_date; generates a review record; routes dossier submission task to faculty; upon submission, routes approval workflow to department, college, and provost levels sequentially with configurable deadline escalation.
reference.data_model: faculty_members (id UUID PK, azure_oid, employee_id, name, rank ENUM assistant/associate/full/adjunct, department_id FK, tenure_status, hire_date, campus_id FK). workload_contracts (id UUID PK, faculty_id FK, academic_year, semester, status ENUM draft/submitted/dept_approved/dean_approved/provost_approved/executed, total_credit_hours, created_at, approved_at). course_sections (id UUID PK, contract_id FK, course_code, section_number, credit_hours, enrollment_cap, sis_section_id). tenure_milestones (id UUID PK, faculty_id FK, milestone_type ENUM annual_review/midpoint/tenure_vote/promotion, due_date, status, dossier_s3_key). hr_sync_records (id UUID PK, employee_id, workday_worker_id, sync_status, last_synced_at, error_detail). audit_logs (id UUID PK, service_name, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, correlation_id, timestamp).
reference.api_design: ContractsService: GET /contracts?faculty_id= (role scoped), POST /contracts (draft creation), PATCH /contracts/:id/submit, PATCH /contracts/:id/approve (role: chair/dean/provost), GET /contracts/:id/audit-log. SchedulingService: GET /course-sections?semester=, POST /batch/generate-contracts (admin trigger). HRSyncService: GET /sync/status, POST /sync/trigger (admin). TenureService: GET /milestones/:faculty_id, POST /milestones/:id/submit-dossier. All services: GET /health, X-Correlation-ID header required.
reference.security_and_compliance: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC with MFA enforced. JWT validation at Azure API Management layer with RBAC claim checking. Spring Security @PreAuthorize annotations at method level for defense in depth. TLS 1.3 on all ingress. Azure Key Vault with AKS Workload Identity — pod identity bound to service account for secret access. NOTE: SOC 2 Type II audit controls are intentionally underspecified here — audit log retention policy and access review procedures missing despite compliance_context requiring them.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipelines: Maven build, JUnit unit and integration tests, SonarQube quality gate (80% coverage), Docker build, ACR push, Helm chart deploy to AKS namespace. Environments: dev (shared 2-node cluster), staging (production mirror, single campus dataset), production (3-node general pool + 1-node high-memory for reporting). Flyway database migrations applied pre-deploy via Kubernetes Job. Helm rollback on failed liveness probes. Azure Blob lifecycle policy archives records exceeding retention tiers.
reference.observability: Azure Monitor collects AKS node and pod metrics. Grafana dashboards: contract approval throughput by campus, Workday sync lag per faculty cohort, tenure milestone overdue count. Application logs structured JSON with correlation_id. PagerDuty alerts: batch job failure (SchedulingService), Workday sync failure for >100 records, API error rate >2%, AKS node NotReady.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $6500/month on AKS for 3 campuses at 800 faculty. AKS general-purpose nodes 3x D4s_v3 ~$700/month; PostgreSQL Flexible Server 2x ~$400/month; Azure Cache for Redis ~$150/month; API Management Developer tier ~$50/month. Reserved instance discounts reduce compute ~30%. Scale path: horizontal pod autoscaling on CPU 70% for ContractsService during semester start spikes.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Pilot Campus (12 weeks): ContractsService with approval workflow, Vue 3 frontend for contract authoring, Azure AD SSO, PostgreSQL schema setup, CI/CD pipeline, Workday HR sync (nightly batch). Phase 2 — Scheduling Automation (8 weeks): SchedulingService batch contract generation from SIS, course section assignment grid, campus calendar integration. Phase 3 — Tenure Tracking (8 weeks): tenure milestone timeline, dossier upload, provost approval workflow, department allocation dashboard. Phase 4 — Multi-Campus Rollout (6 weeks): multi-campus namespace isolation, cross-campus reporting in ReportingService, analytics dashboards.
reference.development_guidelines: Java 21 with virtual threads for blocking I/O in sync tasks. Spring Boot 3 with Spring Security for auth. Flyway for database migrations — no manual schema changes. OpenAPI 3.0 specs code-generated from annotations. SonarQube quality gate in CI. RBAC annotations required on all controller methods — no unannotated endpoints. Correlation ID injected into all outbound Feign client calls via RequestInterceptor.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Workday REST API rate limiting during large HR sync batches — mitigation: chunked batches of 100 records with exponential backoff and dead-letter queue for failed records. Risk: multi-campus PostgreSQL schema drift if migrations run out of order — mitigation: Flyway with ordered versioned migrations and pre-deploy validation. Tradeoff: per-microservice PostgreSQL schema over a single shared database for service isolation at the cost of cross-service reporting complexity, addressed by a read-only ClickHouse replica fed by logical replication.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected per-service PostgreSQL schema over separate database instances for cost efficiency while maintaining logical isolation. Chose daily batch Workday sync over event-driven for compatibility with Workday's batch-update cadence. Confirmed AKS Helm deployments over Azure App Service for microservice orchestration flexibility.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for a Next.js 14 static landing page for a pumped hydro investment project. The primary fix is adding server-side reCAPTCHA token verification in the Next.js API route to prevent bot submissions. The Salesforce field mapping is also specified in the architecture document for CRM team configuration. The plan quality is weak per manifest: the data_model section is thin and the deployment_and_operations section is thin as well.

fix_report.action_taken: Added server-side reCAPTCHA v3 token verification in Next.js API route POST /api/inquiry: validates reCAPTCHA token via Google siteverify API before forwarding to Salesforce Lead API; added Vercel Edge middleware rate limiting token bucket to prevent abuse
fix_report.expected_outcome: Bot submissions blocked; reCAPTCHA score below 0.5 silently returns 200 without feedback loop

parsed.title: Pumped Hydro Investment Landing Page Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 static landing page for a 800 MW pumped hydro storage project, deployed on Vercel Edge, with a server-side reCAPTCHA v3 token verification API route that prevents bot form submissions and forwards inquiries to Salesforce CRM. The page features a construction timeline, financial highlights, and an investor inquiry form with a PDF download. Plausible Analytics provides cookieless investor engagement metrics.
parsed.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 static export generates the landing page at build time from YAML config files for milestones, specs, and team profiles. The Next.js API route POST /api/inquiry validates the reCAPTCHA token server-side via Google reCAPTCHA v3 siteverify API before forwarding the form payload to the Salesforce Lead API with field mapping. Vercel Edge middleware enforces a token bucket rate limit of 5 POSTs per IP per 10 minutes. The page is deployed to Vercel with a custom domain and Vercel-managed SSL. Plausible Analytics is a cookieless analytics solution that tracks page views, inquiry form conversions, and PDF download events.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query for async data fetching. API: Next.js API route with Zod InquirySchema for form validation, Vercel Edge middleware for reCAPTCHA token verification and rate limiting. Analytics: Plausible Analytics. CRM: Salesforce Lead API. Hosting: Vercel Edge. CI: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI gate.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Project Overview: 800 MW capacity, storage hours, reservoir volume, head elevation, turbine units, expected annual generation, permitting status, target COD. Construction Timeline: interactive SVG timeline with milestone dates and status. Financial Highlights: capacity factor, levelized cost of energy, IRR, NPV. Investor Contact/NDA Request Form: server-side reCAPTCHA v3 token verification, Salesforce Lead API forwarding with field mapping. PDF Download: project summary document generated via PDFKit from YAML config. Plausible Analytics: page views, inquiry form conversion rate, PDF download events.
parsed.system_components: Next.js 14 static export (page generation), Next.js API route POST /api/inquiry (reCAPTCHA token verification, Zod validation, Salesforce Lead API forwarding), Vercel Edge middleware (reCAPTCHA v3 siteverify API call, token bucket rate limiting), Salesforce Lead API (inquiry lead creation), Plausible Analytics (cookieless analytics), GitHub Actions (CI with Lighthouse CI gate), Vercel (deployment and KV rate limit state).
parsed.workflows: Investor Inquiry Submission: Investor fills out the inquiry form on the page; browser submits POST /api/inquiry; Next.js API route validates reCAPTCHA token via Google siteverify API (returns 200 if valid, 200 silently if invalid); validates form fields against Zod InquirySchema; forwards payload to Salesforce Lead API with field mapping: name to FirstName+LastName, email to Email, organization to Company, source tag to InquirySource; Vercel Edge middleware enforces token bucket rate limit (5 POSTs per IP per 10 minutes); returns 200 success to user; Salesforce creates lead record with custom field tags. PDF Download: Investor clicks Download Project Summary PDF link; Next.js API route calls PDFKit to render project summary from YAML config; returns PDF binary to browser; Plausible Analytics tracks download event.
parsed.data_model: milestones.yaml: [{id, label, date ISO8601, status ENUM completed/in_progress/upcoming, description}] validated by MilestoneSchema Zod. project_specs.yaml: {capacity_mw, storage_hours, reservoir_capacity_mwh, upper_reservoir_volume_af, head_elevation_ft, turbine_units, expected_annual_generation_gwh, permitting_status, target_cod, project_name, location} validated by ProjectSpecsSchema Zod. team_profiles.yaml: [{name, title, bio, photo_filename}]. Salesforce Lead fields: FirstName, LastName, Email, Company, Description, LeadSource, Campaign_Tag__c.
parsed.api_design: POST /api/inquiry {name: string, email: string, organization: string, investment_interest_text: string, recaptcha_token: string, hp_field: string} - validates reCAPTCHA token via Google siteverify API, validates form fields against Zod InquirySchema, forwards to Salesforce Lead API with field mapping, returns 200 success or 200 silently on bot feedback. Rate limiting: Vercel Edge middleware token bucket with 5 tokens per IP per 10 minutes. Response: {success: true, message: 'Inquiry received', lead_id: string}.
parsed.security_and_compliance: reCAPTCHA v3 server-side token verification: Next.js API route calls Google reCAPTCHA v3 siteverify API with the form submission payload; returns 200 if token is valid, 200 silently if token is invalid (prevents bot feedback loop); reCAPTCHA score below 0.5 returns 200 silently to avoid bot feedback. Honeypot field: hp_field must be empty; form submission with non-empty hp_field returns 200 silently. CSP: Content-Security-Policy header set in Vercel environment config; default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://www.google-recaptcha.com; img-src 'self' data: https://*.google-recaptcha.com. HSTS: Strict-Transport-Security header set in Vercel environment config; max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload. No PII stored in app; inquiry name and email forwarded to Salesforce only. GDPR: cookieless analytics; no consent banner required.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: Vercel project linked to GitHub; automatic preview deployments on PR branches; production deployment on main branch push. GitHub Actions CI: npm ci, next build, Lighthouse CI gate >= 95, axe-core WCAG 2.1 AA check on built pages. Vercel deployment triggered automatically on GitHub push via Vercel Git integration. Vercel KV for Edge middleware rate limit state. Custom domain with Vercel-managed SSL. Rollback: Vercel instant rollback to any previous deployment via Vercel dashboard; deployment history retained 30 days. Environments: preview (Vercel Git branch), production (main branch).
parsed.observability: Plausible Analytics: page views, inquiry form conversion rate (form to inquiry submission), PDF download events, inquiry form fields distribution (name, email, organization). Vercel deployment logs: production deployment duration, error rate. No APM required for a static landing page.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Hobby plan: $0/month for static hosting. Vercel Pro plan: $20/month for production deployment and KV rate limit state. Google reCAPTCHA v3: free tier for standard use. Salesforce Lead API: free tier for standard use. Plausible Analytics: free tier for up to 1000 page views/month. Total estimated cost: $0/month.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Next.js 14 static export with Tailwind, YAML config files for milestones and specs, server-side reCAPTCHA v3 token verification in API route, Salesforce Lead API forwarding with field mapping, Plausible Analytics, Vercel deployment, Lighthouse CI gate. Phase 2 (post-Series A): PDF download generator, investor NDA request form with server-side reCAPTCHA, multilingual support for Japanese and Korean institutional investors, Vercel Edge caching for static assets.
parsed.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for Next.js API route. Zod schemas for all config files; no raw YAML in API route. Lighthouse CI gate blocks merge if score drops below 95. axe-core WCAG 2.1 AA check on all built pages. No inline styles in Next.js components; Tailwind only. No third-party scripts in Next.js pages except Plausible Analytics script tag. PR requires 1 approval for production deployment.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Salesforce Lead API outage blocks inquiry submissions. Mitigation: fallback to email forwarding to investor relations team; alert in Plausible Analytics. Risk: reCAPTCHA v3 siteverify API rate limit exceeded during high-volume campaign launch. Mitigation: Vercel Edge middleware rate limiting with 5-token bucket; alert in Vercel deployment logs. Tradeoff: server-side reCAPTCHA token verification adds 50ms latency to form submission; acceptable for a static landing page. Tradeoff: Vercel KV for rate limit state over a database for simplicity at this scale.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Selected Vercel Edge over Netlify for global CDN performance. Confirmed Salesforce Lead API over Pardot for simpler integration. Chose Plausible Analytics over Google Analytics 4 for GDPR compliance. Confirmed YAML config files over JSON for schema validation.

reference.thinking_summary: Resolving ISS-002 by adding server-side reCAPTCHA v3 token verification in the POST /api/inquiry route via Google siteverify API before any Salesforce forwarding. reCAPTCHA v3 script loaded with IntersectionObserver only when inquiry form enters viewport to protect Lighthouse score. Also specifying Salesforce Lead field mapping to unblock the CRM team's configuration. The landing_page class means no persistent backend; the API route is a thin serverless function.

fix_report.action_taken: POST /api/inquiry now calls https://www.google.com/recaptcha/api/siteverify with RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY Vercel environment variable and submitted token; requests with score below 0.5 silently return 200 without Salesforce forward (bot feedback prevention); RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY stored in Vercel encrypted environment variables never exposed to client. reCAPTCHA v3 script loaded with async defer via IntersectionObserver triggered when inquiry section enters viewport, protecting Lighthouse performance score.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Bot form submissions blocked server-side; Lighthouse score maintained >= 95 with deferred reCAPTCHA load; no exploitable bot feedback channel via error responses

reference.title: Pumped Hydro Investment Landing Page - v2 Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 static landing page presenting the 800 MW Mountain Creek Pumped Hydro Storage project to institutional energy infrastructure investors. Features a construction timeline visualization driven by YAML milestone config, technical specifications, team profiles, and an investor inquiry form with server-side reCAPTCHA v3 verification and Salesforce Lead API integration. Deployed to Vercel Edge; Plausible cookieless analytics tracks form conversion and PDF download events.
reference.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 statically generated site deployed to Vercel Edge. All page content generated at build time from Zod-validated YAML config files (milestones.yaml, project_specs.yaml) and static assets. Single serverless API route POST /api/inquiry handles form submissions: validates reCAPTCHA token server-side via Google siteverify API, applies IP-based rate limiting via Vercel Edge middleware token bucket backed by Vercel KV, validates form body with Zod InquirySchema, then maps and forwards validated lead data to Salesforce Lead API. No persistent backend service; no database; no server-side sessions. Vercel handles HTTPS, CDN distribution, and automatic preview deployments on PR branches.
reference.technology_stack: Next.js 14 with TypeScript (static generation via getStaticProps). Tailwind CSS for responsive styling. Framer Motion for timeline animation. Plausible Analytics self-proxied via Next.js rewrites. reCAPTCHA v3 with async defer and IntersectionObserver load. Zod for form and YAML schema validation. js-yaml for YAML parsing at build time. Vercel Edge runtime for API route and middleware. Vercel KV for rate limit token bucket state. GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI and axe-core for CI.
reference.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: project name, 800 MW and 8-hour storage highlights, primary CTA scrolling to inquiry form. Project Specifications: capacity_mw, storage_hours, annual_generation_gwh, reservoir details from project_specs.yaml. Construction Timeline: animated horizontal timeline from milestones.yaml with status indicators and date labels. Team Profiles: static cards from team_profiles.yaml. Investment Thesis: narrative section with downloadable project summary PDF tracked as Plausible goal event PDF_Download. Investor Inquiry Form: name, email, organization, interest description, honeypot field, server-side reCAPTCHA v3, Salesforce Lead API.
reference.system_components: Next.js static pages: index page with all sections as scroll-linked components. Serverless API route pages/api/inquiry.ts: reCAPTCHA siteverify, Zod InquirySchema validation, honeypot check, Salesforce Lead API POST with field mapping. Vercel Edge middleware middleware.ts: rate limit check using Vercel KV token bucket (5 POSTs per IP per 10 minutes). YAML config loader lib/config.ts: reads and validates milestones.yaml and project_specs.yaml against Zod schemas in getStaticProps. Plausible Analytics: self-proxied via Next.js rewrites to /p/js/script.js to prevent ad-blocker interference. reCAPTCHA v3: IntersectionObserver loads script only when inquiry section enters viewport.
reference.workflows: Investor Inquiry Submission: investor fills form and submits; Next.js client validates required fields; reCAPTCHA v3 executes and appends token to POST body; POST /api/inquiry sent; Vercel Edge middleware checks rate limit token bucket in Vercel KV; API route calls Google siteverify with RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY; if score >= 0.5, validates body with Zod InquirySchema, maps to Salesforce Lead fields (FirstName+LastName from name, Email, Company from organization, Description from investment_interest_text, LeadSource='Mountain_Creek_Landing_2026', Campaign_Tag__c='series_a_2026'), POSTs to Salesforce Lead API with Bearer token; returns {success: true} regardless of Salesforce outcome to prevent information leakage. Milestone Update: project team updates milestones.yaml via GitHub PR; Vercel creates preview deployment with updated timeline; PM reviews at preview URL; PR merged to main; production deployment live within 2 minutes.
reference.data_model: milestones.yaml: [{id: str, label: str, date: str ISO8601, status: 'completed'|'in_progress'|'upcoming', description: str}] validated by MilestoneSchema Zod. project_specs.yaml: {capacity_mw: number, storage_hours: number, reservoir_capacity_mwh: number, upper_reservoir_volume_af: number, head_elevation_ft: number, turbine_units: number, expected_annual_generation_gwh: number, permitting_status: str, target_cod: str, project_name: str, location: str} validated by ProjectSpecsSchema Zod. team_profiles.yaml: [{name, title, bio, photo_filename}]. No database. Salesforce Lead fields: FirstName, LastName, Email, Company, Description, LeadSource, Campaign_Tag__c.
reference.api_design: POST /api/inquiry request body: {name: string, email: string, organization: string, investment_interest_text: string, recaptcha_token: string, hp_field: string (honeypot, must be empty)}. Responses: 200 {success: true} always returned regardless of outcome; 429 returned by Vercel Edge middleware for rate limit exceeded before reaching route. Internal logic: honeypot non-empty returns 200 without forward; reCAPTCHA score < 0.5 logs score and returns 200; Salesforce API error logs payload to Vercel log drain and returns 200. Plausible goal events: PDF_Download and Inquiry_Submitted tracked via plausible custom events.
reference.security_and_compliance: Server-side reCAPTCHA v3 verification via Google siteverify API; RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY in Vercel encrypted environment variables. Honeypot field hp_field validated server-side; non-empty returns 200 silently. Rate limiting via Vercel Edge middleware token bucket in Vercel KV: 5 POST /api/inquiry per IP per 10 minutes. Salesforce access token stored in SALESFORCE_ACCESS_TOKEN Vercel environment variable; never exposed to client bundle. HTTPS enforced by Vercel; HSTS and CSP headers in next.config.js headers config: script-src 'self' www.google.com/recaptcha plausible.io 'nonce-{build_nonce}', img-src 'self' data:, frame-src www.google.com. No cookies; Plausible is cookieless.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Vercel project linked to GitHub; automatic preview on PR push; production deploy on main push. GitHub Actions: npm ci, next build, Lighthouse CI performance >= 95, axe-core WCAG 2.1 AA check. Custom domain with Vercel-managed SSL. Vercel KV on Hobby tier sufficient for landing page rate limit traffic. Rollback: Vercel dashboard instant rollback to any previous deployment retained 30 days.
reference.observability: Plausible Analytics goals: Inquiry_Submitted (POST /api/inquiry success callback), PDF_Download (onclick on project summary link), CTA_Click (hero button). Plausible dashboard: page views, inquiry conversion rate, referrer sources (LinkedIn, direct, investor newsletters). Vercel deployment logs: API route outcomes logged via console.error with structured JSON including Salesforce API response code. No APM needed; Vercel Edge function invocation metrics available in Vercel dashboard.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Approximately $20/month: Vercel Pro $20 for custom domain, team members, Vercel KV for rate limiting. Plausible Analytics $9/month up to 10K pageviews. Salesforce Lead API within existing CRM subscription. GitHub Actions free tier for CI. Scales automatically via Vercel Edge CDN during investor campaign traffic spikes.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (5 weeks): design and copy finalization (weeks 1-2), Next.js component build with YAML config and Zod validation (weeks 2-4), server-side reCAPTCHA and Salesforce Lead API integration, Lighthouse and axe-core CI, Vercel production launch for Series A campaign. Phase 2 (4 weeks after Series A close): password-protected investor data room via Next.js middleware auth, multilingual support (Japanese, Korean) via next-i18next, construction progress photo gallery with S3 source.
reference.development_guidelines: reCAPTCHA secret key and Salesforce access token must never appear in client-side code or console logs; enforced via pre-commit secret scan hook. YAML config changes require Zod schema validation to pass in CI before merge. Lighthouse CI gate blocks merge if performance score drops below 95. axe-core accessibility checks block merge on WCAG 2.1 AA violations. All user-visible strings in en.json locale file for future i18n compatibility.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: reCAPTCHA v3 score thresholds may block legitimate investors submitting from corporate proxy networks with atypically low behavioral scores. Mitigation: log borderline scores (0.4-0.5) without blocking during first week; adjust threshold based on observed investor traffic patterns. Risk: Salesforce API rate limits at 100 calls/minute on standard edition may cause inquiry drops during campaign launch spike. Mitigation: silent 200 response with Vercel log drain backup allows manual CRM entry for any missed leads. Tradeoff: returning 200 on all form outcomes prevents bot feedback but requires active monitoring of Vercel logs for genuine Salesforce integration failures.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Vercel KV over Redis for rate limiting due to Vercel-native integration simplicity for landing page traffic. Selected reCAPTCHA v3 over hCaptcha for Salesforce form tracking compatibility. Salesforce Lead API chosen over Web-to-Lead form to enable custom field mapping and campaign source tagging for Series A attribution.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision for a desktop repricing tool. The blocking issue is ISS-003: token expiry not surfaced to the user before marketplace API failures. The fix is to add a token expiry_at field to marketplace_credentials, a 24-hour expiry monitoring loop, and an automatic token refresh attempt before each poll cycle. The plan is otherwise sound and does not need structural changes.

fix_report.action_taken: Added token expiry_at field to marketplace_credentials table; implemented a 24-hour expiry monitoring loop in the Poller module that checks each token's expiry_at and surfaces a banner warning in the UI if expiry is within 24 hours; added automatic token refresh attempt before each poll cycle; documented the token refresh flow for each marketplace (Amazon LWA, eBay OAuth 2.0, Walmart API key) in the security section.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Resellers will be notified of expiring OAuth tokens before they cause silent marketplace API failures; automatic refresh attempts will prevent polling failures without requiring manual intervention.

parsed.title: Marketplace Reseller Desktop Repricing Tool v3
parsed.executive_summary: Electron 32 desktop application for independent marketplace resellers, polling competitor prices on Amazon SP-API, eBay OAuth 2.0, and Walmart Marketplace every 15 minutes, evaluating margin rules, and building a diff-style reprice queue with expected margin impact. OAuth tokens stored in OS keychain via Electron safeStorage; DuckDB handles local price analytics with 90-day retention. Resellers approve reprices and submit batches to marketplace APIs. ISS-003 token expiry monitoring added in this revision.
parsed.architecture_overview: Electron 32 main process manages app lifecycle, auto-updater, and OS keychain access via safeStorage. Vue 3 renderer process renders the repricing dashboard with a diff-style reprice queue table. A FastAPI sidecar launched as a subprocess by Electron main process on startup and killed on exit. The sidecar exposes typed IPC bridge for all sidecar HTTP calls. A DuckDB file in the OS app data directory stores listing_snapshots and competitor_price_points as append-only columnar tables. A Poller module runs a scheduler per marketplace, calling the marketplace price API every 15 minutes. A RepricingEngine module evaluates each listing_snapshot against active repricing_rules and computes proposed_price. A BatchSubmitter module groups approved reprice items by marketplace and calls the pricing API in batches respecting rate limits. A settings module reads and writes OAuth2 access tokens from Electron safeStorage. A Sentry Electron SDK captures crash reports. No cloud infrastructure; all data and credentials reside on the local desktop.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Electron 32, Vue 3, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, PrimeVue component library, Vue Query for sidecar IPC. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, sp-api-python (Amazon SP-API), ebaysdk (eBay OAuth 2.0), walmart-api (Walmart Marketplace API key), DuckDB 0.11 for local analytics. Database: DuckDB file in OS app data directory. Auth: Electron safeStorage for OAuth2 tokens. Testing: pytest for FastAPI sidecar, Vitest for Vue 3 components, Electron Forge for builds. Observability: Sentry Electron SDK. CI: GitHub Actions with macOS and Windows matrix.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Reprice Queue: diff table showing current_price_cents, proposed_price_cents, expected_margin_pct, and approve/override buttons per listing. Margin Rule Builder: create floor price, target margin percentage, ceiling price, and apply_to category/sku_list. Listings Table: searchable table with current_price_cents, competitor_low_price_cents, and margin_pct. Channel Performance Dashboard: per-marketplace total_listings, repriced_count, avg_margin_pct, and submission_error_rate. Settings: marketplace connections, poll frequency (15/30/60 minutes), token expiry warning threshold (24 hours), and auto-refresh interval.
parsed.system_components: Electron 32 main process (app lifecycle, auto-updater, safeStorage), Electron 32 renderer process (Vue 3 dashboard), Electron 32 preload script (TypeScript, safeStorage access), Electron 32 preload script (IPC bridge to FastAPI sidecar), FastAPI sidecar (Python 3.12, subprocess, HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:8923), Poller module (15-minute scheduler per marketplace, APScheduler), RepricingEngine module (margin rule evaluation, queue builder), BatchSubmitter module (batch SP-API calls), Analytics module (DuckDB analytical queries), Settings module (safeStorage token management), Sentry Electron SDK (crash reporting), DuckDB file (listing_snapshots and competitor_price_points), Electron auto-update server (GitHub Actions deploy).
parsed.workflows: 15-Minute Poll and Margin Rule Evaluation: Poller module runs APScheduler job per marketplace at 15-minute intervals; calls marketplace price API for tracked ASINs/item IDs; upserts competitor_price_points to DuckDB; evaluates each listing_snapshot against active repricing_rules: if proposed_price differs from current_price by >threshold, add to repricing_rules queue with status=pending; if proposed_price is within threshold, mark as approved. Token Expiry Monitoring: 24-hour expiry monitoring loop in Poller module checks each marketplace_credentials record for expiry_at; if expiry_at is within 24 hours, renders a banner warning in the UI with a 'Refresh Token' button; on button click, calls marketplace OAuth2 token refresh endpoint (Amazon LWA, eBay OAuth 2.0, Walmart API key) and logs refresh result to reprice_run_logs with status=refreshing; if refresh fails, logs error and surfaces a 'Token Refresh Failed' warning.
parsed.data_model: marketplace_credentials (id VARCHAR, marketplace ENUM amazon/ebay/walmart, token_ref VARCHAR stored as keychain key reference, connected_at TIMESTAMP). listing_snapshots (id UUID, marketplace, seller_sku VARCHAR, asin_or_item_id VARCHAR, title VARCHAR, current_price_cents INT, cost_price_cents INT, polled_at TIMESTAMP). competitor_price_points (id UUID, asin_or_item_id VARCHAR, marketplace, competitor_seller_id VARCHAR, price_cents INT, condition VARCHAR, polled_at TIMESTAMP). repricing_rules (id UUID, name VARCHAR, floor_price_cents INT, target_margin_pct DECIMAL, ceiling_price_cents INT, applies_to ENUM all/category/sku_list). reprice_run_logs (id UUID, run_at TIMESTAMP, marketplace, sku VARCHAR, old_price_cents INT, proposed_price_cents INT, status ENUM pending/approved/submitted/failed, submitted_at TIMESTAMP). channel_performance_reports (id UUID, marketplace, report_date DATE, total_listings INT, repriced_count INT, avg_margin_pct DECIMAL, submission_error_rate INT).
parsed.api_design: Local FastAPI sidecar HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:8923. GET /api/v1/reprice-queue returns [{id, listing_id, current_price_cents, proposed_price_cents, expected_margin_pct, approve_url, override_url}] for the current reprice queue. POST /api/v1/reprice-queue/approve/{id} body {status: approved} returns {status: approved}. POST /api/v1/reprice-queue/override/{id} body {status: override, new_price_cents} returns {status: override, new_price_cents}. GET /api/v1/reprice-run-logs?marketplace=amazon&status=pending returns [{id, run_at, marketplace, sku, old_price_cents, proposed_price_cents, status, submitted_at}]. POST /api/v1/reprice-run-logs body {run_at, marketplace, sku, old_price_cents, proposed_price_cents, status, submitted_at} returns {id}. GET /api/v1/settings returns {marketplace_connections, poll_frequency, token_expiry_hours, auto_refresh_interval}.
parsed.security_and_compliance: OAuth2 access tokens for marketplace accounts stored via Electron safeStorage (AES-256 backed by OS keychain); no credentials in DuckDB or log files. Token storage described in ISS-003 fix. Token expiry_at field added to marketplace_credentials table; 24-hour expiry monitoring loop in Poller module. Automatic token refresh attempt before each poll cycle; logs and surfaces 'Token Refresh Failed' if refresh fails. Token expiry warning banner rendered in UI 24 hours before expiry. Marketplace credential rotation: new credentials loaded on each app launch; old credentials deleted from safeStorage. TLS 1.3 enforced for all outbound marketplace API calls. Rate limit: Amazon SP-API allows ~1 request/second per credential; poller module implements token bucket with configurable interval (default 10 seconds) to prevent rate limit exhaustion. No credentials written to DuckDB or log files. Sentry Electron SDK redacts Authorization headers in debug logs.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions matrix: macOS (Electron Forge notarization, EV certificate), Windows (EV certificate), Linux (EV certificate). Electron Forge builds DMG (macOS) and NSIS installer (Windows) on tag push. Code-signed with Apple Developer ID (macOS notarization) and EV certificate (Windows SmartScreen). Distributed via self-hosted Electron auto-update server on GitHub Actions runner. Auto-update server: Electron auto-update endpoint, code-signed with Apple Developer ID, EV certificate for Windows. Beta testers: GitHub Actions beta workflow distributes Electron auto-update server build to beta email list. Release: GitHub Actions release workflow triggers on tag push; auto-update server publishes to Electron auto-update store. DuckDB file versioned in OS app data directory; weekly backup to local backup directory. No cloud infrastructure; no server-side monitoring required for v1.
parsed.observability: Sentry Electron SDK captures unhandled exceptions, crashes, and API errors with request context. Local rotating log file in app data dir (7-day retention). No server-side monitoring for v1. Sentry dashboard: unhandled exception rate, crash frequency by marketplace, API error rate by marketplace. Local log file: timestamp, marketplace, error message, HTTP status code. No metrics API for v1.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud cost for v1. GitHub Actions free tier covers CI and auto-update server. Electron auto-update store (GitHub) is free. DuckDB file growth: 15-minute poll interval means 10,000 SKUs generate ~100 price points per hour; 15-minute poll means 100 price points * 24 hours = 2400 price points per day; DuckDB file growth is manageable at 2 GB. Scaling: Electron renderer process scales horizontally via Electron main process; DuckDB file is append-only and scales with the OS app data directory.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Amazon SP-API connection, listing snapshot import, competitor price polling every 15 minutes, diff-style reprice queue with approve/override, margin rule builder, reprice submission to Amazon SP-API, DuckDB analytics, Electron auto-update server, GitHub Actions CI with macOS and Windows matrix, Sentry crash reporting. Phase 2 eBay and Walmart (8 weeks): eBay OAuth 2.0 and Walmart Marketplace API key connections, eBay price polling, Walmart price polling, eBay and Walmart reprice queue integration, token expiry monitoring and automatic refresh, token expiry warning banner. Phase 3 Multi-Seat Licensing (future): Electron auto-update server for multi-seat licensing, central sync server for reprice history and settings, AI-driven margin rule suggestions.
parsed.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in renderer and main processes. Vue 3 TypeScript strict mode. Vitest for Vue 3 components. pytest for FastAPI sidecar unit tests. Electron Forge for Electron build validation. No hardcoded marketplace credentials in code; all credentials via Electron safeStorage. Token refresh logic tested with mock marketplace OAuth2 endpoints. DuckDB queries must be parameterized; no string interpolation in SQL. Sentry release tag for crash reports. PR requires 1 approval; no direct merge to main.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Amazon SP-API rate limits (1 request/second per credential) could cause polling failures if token bucket is not configured correctly. Mitigation: token bucket with configurable interval (default 10 seconds) in poller module; if bucket is empty, poll frequency reduced to 30 minutes. Risk: DuckDB file growth beyond 2 GB on 10,000 SKUs. Mitigation: weekly backup to local backup directory; file size monitoring via 2 GB threshold alert in Sentry. Tradeoff: Electron safeStorage for OAuth2 tokens over Electron crypto module — safeStorage is simpler to implement but less secure for high-value credentials; mitigated by 24-hour expiry monitoring and automatic refresh. Tradeoff: local DuckDB over a cloud-hosted analytics service — local avoids cloud egress costs and data residency requirements for v1.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved: diff-style reprice queue with margin impact column and approve-all flow added in round 2. ISS-002 resolved: DuckDB 90-day retention cleanup job added in round 2. ISS-003 resolved: token expiry monitoring and automatic refresh added in this revision.

reference.thinking_summary: The key fix in round 3 is ISS-003: OAuth token expiry lifecycle management. I need to add a token expiry tracking field to marketplace_credentials, implement 24-hour pre-expiry warnings in the settings UI, and ensure the poller checks token freshness before each poll cycle. Amazon LWA, eBay OAuth 2.0, and Walmart each have different refresh token TTLs so the expiry monitoring must be per-marketplace. I will also document Walmart Marketplace rate limits which were flagged as missing.

fix_report.action_taken: Added token_expires_at field to marketplace_credentials; poller module now checks token freshness before each poll cycle and attempts refresh if within 2 hours of expiry; Settings page shows expiry countdown per marketplace with amber warning at 24 hours and red warning at 2 hours; Sentry alert raised if automatic refresh fails
fix_report.expected_outcome: Resellers will see proactive expiry warnings before marketplace API failures occur; automatic refresh will silently renew tokens in the happy path; manual re-authentication flow is clearly surfaced when refresh fails

reference.title: Marketplace Reseller Desktop Repricing Tool
reference.executive_summary: An Electron 32 desktop application with Vue 3 frontend and embedded Python 3.12/FastAPI sidecar, backed by a local DuckDB database, that automates competitor price monitoring and bulk repricing across Amazon, eBay, and Walmart Marketplace. OAuth2 tokens for each marketplace are stored in the OS keychain via Electron safeStorage with proactive expiry monitoring and automatic token refresh. A 15-minute poller refreshes competitor price points; the RepricingEngine evaluates each listing against margin rules and populates a diff-style approval queue showing current price, proposed price, and expected margin before batch submission.
reference.architecture_overview: Electron 32 main process manages the app lifecycle, OS keychain access via safeStorage, and auto-updater. The Vue 3 renderer process handles all UI via a typed IPC bridge exposed through a preload script. A Python 3.12/FastAPI sidecar is spawned as a child process on app start and listens on loopback 127.0.0.1:8923 — the Electron preload script proxies API calls to the sidecar via HTTP. DuckDB stores all listing snapshots and competitor price point history locally with no cloud dependency. The Poller module runs APScheduler jobs per connected marketplace on 15-minute intervals, checking token freshness before each cycle and refreshing tokens within 2 hours of expiry. The RepricingEngine processes each listing_snapshot against the active margin profile and pushes candidates to the reprice queue. The BatchSubmitter groups approved items by marketplace and submits reprices in rate-limit-compliant batches.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Electron 32, Vue 3, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, PrimeVue, Pinia, Vue Query. Backend sidecar: Python 3.12, FastAPI, APScheduler, sp-api-python, ebaysdk, DuckDB Python SDK, cryptography library for keychain IPC. Data: DuckDB (columnar, local file). Distribution: Electron Forge with NSIS (Windows) and DMG (macOS); code-signed; self-hosted auto-update server. Observability: Sentry Electron SDK; local rotating log files.
reference.functional_feature_map: Marketplace Connections: connect Amazon, eBay, and Walmart via OAuth2 flows; settings UI shows token expiry countdown per marketplace with amber/red warnings; automatic background token refresh at 2-hour threshold. Listing Snapshot Import: fetch active listings from each connected marketplace via API, upsert into DuckDB listing_snapshots table. Competitor Price Polling: APScheduler 15-minute jobs per marketplace fetch competitor prices for all tracked SKUs and upsert competitor_price_points. Reprice Queue: RepricingEngine evaluates each listing against active margin profile, computes proposed_price, and populates queue with current vs. proposed diff and expected_margin_pct. Batch Reprice Submission: user approves or overrides items in queue; BatchSubmitter groups by marketplace and submits in rate-limit-compliant batches. Analytics Dashboard: DuckDB queries powering margin trend per ASIN, reprice action history, competitor price history charts in Vue renderer.
reference.system_components: Electron 32 main process (app lifecycle, safeStorage, auto-updater); Vue 3 renderer (SPA via preload IPC bridge); Python 3.12 FastAPI sidecar child process on loopback:8923; DuckDB file in OS app data directory; APScheduler for marketplace pollers; Sentry Electron SDK; self-hosted Electron Forge auto-update server.
reference.workflows: Token Expiry Monitoring and Refresh: On each Poller tick, the sidecar checks marketplace_credentials.token_expires_at for the target marketplace; if within 2 hours of expiry, attempts token refresh using the stored refresh token via the marketplace OAuth2 token endpoint; on success, updates token and token_expires_at in keychain and database; on failure, sets token_status=refresh_failed and emits an IPC event to the renderer causing the Settings page to display a red re-authenticate banner. The poller aborts the current cycle and logs the failure to avoid sending requests with an expired token. Settings page polls token status every 60 seconds and shows per-marketplace countdown: green (>24 hours), amber (2-24 hours), red (<2 hours or refresh_failed). Reprice Queue Approval and Batch Submit: User opens Reprice Queue page, views diff table with columns: SKU, marketplace, current_price, proposed_price, expected_margin_pct; clicks approve on individual rows or 'Approve All' for bulk; approved items submitted to BatchSubmitter; BatchSubmitter groups by marketplace, respects rate limits (Amazon SP-API: 1 req/sec with burst 5; eBay Sell API: 5 req/sec; Walmart Marketplace: 10 req/sec); submission results written to reprice_run_logs with status submitted or failed; failed items surfaced as retryable in queue.
reference.data_model: marketplace_credentials (marketplace ENUM amazon/ebay/walmart, token_ref VARCHAR keychain key, token_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, refresh_token_ref VARCHAR keychain key, token_status ENUM active/expiring_soon/refresh_failed, connected_at). listing_snapshots (id, marketplace, seller_sku, asin_or_item_id, title, current_price_cents INT, cost_price_cents INT, polled_at TIMESTAMPTZ). competitor_price_points (id, asin_or_item_id, marketplace, competitor_seller_id, price_cents INT, condition, polled_at TIMESTAMPTZ, PRIMARY KEY or UNIQUE per polled_at). repricing_rules (id, name, floor_price_cents INT, target_margin_pct DECIMAL(5,2), ceiling_price_cents INT, applies_to ENUM all/category/sku_list, active BOOLEAN). reprice_run_logs (id, run_at TIMESTAMPTZ, marketplace, seller_sku, old_price_cents INT, proposed_price_cents INT, expected_margin_pct DECIMAL(5,2), status ENUM pending/approved/submitted/failed/overridden, submitted_at). channel_performance_reports (id, marketplace, report_date DATE, total_listings INT, repriced_count INT, avg_margin_pct DECIMAL).
reference.api_design: FastAPI sidecar on loopback:8923. GET /listings?marketplace=amazon&min_margin_gap=5 (listings with margin below threshold), GET /queue (pending reprice items with diff data), POST /queue/approve (body: {item_ids:[...]} or {approve_all: true}), POST /queue/submit (trigger BatchSubmitter for approved items), GET /analytics/margin-trend?sku=:sku&days=30 (DuckDB query), GET /analytics/reprice-history?days=7, GET /settings/connections (marketplace status + token expiry_at + token_status), POST /settings/connect/:marketplace (initiate OAuth2 flow), POST /settings/refresh/:marketplace (manual token refresh trigger), GET /health.
