parsed.thinking_summary: This is a simple CLI tool for food truck operators. The API backend is a single Express service on App Platform with no frontend. The CLI is a standalone npm package. Key decisions: Commander.js for typed CLI arg parsing, JWT via keytar for OS keychain storage, PostgreSQL 16 on DigitalOcean Managed Databases for the relational store, and a webhook-based notification pattern for display board push rather than polling. The plan quality is intentionally weak — the plan sections are thin and the data model is incomplete.
parsed.title: Food Truck Menu Board CLI Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: A CLI tool for food truck operators to push menu item availability (86 items), update prices, manage display zones, and list board status. Deployed on DigitalOcean App Platform with a single Express REST API and a standalone npm package. JWT authentication via keytar stores tokens in the OS keychain. Menu item state is persisted in PostgreSQL 16 on DigitalOcean Managed Databases.
parsed.architecture_overview: Single-service Express API on DigitalOcean App Platform (Node.js 22) serving REST endpoints consumed by the CLI npm package. CLI is a standalone package with typed command definitions using Commander.js. JWT tokens issued by the API via /auth/login are stored in the OS keychain via keytar. PostgreSQL 16 on DigitalOcean Managed Database stores menu item and board configuration state. A webhook pattern is used to notify display boards of menu item changes rather than polling at short intervals.
parsed.technology_stack: Runtime: Node.js 22. CLI: Commander.js with @commanderjs/cli. API: Express 4 with TypeScript. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on DigitalOcean Managed Databases. Auth: JWT via keytar npm package. Hosting: DigitalOcean App Platform (Node.js runtime, 2 replicas). CI: GitHub Actions with npm publish on tag. Observability: Datadog APM agent on App Platform.
parsed.functional_feature_map: menu:eighty-six: toggle availability of an item on a board, with confirmation prompt. menu:price: update price_cents for an item. board:list: get current board status and item list. board:status: set board status to online/offline/error. schedule:set: create availability window for an item on a board. schedule:list: get availability windows for an item on a board. webhook:create: register a webhook URL for a display board to receive menu item changes.
parsed.system_components: DigitalOcean App Platform Node.js API service (2 replicas, auto-scaling). DigitalOcean Managed PostgreSQL 16 database (2 replicas, auto-scaling). DigitalOcean Managed Redis 7 (CLI cache). keytar npm package for JWT keychain storage. Express API router with menu, boards, schedules, and auth modules. CLI command router with Commander.js. Datadog APM agent on App Platform.
parsed.workflows: Menu Item Push: Operator runs 'menu:eighty-six --board-id 1 --item-id 123 --reason unavailable' in the terminal. CLI calls POST /api/v1/items/123/availability with body {availability: unavailable}. API validates JWT, checks board ownership, inserts item_updates row, returns 200. Operator sees confirmation message. Webhook Notification: Operator runs 'menu:webhook --board-id 1 --url https://my-display-board.com'. API creates webhook_events record, sends POST to URL with JSON payload {item_id, board_id, action: 'menu_updated', reason}. Display board receives webhook and updates its menu display. Price Update: Operator runs 'menu:price --item-id 123 --price 12.50'. CLI calls PATCH /api/v1/items/123/price with body {price_cents: 12500}. API validates JWT, checks board ownership, updates item price, inserts item_updates row, returns 200.
parsed.data_model: display_boards (id UUID PK, operator_id UUID FK, name VARCHAR, location_label VARCHAR, status ENUM online/offline/error, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ). menu_items (id UUID PK, board_id UUID FK, name VARCHAR, price_cents INT, category VARCHAR, availability ENUM available/eighty_six, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). display_zones (id UUID PK, board_id UUID FK, zone_name VARCHAR, zone_type ENUM main/specials/drinks). availability_schedules (id UUID PK, item_id UUID FK, zone_id UUID FK, start_time TIME, end_time TIME, days_of_week INT[]). item_updates (id UUID PK, item_id UUID FK, action VARCHAR, old_value VARCHAR, new_value VARCHAR, changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
parsed.api_design: REST at /api/v1. GET /boards?status=online&operator_id=X&page=1 -> list boards with status and item count. POST /boards -> create board with name and location. PATCH /boards/:id -> set board status. GET /items?board_id=X -> list items on board with availability. PATCH /items/:id -> update item availability or price. POST /schedules -> create availability window. GET /schedules?item_id=X -> list availability windows for item. POST /webhooks -> register webhook URL. All endpoints require JWT Bearer token.
parsed.security_and_compliance: JWT issued by /auth/login with 24-hour expiry, stored in OS keychain via keytar. API validates JWT on every request; unauthorized requests return 401 with error code. Board ownership enforced at API layer — all mutations require operator_id FK match. Rate limiting: 100 requests per minute per operator_id via Redis token bucket. TLS enforced on all API endpoints. No plaintext credentials in source code or build artifacts.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: DigitalOcean App Platform: Node.js runtime, 2 replicas, auto-scaling on CPU 60%. Managed PostgreSQL 16 with pgBouncer connection pooling. Managed Redis 7 for CLI cache. GitHub Actions CI: on push to main, tsc typecheck + Jest unit tests, Docker build for API image, deploy to App Platform via doctl. On git tag v*, npm publish CLI package via npm publish. Rollback: App Platform auto-scales to 0 replicas on API error. Database migrations via Alembic run as one-off job before deploy.
parsed.observability: Datadog APM agent on App Platform: custom metrics: item_availability_changes_per_hour by board_id, board_status_change_frequency, api_latency_p99. Structured JSON logs with operator_id and board_id on every request. Alert: API p99 latency above 2000ms triggers PagerDuty P2.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: DigitalOcean App Platform: ~$20/month for 2 replicas. Managed PostgreSQL: ~$10/month. Managed Redis: ~$5/month. Total ~$35/month. Scaling: App Platform auto-scales to 0 replicas on CPU 60% target. PostgreSQL connection pool handles 100 concurrent CLI sessions.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): JWT auth, menu:eighty-six and restore, menu:price, board:list and status, schedule:set, webhook:create, CLI distribution via npm. Phase 2 (4 weeks): schedule:list, item_updates audit log, webhook:receive, board:zone management. Phase 3 (4 weeks): web dashboard for operators, multi-location chain support, image upload for menu items.
parsed.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for CLI commands. Commander.js for typed command definitions. Jest unit tests for all API handlers and CLI command logic. No plaintext credentials in any file. Alembic migrations for database schema changes. PR requires one senior review.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: keytar keychain storage failure leaves operators unable to log in. Mitigation: CLI prompts for password on first use; keytar fallback to plaintext file. Risk: PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion under high CLI concurrency. Mitigation: Redis token bucket rate limiting. Tradeoff: webhook-based notification over polling avoids polling load at scale but requires display board to be configured with a webhook URL.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Selected keytar over localforage for JWT keychain storage. Confirmed webhook over polling for display board notification. Chose PostgreSQL 16 over MongoDB for relational item/board/schedule state.

reference.thinking_summary: Designing a simple CLI tool for food truck operators with a companion REST API backend. Key decisions: Commander.js for typed command definitions with auto-generated help, JWT in OS keychain for secure credential storage without asking operators to manage files, PostgreSQL for board/item state with an append-only item_updates table for audit history. Keeping the API surface minimal — four core commands map to four API endpoints — so the MVP can ship in 8 weeks and be validated with operators before adding scheduling and web UI.
reference.title: Digital Menu Board CLI Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A Node.js 22 TypeScript CLI tool (menuboard-cli) for food truck operators and small restaurant owners to manage cloud-connected digital display boards from the command line. Operators run commands to 86 items, update prices, manage display zones, and configure scheduled availability windows. A companion REST API on DigitalOcean App Platform persists board state in PostgreSQL and pushes changes to display hardware. The CLI is distributed via npm and works on macOS and Linux terminals with JWT-based authentication stored securely in the OS keychain.
reference.architecture_overview: Two-component architecture: a Node.js 22 CLI package (menuboard-cli on npm) and a companion REST API backend on DigitalOcean App Platform. The CLI wraps authenticated HTTPS calls to the API; no logic executes locally beyond argument parsing, output formatting, and credential retrieval from the OS keychain. The API backend manages all persistent state in a PostgreSQL 16 managed database — menu items, board configurations, display zones, and availability schedules. Display boards poll the API on a configurable TTL (default 10 seconds) to refresh their displayed content, ensuring 86 status changes are visible on screens within one poll cycle. Board online/offline status is tracked via last_seen_at heartbeat timestamps.
reference.technology_stack: CLI: Node.js 22, TypeScript 5.4, Commander.js for command/flag parsing, keytar for OS keychain credential storage, chalk for terminal output formatting, ora for progress spinners. API: Node.js 22, TypeScript 5.4, Express 4, pg driver with pgBouncer connection pooling. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on DigitalOcean Managed Databases. Infrastructure: DigitalOcean App Platform (2 replicas), GitHub Actions CI/CD. Observability: Datadog APM, structured JSON logging. Distribution: npm package registry.
reference.functional_feature_map: menuboard item:eighty-six <item-id|name> — marks item as sold out, pushes to all configured boards immediately. menuboard item:restore <item-id|name> — marks item as available again. menuboard item:price <item-id> --price <cents> — updates displayed price in cents to avoid floating-point issues. menuboard board:list — shows all operator boards with online/offline status and item counts. menuboard board:status <board-id> — displays current zone layout and all item availability states. menuboard schedule:set <item-id> --start 07:00 --end 11:00 --days mon,tue,wed,thu,fri — configures automatic show/hide windows. menuboard auth:login --email — initiates authentication and stores JWT in OS keychain.
reference.system_components: menuboard-cli npm package: Commander.js command tree, HTTP client with retry logic, keytar credential vault integration, chalk-formatted tabular output for board status. REST API: Express router with versioned /api/v1 prefix, JWT middleware for auth, operator ownership validation middleware, menu-items module, boards module, schedules module. PostgreSQL 16: display_boards, menu_items, display_zones, availability_schedules, item_updates (audit log). DigitalOcean App Platform: 2 API replicas with health check on /health, App Platform Managed DB connection, Datadog APM agent sidecar.
reference.workflows: Item 86 Workflow: Operator runs 'menuboard item:eighty-six tacos'. CLI retrieves JWT from keychain, calls PATCH /api/v1/items/tacos/availability with body {availability: 'eighty_six'}. API validates operator owns the board containing the item, updates menu_items.availability, inserts record to item_updates audit log, returns updated item. CLI prints 'Tacos marked as 86d on Truck Board 1'. Board polls API within 10 seconds and updates display. Price Update Workflow: Operator runs 'menuboard item:price breakfast-burrito --price 1295'. CLI calls PATCH /api/v1/items/breakfast-burrito/price. API validates ownership, updates price_cents field, logs change to item_updates with previous_price_cents for rollback reference. CLI confirms with formatted output showing old and new price.
reference.data_model: display_boards (id UUID PK, operator_id UUID FK, name VARCHAR, location_label VARCHAR, status ENUM online/offline/error, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). menu_items (id UUID PK, board_id UUID FK, zone_id UUID FK nullable, name VARCHAR, price_cents INT, category VARCHAR, availability ENUM available/eighty_six, display_order INT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). display_zones (id UUID PK, board_id UUID FK, zone_name VARCHAR, zone_type ENUM main/specials/drinks, position INT). availability_schedules (id UUID PK, item_id UUID FK, start_time TIME, end_time TIME, days_of_week SMALLINT, active BOOLEAN). item_updates (id UUID PK, item_id UUID FK, operator_id UUID FK, change_type ENUM price/availability/schedule, previous_value JSONB, new_value JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
reference.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. Authentication: POST /auth/login (issue JWT), POST /auth/refresh. Items: PATCH /api/v1/items/:id/availability {availability: 'eighty_six'|'available'}, PATCH /api/v1/items/:id/price {price_cents: INT}, GET /api/v1/items?board_id=:id. Boards: GET /api/v1/boards (list operator boards with status), GET /api/v1/boards/:id/status (full zone and item state). Schedules: POST /api/v1/schedules {item_id, start_time, end_time, days_of_week}, DELETE /api/v1/schedules/:id. All responses: {data, meta} envelope. Errors: {error: {code, message, request_id}}.
reference.security_and_compliance: Authentication: JWT HS256 signed with 512-bit secret, 24-hour expiry. CLI stores token via keytar in OS keychain (macOS Keychain, Linux Secret Service, Windows Credential Manager) — never written to disk as plaintext. Operator ownership enforcement: every board/item mutation runs SELECT board_id FROM display_boards WHERE operator_id = $jwt_sub before executing; returns 403 if mismatch. Transport: HTTPS only; HTTP connections refused. Rate limiting: 60 requests/minute per JWT on all mutation endpoints via express-rate-limit. Input validation: Joi schemas on all request bodies to prevent injection. API deployed with Helmet.js security headers.
reference.deployment_and_operations: DigitalOcean App Platform: 2 replicas of the Node.js API, auto-deploy on push to main via GitHub integration. Managed PostgreSQL 16 on same DO datacenter (NYC3) with automatic daily backups and point-in-time recovery. pgBouncer connection pooling at 20 connections per replica. Health check endpoint GET /health returns DB ping status. GitHub Actions pipeline: on push to main — tsc --noEmit type check, Jest unit tests (70% coverage gate), Docker build, App Platform deploy via doctl CLI. On git tag v* — run full test suite, npm publish --access public.
reference.observability: Datadog APM on API with trace sampling at 100% for write operations (item availability/price changes). Structured JSON logs on every request: operator_id, board_id, command, duration_ms, status_code, request_id. Custom Datadog metrics: menuboard.item_eighty_six_count (counter by board), menuboard.price_updates_per_hour, menuboard.board_offline_duration_minutes. Alert: board offline > 30 minutes triggers Datadog monitor notification to operator email.
reference.cost_and_scaling: DigitalOcean App Platform Basic plan (~$24/month for 2 replicas) and Managed PostgreSQL Basic plan (~$15/month) totals approximately $40/month for the backend. npm package hosting is free. At 1000 daily CLI commands across all operators, PostgreSQL handles load comfortably on the smallest instance class. Scaling path: upgrade to DO App Platform Professional tier and add read replicas when operator count exceeds 500.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 weeks): Core four commands (item:eighty-six, item:restore, item:price, board:list), JWT auth with keychain storage, PostgreSQL schema with item_updates audit log, REST API on App Platform, GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline, npm package published. Phase 2 (3 weeks): menuboard board:status with zone breakdown, schedule:set for availability windows, board heartbeat and online/offline tracking, Datadog APM integration. Phase 3 (future): Web dashboard UI for operators preferring GUI, multi-location chain support with board grouping, image upload for menu items, webhook push to boards replacing polling.
reference.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode throughout CLI and API. Commander.js command files colocated with their HTTP client calls — one file per command group (items.ts, boards.ts, schedules.ts). API route handlers thin — business logic in service modules, not Express handlers. Jest unit tests for all service module functions with PostgreSQL queries mocked via pg-mock. Integration tests against a real Postgres test container in CI. Semantic versioning for CLI; breaking flag changes require major version bump. CLI --help output tested as part of CI to prevent accidental interface regressions.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Board polling interval creates up to 10-second delay between CLI 86 command and screen update. Mitigation: document this to operators; Phase 2 can add WebSocket push for instant updates if delay is unacceptable. Risk: keytar dependency requires native OS keychain access, which can fail in some CI or container environments. Mitigation: fallback to encrypted local config file with user warning. Tradeoff: Commander.js over Ink/React-based TUI — simpler implementation and faster startup time at the cost of no interactive prompts in MVP. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL over Redis for item state — durability and relational queries justify the cost over a cache-only approach.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected Commander.js over yargs for better TypeScript ergonomics and built-in help generation. Chose npm distribution over Homebrew tap for faster release iteration. Selected OS keychain via keytar over environment variable credentials to avoid operators accidentally committing secrets to dotfiles.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 3 must resolve two high-severity issues: ISS-001 (compliance report contention with write path) and ISS-002 (Redis cache key missing tenant scope). The fix for ISS-001 is to provision a dedicated Aurora read replica for compliance report queries and route all SELECT queries to the replica endpoint. The fix for ISS-002 is to prefix all Redis evaluation cache keys with SHA-256 of tenant_context_id to prevent cross-tenant cache collisions. Both fixes are targeted at the primary DB contention and cache key design flaws.

fix_report.action_taken: Added dedicated Aurora read replica named compliance-report-db for all SELECT queries targeting audit_events; all compliance report queries now route to this replica via a read replica endpoint configured in the ALB target group; p99 latency alarm on compliance-report-db p99 latency metric in Datadog
fix_report.expected_outcome: Compliance report batch queries no longer contend with real-time write path; p99 latency on compliance-report-db is isolated from primary DB write path

fix_report.action_taken: Changed Redis evaluation cache key format from (principal_id, resource, action) to sha256(tenant_context_id):principal_id:resource:action; tenant_context_id is the SHA-256 hash of the tenant_context record in PostgreSQL; this prevents cross-tenant cache collisions and ensures cache keys include the tenant scope
fix_report.expected_outcome: Cache keys include tenant scope, preventing cross-tenant decision bleed; ISS-002 resolved

parsed.title: Enterprise Authorization Policy Service - Round 3
parsed.executive_summary: A Go-based authorization service on ECS Fargate with PostgreSQL 16 RLS and Redis 7 cluster mode, serving RBAC/ABAC policy evaluation for enterprise SaaS tenants. Round 3 resolves two high-severity issues: a dedicated Aurora read replica for compliance report queries isolates the write path from the evaluation path, and Redis cache keys now include the tenant_context SHA-256 hash to prevent cross-tenant decision bleed. The service enforces SOC 2 CC6/CC7/CC9 controls and produces immutable audit trails for GDPR data residency.
parsed.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture on ECS Fargate: Gin API tier (Go 1.22) behind ALB with JWT validation and tenant-context injection; policy_engine tier (Go ABAC evaluation tree and role resolver); tenant_router tier (Go JWT validation and tenant_context injection). PostgreSQL 16 with RLS policies per tenant_context enforces tenant isolation. Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster mode stores policy decision cache with SHA-256 tenant-scoped keys. Dedicated Aurora read replica named compliance-report-db handles all SELECT queries targeting audit_events. GitHub Actions CI gates every deploy on p99 latency alarm. Datadog APM traces every evaluation request with custom metrics.
parsed.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, golang-jwt for JWT validation, pgx/v5 for PostgreSQL, redis/v9 for Redis. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS, append-only audit trigger, PgBouncer connection pool. Cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster mode. Storage: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ for primary and read replica. Auth: AWS Cognito for JWKS. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, ECR, S3 for compliance report archives. Observability: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Policy Evaluation Endpoint: POST /v1/evaluate accepts JWT and tenant context, evaluates ABAC policy tree, returns allow/deny decision with policy_matched_id. RBAC Role Management: POST /v1/role-assignments creates role assignments with expires_at. Audit Event Stream: GET /v1/audit-events paginated with principal_identity_id, resource_target, action, decision. Compliance Report Generation: POST /v1/compliance-reports triggers nightly Prefect job to aggregate audit_events into immutable snapshot, uploads to S3 with SHA-256 manifest. Audit Log Export: GET /v1/audit-events/:id returns full audit record for SOC 2 evidence.
parsed.system_components: ALB with target group routing to ECS Fargate cluster (3-7 tasks auto-scaling on p95 latency). ECS Fargate tasks: policy_engine (Go ABAC evaluation), tenant_router (JWT validation and tenant_context injection), audit_writer (append-only PostgreSQL publisher). Dedicated Aurora read replica: compliance-report-db (read-only, no write access). Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster mode: policy decision cache with SHA-256 tenant-scoped keys. PostgreSQL 16 Aurora multi-AZ: primary and compliance-report-db. S3 bucket: compliance-report-archive for nightly snapshot uploads. Datadog APM agent sidecar on each ECS task.
parsed.workflows: Policy Evaluation: Tenant sends POST /v1/evaluate with JWT and tenant context; tenant_router validates JWT and injects tenant_context; policy_engine evaluates ABAC policy tree (RBAC: role_name, action, resource_expr); Redis decision cache checked; if miss, policy_engine queries PostgreSQL policy_rules for matching rule_type and subject_expr; if miss, policy_engine queries compliance-report-db for matching audit_events; if miss, policy_engine emits audit_events row; response returned within 5ms. Compliance Report Generation: Prefect job triggers nightly at 02:00 UTC; job queries compliance-report-db for all audit_events from last 24 hours for the tenant context; job aggregates to monthly summary; job writes immutable snapshot to S3 compliance-report-archive/YYYY-MM-DD/; job returns SHA-256 manifest URL; Datadog metric compliance_report_latency_ms updated.
parsed.data_model: policy_rules (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK, rule_type ENUM rbac/abac, subject_expr TEXT, resource_expr TEXT, action_expr TEXT, effect ENUM allow/deny, priority INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). role_assignments (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK, principal_identity_id UUID FK, role_name TEXT, granted_by UUID, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ). permission_scopes (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK, scope_name TEXT, actions TEXT[]). audit_events (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK, principal_identity_id UUID, resource_target TEXT, action TEXT, decision ENUM allow/deny, policy_matched_id UUID, evaluated_at TIMESTAMPTZ) - APPEND ONLY; trigger: UPDATE/DELETE on audit_events raises exception. tenant_contexts (id UUID PK, jwks_url TEXT, encryption_key_arn TEXT, tier ENUM dev/prod, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
parsed.api_design: POST /v1/evaluate {jwt, tenant_context} -> {decision: allow/deny, policy_matched_id, latency_ms}. GET /v1/policies?tenant_context_id=X -> list of policy_rules. POST /v1/policies {rule_type, subject_expr, resource_expr, action_expr, effect, priority, tenant_context_id} -> {id, created_at}. DELETE /v1/policies/:id -> {success: true}. POST /v1/role-assignments {principal_identity_id, role_name, tenant_context_id} -> {id, expires_at}. GET /v1/audit-events?tenant_context_id=X&from=&to= -> paginated audit_events. POST /v1/compliance-reports -> {job_id, status}. GET /v1/compliance-reports/:job_id -> {status, progress, completed_at}. GET /v1/audit-events/:id -> full audit record.
parsed.security_and_compliance: SOC 2 Type II controls: JWKS rotation every 24h via Cognito token refresh; RLS policies on tenant_context enforced at PostgreSQL level; append-only audit_events trigger satisfies CC7.2; GDPR: ALB security group restricts traffic to us-east-1 only; S3 compliance-report-archive bucket policy blocks public access; Datadog log correlation for cross-event audit trail.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: golangci-lint, go test -race, k6 load test at 10K RPS, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with canary 10% traffic shift gated on p99 latency alarm (PagerDuty alert if p99 > 8ms). Environments: dev (single-AZ us-east-1a/1c/1d), staging (multi-AZ us-east-1a/1c/1d), prod (multi-AZ us-east-1a/1c/1d). Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for Cognito JWKS and Aurora credentials. Environments: dev uses dev Aurora instance, staging uses dev Aurora instance with 20% capacity, prod uses Aurora multi-AZ with 3-7 tasks.
parsed.observability: Datadog APM traces on every evaluation request with custom metrics: eval_latency_ms histogram (p50/p95/p99), policy_cache_hit_ratio, audit_write_lag_ms, active_tenant_count. PagerDuty alerts: p99 latency > 8ms (high), compliance-report-db p99 latency > 500ms (medium), audit_write_lag_ms > 500ms (medium), compliance_report_latency_ms > 30s (medium).
parsed.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate: ~$150/month for 3-7 tasks auto-scaling on p95. Aurora PostgreSQL: ~$200/month for multi-AZ primary and read replica. Redis ElastiCache cluster mode: ~$100/month for 3-node cluster. S3 compliance report archive: ~$5/month. Total ~$500/month at 10K RPS. Scaling: ECS auto-scaling handles burst; PgBouncer connection pool prevents memory exhaustion at high tenant counts.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (16 weeks): policy_engine with ABAC evaluation, RBAC role management, JWT auth, PostgreSQL RLS, append-only audit trigger, Cognito JWKS, ALB security group, Datadog APM, GitHub Actions CI with k6 load test gate. Phase 2 (8 weeks): compliance report generation with dedicated Aurora read replica, SHA-256 tenant-scoped Redis cache keys, Prefect nightly job, S3 compliance report archive, Datadog compliance report latency metrics. Phase 3 (8 weeks): Rego policy language support for complex ABAC expressions, OPAL-based real-time policy sync, GraphQL evaluation API, multi-tenant tenant isolation improvements.
parsed.development_guidelines: Go modules with go.sum. golangci-lint enforced in CI. All policy evaluation logic in policy_engine package; no business logic in tenant_router or audit_writer. Redis cache keys must include tenant_context SHA-256 hash; use sha256(tenant_context_id) as key prefix. PostgreSQL RLS policies must be tested with pgAdmin before merge. k6 load test must pass at 10K RPS before any deploy.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ABAC policy tree complexity causes evaluation latency spikes for complex tenant policies. Mitigation: pre-compile policy graphs in Redis and warm Fargate tasks with PgBouncer connection pool. Risk: Redis cache miss during high-volume evaluation traffic. Mitigation: PgBouncer connection pool of 50 per Fargate task; Redis cluster mode with 3-node cluster. Tradeoff: append-only audit_events over materialized view for audit trail immutability; tradeoff accepted for simplicity at this scale.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed append-only audit_events over materialized view for audit trail immutability. Confirmed SHA-256 tenant-scoped Redis keys over raw principal_id:resource:action. Confirmed dedicated Aurora read replica for compliance report queries over routing all SELECT queries to primary. Confirmed Cognito JWKS over self-hosted key store for simplicity.

reference.thinking_summary: Addressing two high-severity issues in round 3: (1) routing compliance report queries to a dedicated Aurora read replica to eliminate write-path contention, and (2) fixing the Redis cache key to include sha256(tenant_context_id) as a prefix to prevent cross-tenant decision bleed. Both fixes are surgical changes to the deployment topology and the policy_engine cache key construction, leaving the core ABAC evaluation architecture intact.

fix_report.action_taken: Added dedicated Aurora read replica endpoint to deployment topology; compliance report generation path now exclusively uses the replica connection string injected via COMPLIANCE_DB_READER_URL env var; primary endpoint reserved for evaluation write path
fix_report.expected_outcome: Nightly compliance report batch queries no longer contend with real-time audit_events writes; p99 evaluation latency returns to baseline during report generation windows

fix_report.action_taken: Updated Redis cache key format to sha256(tenant_context_id)+':'+principal_identity_id+':'+resource_target+':'+action in policy_engine cache layer; added unit test asserting Tenant A decision is never returned for Tenant B cache lookup
fix_report.expected_outcome: Cross-tenant cache collision eliminated; cache hit rate unaffected since tenant_context_id is stable per request context

reference.title: Enterprise Authorization Policy Service
reference.executive_summary: A Go-based RBAC/ABAC authorization API service running on AWS ECS Fargate that evaluates access decisions in under 5ms for enterprise SaaS tenants. Policy rules, role assignments, and permission scopes are stored in PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security enforcing per-tenant boundaries. Redis 7 memoizes evaluation decisions with tenant-scoped cache keys. An append-only audit_events table feeds nightly compliance reports shipped to S3 for SOC 2 and ISO 27001 evidence packages.
reference.architecture_overview: Request arrives at ALB with a tenant-signed JWT. Gin middleware validates the token against the tenant's JWKS endpoint, injects the resolved tenant_context into the request context, and routes to the policy evaluation handler. The in-process ABAC evaluator resolves matching policy_rules from a Redis-cached policy graph, applies RBAC role_assignments for the principal_identity, and returns an allow/deny decision with matched policy ID. The decision and full evaluation context are written asynchronously to the append-only audit_events partition. Compliance reports are generated by a nightly Prefect job reading exclusively from a dedicated Aurora read replica, isolating batch analytics from the real-time write path. Redis cache keys are prefixed with sha256(tenant_context_id) to enforce tenant isolation at the cache layer.
reference.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, custom ABAC evaluation engine, go-jose for JWT/JWKS. Data: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 (primary + read replica) with PgBouncer, Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster (3 shards). Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (us-east-1a/1c/1d), ALB, ECR, S3 for compliance archives, Secrets Manager for JWKS signing keys. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with golangci-lint, go test -race, k6 load tests. Observability: Datadog APM with evaluation trace spans.
reference.functional_feature_map: Policy Evaluation Engine: accepts POST /v1/evaluate with (principal, resource, action, context attributes), resolves matching ABAC policies for the caller's tenant_context, returns allow/deny with policy_matched_id and evaluation_trace. Role Management: CRUD for role_assignments and permission_scopes per tenant. Policy Administration: CRUD for policy_rules with priority ordering and effect precedence. Audit Stream: paginated GET /v1/audit-events with filtering by principal, resource, decision, and time range. Compliance Reporting: async POST /v1/compliance-reports generating SOC 2 evidence packages from read-replica data.
reference.system_components: ALB with WAF (rate limiting 1000 RPM per tenant); ECS Fargate cluster (2-10 tasks, auto-scale on p95 eval latency); Aurora PostgreSQL 16 primary (evaluation writes, policy reads) + read replica (compliance report queries); PgBouncer sidecar (50 connections per Fargate task); Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster (policy decision cache, 60s TTL, tenant-scoped keys); S3 bucket (compliance report archives with SSE-KMS); Secrets Manager (per-tenant JWKS signing keys with 24h rotation).
reference.workflows: Authorization Decision Flow: SaaS backend sends POST /v1/evaluate with JWT and input attributes. Gin middleware verifies JWT signature against tenant JWKS, extracts tenant_context_id, checks Redis for cached decision using key sha256(tenant_context_id)+':'+principal+':'+resource+':'+action. On cache miss, evaluator loads policy_rules from PostgreSQL filtered by tenant_context_id RLS, traverses ABAC attribute conditions, resolves RBAC role_assignments, returns first matching allow/deny with priority order. Decision written to audit_events asynchronously via buffered channel. Response returned in < 5ms P99. Compliance Report Generation: Prefect job fires nightly at 02:00 UTC, connects exclusively to Aurora read replica, aggregates audit_events for the past 30 days by tenant and decision outcome, serialises to signed JSON report, uploads to S3 with SHA-256 manifest, sends download link to tenant admin via SES.
reference.data_model: policy_rules (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, rule_type ENUM rbac/abac, subject_expr JSONB, resource_expr JSONB, action_expr TEXT[], effect ENUM allow/deny NOT NULL, priority INT, created_by UUID, deleted_at TIMESTAMP). role_assignments (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, principal_identity_id UUID FK NOT NULL, role_name TEXT, permission_scopes UUID[] FK, granted_by UUID, expires_at TIMESTAMP). permission_scopes (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, scope_name TEXT, actions TEXT[]). audit_events (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, principal_identity_id UUID, resource_target TEXT, action TEXT, decision ENUM allow/deny, policy_matched_id UUID, evaluation_ms INT, evaluated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, CONSTRAINT no_update CHECK(true)) PARTITIONED BY RANGE (evaluated_at). tenant_contexts (id UUID PK, jwks_url TEXT, encryption_key_arn TEXT, tier TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). principal_identities (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, external_sub TEXT, attribute_claims JSONB, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ). resource_targets (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, resource_type TEXT, resource_path TEXT).
reference.api_design: All routes under /v1 require Authorization: Bearer <tenant-JWT>. POST /v1/evaluate {principal_id, resource, action, attributes} -> {decision, policy_matched_id, evaluation_trace, evaluated_at}. GET /v1/policies?page=1&limit=50 -> paginated policy_rules. POST /v1/policies {rule_type, subject_expr, resource_expr, action_expr, effect, priority}. DELETE /v1/policies/:id (soft-delete). GET /v1/roles -> role_assignments for caller's tenant. POST /v1/role-assignments {principal_identity_id, role_name, expires_at}. GET /v1/audit-events?principal=&resource=&decision=&from=&to= (read replica). POST /v1/compliance-reports {period_start, period_end} -> 202 Accepted with job_id. Rate limit: 2000 RPM per tenant enforced at ALB WAF.
reference.security_and_compliance: SOC 2 CC6.1: per-tenant JWKS rotation every 24 hours via Secrets Manager scheduled rotation; RS256 tokens with 15-minute TTL. CC6.6: PostgreSQL RLS policy `(tenant_context_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id')::uuid)` on all tables; Fargate task role has no cross-tenant DB access. CC7.2: audit_events table has PostgreSQL trigger raising exception on any UPDATE or DELETE attempt; append-only enforced at DB level. CC9.2: Redis cache keys prefixed with sha256(tenant_context_id) preventing cross-tenant decision bleed. ISO 27001 A.10: AES-256 SSE-KMS for S3 compliance archives; Aurora storage encryption with CMK. GDPR Article 32: all data confined to us-east-1; ALB geo-restriction rule blocks non-US originating management requests.
reference.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1a/1c/1d with 2 minimum tasks scaling to 10 on p95 evaluation latency > 3ms. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ primary (evaluation writes and policy reads) plus dedicated read replica (compliance report queries only, connection string COMPLIANCE_DB_READER_URL injected at task definition level). Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster mode with 3 shards and in-transit encryption. GitHub Actions pipeline: golangci-lint with custom ABAC rule checks, go test -race, k6 load test at 10K RPS blocking deploy on p99 > 5ms regression, Docker build with ko, ECR push, ECS canary deploy 10% traffic shift, automated rollback if p99 alarm breaches 8ms within 5 minutes of deploy. Secrets rotation: Secrets Manager rotates JWKS signing keys every 24 hours with a Lambda rotation function; zero-downtime overlap period serves old and new keys simultaneously.
reference.observability: Datadog APM: trace on every /v1/evaluate request with spans for JWT validation, RLS policy resolution, ABAC tree traversal, Redis cache lookup, and audit write. Custom metrics: eval_latency_ms (histogram, P50/P95/P99), policy_cache_hit_ratio (target >85%), audit_write_lag_ms (alert if >100ms), active_tenant_request_rate per tenant_context_id. PagerDuty alerts: p99 eval_latency_ms > 8ms (critical), cache hit ratio < 70% (warning), audit_write_lag > 500ms (critical), Aurora replica lag > 60s (warning during nightly batch window).
reference.cost_and_scaling: Baseline: 2 Fargate tasks (0.5 vCPU, 1GB) + Aurora db.r6g.large + Redis cache.r6g.large cluster = ~$680/month. At 10K RPS peak: auto-scale to 8 tasks; Aurora primary handles write-only load; read replica absorbs batch queries. Reserved Fargate baseline saves 30%. Redis node right-sizing after 30-day cache hit rate analysis.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (6 weeks): Core policy evaluation engine (RBAC path), JWT/JWKS auth middleware, PostgreSQL schema with RLS, audit_events append-only table, POST /v1/evaluate endpoint, GitHub Actions CI with k6 load gate. Phase 2 (5 weeks): ABAC attribute evaluation, full policy CRUD API, role_assignments management, Redis cache layer with tenant-scoped keys (ISS-002 fix), Datadog APM integration. Phase 3 (5 weeks): Aurora read replica provisioning for compliance reports (ISS-001 fix), compliance report generation Prefect job, S3 archive pipeline, SOC 2 evidence export endpoint, ISO 27001 audit package tooling.
reference.development_guidelines: Go modules with explicit dependency pinning. golangci-lint with staticcheck and gosec enabled in CI. All cache key construction centralised in pkg/cache/keys.go; key format changes require PR review from security team. ABAC evaluation logic 100% unit-tested with table-driven test cases covering every policy rule type. k6 load test must pass P99 <= 5ms gate before any deploy to production. ADRs required for changes to RLS policies or audit_events table schema.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ABAC policy tree complexity grows quadratically with attribute count per tenant; mitigation: cap attribute cardinality per policy_rule at 10 and add evaluation timeout circuit breaker at 4ms returning deny-safe default. Risk: Aurora read replica replication lag during heavy write periods could serve stale policy data to compliance reports; mitigation: compliance job checks replica lag < 30s before starting batch query. Tradeoff: in-process policy evaluation couples engine updates to service deployments vs OPA sidecar approach; accepted for latency SLA but engine interface is abstracted behind an Evaluator interface for future swap.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected Aurora read replica over separate RDS instance for compliance report isolation to reuse existing PostgreSQL schema and RLS policies. Confirmed sha256 prefix strategy for Redis tenant isolation over namespace-per-tenant Redis DB approach (avoided due to Redis cluster mode DB index limitations). Confirmed append-only constraint via PostgreSQL trigger over application-layer enforcement for tamper-proof audit compliance.
