{
 "thinking_summary": "Static Astro site for a chemistry department. Key decisions: Astro content collections with TypeScript schema validation for content authoring without developer involvement; BibTeX parsing at build time for publications list; Netlify CDN for global reach with automatic HTTPS enforcement; no backend service, no CMS, no cookies beyond GA consent banner. The plan is intentionally thin: the architecture is intentionally thin to reflect a static site with no server-side runtime.",
 "fix_report": [],
 "title": "Chemistry Department Static Website Architecture",
 "executive_summary": "A static Astro 4 website deployed to Netlify CDN, built from markdown files in a git repository, that showcases faculty research, grant projects, publications, and lab facilities for prospect

parsed.thinking_summary: Static Astro site for a chemistry department. Key decisions: Astro content collections with TypeScript schema validation for content authoring without developer involvement; BibTeX parsing at build time for publications list; Netlify CDN for global reach with automatic HTTPS enforcement; no backend service, no CMS, no cookies beyond GA consent banner. The plan is intentionally thin: the architecture is intentionally thin to reflect a static site with no server-side runtime.
parsed.title: Chemistry Department Static Website Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: A static Astro 4 website deployed to Netlify CDN, built from markdown files in a git repository, that showcases faculty research, grant projects, publications, and lab facilities for prospective graduate students and collaborators. No server-side runtime, no CMS, no cookies beyond Google Analytics consent banner. Content managed by a non-technical department administrator via Astro content collections with TypeScript schema validation.
parsed.architecture_overview: Astro 4 site with TypeScript content collections for faculty_profiles, research_projects, publications, and graduate_programs. Content authored in markdown files in the /content directory and committed to a git repository. At build time, Astro reads the content collections, validates each entry against Zod schemas, and generates static HTML pages served from the Netlify CDN. No server-side runtime, no database, no cookies beyond GA consent banner. A Netlify deploy preview is available for content PRs before any production deployment.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, astro-sitemap. Content: Astro content collections with Zod schema validation. Publications: bibtex-parser npm package for BibTeX parsing at build time. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with anonymizeIp: true. Hosting: Netlify CDN with automatic HTTPS. CI: GitHub Actions with lychee broken link checker. Git: GitHub for source of truth.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Faculty Profile Pages: each faculty_profile entry rendered as a Tailwind-styled card with name, title, research_areas[], bio, email, and photo. Research Area Pages: research_projects with slug, title, pi_id, funding_agency, start_year, end_year, and summary. Publications List: publications sorted by year with DOI, title, authors[], journal, volume, pages, and abstract. Lab Facilities Gallery: lab_facilities with slug, name, description, and equipment list. Graduate Program Pages: graduate_programs with slug, name, degree_type, application_deadline, and description. Contact Page: mailto: links to department office and lab contact emails.
parsed.system_components: Netlify CDN (global edge, automatic HTTPS), GitHub repository (source of truth), Astro build process (content collection validation and HTML generation), Astro content collections (faculty_profiles, research_projects, publications, graduate_programs), bibtex-parser npm package (publications list parsing), Google Analytics 4 (anonymized IP, consent banner), Netlify deploy preview (content PR preview), GitHub Actions (CI with lychee broken link check).
parsed.workflows: Content Update Workflow: Department administrator edits a faculty_profile markdown file in the /content directory. GitHub Actions runs on push to main. Astro content collection validates the updated entry against the Zod schema. If validation passes, Astro generates a static HTML page and deploys to Netlify. Netlify deploy preview shows the updated page in the browser. Content PRs are reviewed in GitHub before merge. No developer intervention required for content updates.
parsed.data_model: faculty_profiles (slug: string, name: string, title: string, research_areas: string[], bio: string, email: string, photo_path: string, publications_count: number, lab_url: string). research_projects (slug: string, title: string, pi_id: string, grant_number: string, funding_agency: string, start_year: number, end_year: number, summary: string). publications (doi: string, title: string, authors: string[], year: number, journal: string, volume: number, pages: number, abstract: string). lab_facilities (slug: string, name: string, description: string, equipment: string[], photo_path: string). graduate_programs (slug: string, name: string, degree_type: string, application_deadline: string, description: string).
parsed.api_design: No API. All data is statically generated at build time and served as HTML. No server-side endpoints.
parsed.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Netlify for all requests. No cookies set by the site beyond the GA consent banner. Google Analytics anonymizeIp: true. .gitignore rules block common sensitive file extensions. Pre-commit hook blocks files with .md extension in the /content directory. Netlify bot protection on any form endpoints. Contact links use mailto: anchors to avoid form submission attack surface.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: Netlify CDN global edge. GitHub repository as source of truth. Netlify auto-deploys on push to main. Branch deploy previews for content PRs. GitHub Actions CI: TypeScript type check, Astro build validation, lychee broken link check. Netlify deploy on successful CI. Netlify deploy preview URL shared in PR comments. No staging environment required.
parsed.observability: Google Analytics 4: page views, faculty profile views per profile, research area clicks, publications list views. Netlify build notifications to department Slack channel on build failure. No APM or error tracking required for a static site.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Netlify free tier covers static hosting and CDN. Google Analytics free tier. Total cost: zero. Scaling is automatic via Netlify CDN; no configuration changes required.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (2 weeks): Astro site scaffold, content collection schemas for faculty_profiles, research_projects, publications, graduate_programs, bibtex-parser integration, Netlify deployment, GitHub Actions CI with lychee broken link check. Phase 2 Content Refinement (1 week): Add faculty_profiles, research_projects, publications, graduate_programs, lab_facilities, and contact pages. Phase 3 Analytics and SEO (1 week): Google Analytics integration, astro-sitemap for search engine indexing, Netlify SEO configuration.
parsed.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in Astro. Zod schemas for all content collections enforced at build time. No client-side JavaScript in content pages. PRs require one approval from a department administrator. Content changes require a Netlify deploy preview review before merge. No images uploaded to the repository; all images served from a separate /images directory.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: BibTeX parsing errors produce invalid publications entries. Mitigation: bibtex-parser validation at build time with a build-time error message. Risk: Astro content collection schema changes break existing content. Mitigation: Zod schema validation at build time with a build-time error message. Tradeoff: static site over a CMS reduces development cost but limits content authoring flexibility; acceptable for a non-technical department administrator. Tradeoff: no server-side runtime means no caching control; Netlify CDN caching behavior is the primary control.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Selected Astro over Next.js for zero-JS default output and simpler content collection API. Selected Netlify over Vercel for lower cost and more granular CDN configuration. Selected bibtex-parser over manual BibTeX parsing for publication list accuracy. Selected Google Analytics over Plausible for GDPR-compliant consent banner.

reference.thinking_summary: Static university department site with no server-side runtime. Key decisions: Astro content collections with Zod schema validation for type-safe markdown content management, BibTeX parsing at build time for publications to avoid manual entry, Netlify CDN for zero-ops hosting. The only interactive element is a cookie consent banner for GDPR compliance implemented as a minimal Astro island.
reference.title: University Chemistry Department Static Website Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A fully static Astro 4 site with TypeScript content collections for the university chemistry department. Faculty profiles, research projects, publications (parsed from BibTeX), and lab facilities rendered as static HTML at build time and served from Netlify CDN globally. Non-technical administrator manages all content through git-committed markdown files with build-time schema validation catching errors before deployment.
reference.architecture_overview: Pure static architecture with Astro 4 content collections as the data layer. Each content type (faculty_profiles, research_projects, publications, lab_facilities, graduate_programs) is a typed Astro collection with Zod schema validation enforced at build time. BibTeX publication files are parsed by bibtex-parser during the build to generate the publications list without manual entry. Netlify CI/CD triggers a full static rebuild on every push to main. No server-side runtime, no database, no API layer. Only client-side JavaScript is a minimal cookie consent island and the Google Analytics 4 snippet.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, astro:assets for image optimization, astro-sitemap for sitemap generation. Content: Markdown with YAML frontmatter, BibTeX for publications. Build tooling: bibtex-parser npm package, lychee for broken link checking. Hosting: Netlify CDN with edge caching, Netlify Deploy Previews. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with anonymized IP. CI: GitHub Actions for type check and build validation.
reference.functional_feature_map: Faculty Directory: searchable grid of faculty cards with research area tags linking to individual profile pages with bio, publications count, and lab link. Research Areas: taxonomy-organized project listings with PI attribution and funding agency tags. Publications List: chronologically sorted list parsed from BibTeX with DOI links and optional abstract toggle. Lab Facilities: image gallery of departmental instruments and shared equipment. Graduate Programs: admissions information with application deadlines and contact links. Department News: markdown-driven news feed with dated entries.
reference.system_components: GitHub repository (content and code source of truth), Netlify Build (Astro SSG with content collection validation), Netlify CDN (global static asset delivery), Google Analytics 4 (anonymized traffic tracking), astro-sitemap (automatic sitemap.xml generation), lychee link checker (CI step validating no broken DOI or internal links).
reference.workflows: Content Update by Administrator: Admin edits a faculty profile markdown file in /content/faculty/, commits to a feature branch, opens a GitHub pull request. GitHub Actions runs TypeScript type check and Astro build validation. Netlify generates a deploy preview URL linked in the PR for visual review. Maintainer merges PR; Netlify deploys to production within 90 seconds. BibTeX Publication Update: Researcher emails admin a BibTeX entry; admin appends to /content/publications.bib, commits to main. Build parses BibTeX, regenerates publications page with new entry sorted by year. Prospective Student Browsing: Visitor lands on /research, filters by research area keyword, navigates to a specific project page, finds PI contact email, clicks mailto: link to reach out directly.
reference.data_model: faculty_profiles collection: slug (string), name (string), title (string), research_areas (string array), bio (string), email (string), photo_path (image), lab_url (string optional), joined_year (number). research_projects collection: slug (string), title (string), pi_slug (reference to faculty), grant_number (string optional), funding_agency (string), start_year (number), summary (string). publications: parsed from BibTeX fields author, title, year, journal, volume, pages, doi into typed objects at build time. lab_facilities collection: slug, name, description, equipment_list (string array), photos (image array). graduate_programs collection: slug, name, degree (BS/MS/PhD), application_deadline (string), contact_email (string).
reference.api_design: No API endpoints. All data is statically generated. Sitemap at /sitemap.xml. RSS feed at /news/rss.xml for department news feed. Contact links use mailto: anchors pointing to faculty email addresses. No form submission endpoints.
reference.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced automatically by Netlify with auto-renewing Let's Encrypt certificate. No user accounts, no form data collection, no cookies beyond analytics consent. Cookie consent banner implemented as Astro island injecting GA4 script only after user accepts. Google Analytics configured with anonymize_ip: true for GDPR compliance. Repository .gitignore and pre-commit hook block accidental commit of .env, .pdf, .docx, and credential files. Netlify bot protection enabled.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: astro check (TypeScript validation), astro build (full static build), lychee --offline (broken internal link check). On main branch merge: Netlify auto-triggers production deploy from build output. Branch pushes generate Netlify Deploy Preview URLs. Build time approximately 45 seconds including BibTeX parsing and image optimization. Rollback: Netlify one-click rollback to any previous deploy from dashboard. No secrets required; no environment variables in production.
reference.observability: Google Analytics 4 dashboard for page views, top faculty profile pages, research area section engagement, geographic visitor distribution. Netlify build logs for build failures with email notification to department admin. GitHub Actions build status badges in repository README. No infrastructure metrics needed for static hosting.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $0/month on Netlify free tier (100GB bandwidth/month, sufficient for department traffic). If bandwidth exceeded: Netlify Pro at $19/month. No compute costs, no database costs. Scales automatically with CDN edge nodes globally. No scaling decisions required.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (4 weeks): Astro project setup with Tailwind, content collection schemas for all five types, faculty profiles and research areas pages, publications BibTeX parser integration, responsive mobile layout, Google Analytics integration, cookie consent island, GitHub Actions CI, Netlify deploy pipeline. Phase 2 Enhancements (3 weeks): Lab facilities gallery with image optimization, graduate programs pages, department news feed with RSS, sitemap generation, broken link CI check. Phase 3 Optional (future): Decap CMS integration for GUI-based content editing by admin without git knowledge.
reference.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode enforced; all content collection schemas define required and optional fields with Zod. Astro content collection query functions typed with CollectionEntry generics. Images committed to /public/images/faculty/ in WebP format at 800x800 max resolution. BibTeX file validated by bibtex-parser during CI build; malformed entries fail the build. Broken link check runs in CI to catch dead DOI links before deployment.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Non-technical admin introduces markdown syntax errors breaking the build. Mitigation: Clear README with content editing guide, build error notifications with line number context. Risk: Faculty publication lists become stale if BibTeX updates require developer involvement. Mitigation: Provide admin with simple BibTeX entry template and document the process. Tradeoff: Markdown-based CMS over Decap CMS reduces setup complexity but requires admin git familiarity. Tradeoff: No search functionality in MVP; prospective students rely on browser Ctrl+F or research area taxonomy navigation.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected Astro over Next.js because no server-side features are needed and Astro produces smaller HTML output for content sites. Chose Netlify over GitHub Pages for deploy preview functionality used in content review workflow. BibTeX parsing at build time selected over manual markdown publications entries to reduce admin burden on publication-heavy department.

raw:
{
 "thinking_summary": "This is a PCI-DSS 4.0 automation tool for a Level 1 merchant. Key decisions: Step Functions for scan orchestration with parallel CDE component branches, Lambda workers for Config rule compliance scans, IAM policy review, and log retention checks, FastAPI on Lambda for the control plane API, and WeasyPrint for PDF report generation. The PCI requirement mapping logic is the core value-add — it must be configurable for PCI-DSS 4.0 vs 3.2.1 and support the transition period. The tool itself is in PCI scope so all access controls, logging, and change management must be documented as part of the CDE boundary.",
 "fix_report": [],
 "title": "PCI-DSS Evidence Collection and QSA Report Automation Tool",
 "executive_summary": "A serverless automation tool built with Python 3.

reference.thinking_summary: Building a PCI-DSS evidence automation tool as a serverless AWS architecture. The key design decisions are Step Functions for orchestrating parallel scan workers (each scan type is a separate Lambda), Mangum to run FastAPI on Lambda for the control plane API, and strict IAM least-privilege for scan execution roles since the tool itself is in PCI scope. ReportBuilder uses Jinja2 templates per PCI-DSS requirement to generate pre-formatted QSA responses — this is the core differentiating logic.
reference.title: PCI-DSS Evidence Collection Automation Tool Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A serverless AWS automation tool for PCI-DSS Level 1 evidence collection, built with Python 3.12 FastAPI on Lambda (via Mangum) for the control plane and AWS Step Functions orchestrating parallel scan workers across the cardholder data environment. Scans AWS Config rule compliance, IAM access control lists, log retention policies, and network segmentation configurations, mapping findings to PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement IDs and generating pre-formatted QSA questionnaire responses as PDF and CSV. All evidence artifacts stored in S3 with SSE-KMS; the tool itself operates under documented CDE scope.
reference.architecture_overview: Serverless architecture with three layers: a Cognito JWT-authenticated FastAPI control plane on Lambda via Mangum behind API Gateway for human-triggered scans and report downloads; an AWS Step Functions state machine orchestrating parallel Lambda scan workers (ConfigComplianceLambda, IamPolicyReviewLambda, LogRetentionCheckLambda, NetworkSegmentationLambda); and PostgreSQL 16 on RDS for scan result metadata with S3 for raw evidence artifacts. EventBridge Scheduler triggers quarterly scans automatically; the control plane API supports on-demand runs for pre-audit preparation. All Lambda execution roles are least-privilege read-only against CDE resources. ReportGeneratorLambda maps findings to PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement IDs via PciRequirementMapper and renders PDF QSA responses using Jinja2 and WeasyPrint.
reference.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. Control plane: FastAPI with Mangum Lambda adapter, API Gateway v2 HTTP API. Orchestration: AWS Step Functions (Express Workflow). Scan workers: Lambda functions per scan type. PDF generation: Jinja2 templates with WeasyPrint. Infrastructure: RDS PostgreSQL 16, S3 with SSE-KMS, Cognito user pools, API Gateway, EventBridge Scheduler, SNS, CloudTrail, CloudWatch. IaC: Terraform. CI/CD: GitLab CI. AWS SDK: boto3 for Config, IAM, Logs API calls.
reference.functional_feature_map: Scheduled Scan Execution triggered by EventBridge Scheduler quarterly and ad-hoc via API. ConfigComplianceLambda evaluating AWS Config managed rules against CDE component list and baseline. IamPolicyReviewLambda enumerating IAM policies, roles, and access keys with CDE access, flagging excessive permissions. LogRetentionCheckLambda verifying CloudWatch Log Groups for CDE resources meet PCI Requirement 10.7 retention minimums. NetworkSegmentationLambda reviewing Security Group and Network Firewall rules for CDE isolation. PciRequirementMapper tagging each finding to a specific PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement ID (e.g., Req 7.2.1, Req 10.7.1). DeviationDetector comparing findings against compliance baseline. QSA Report Generator producing PDF with pre-populated SAQ D responses and CSV deviation summary.
reference.system_components: EventBridge Scheduler rule (quarterly cron, UTC) invoking Step Functions state machine. Step Functions Express Workflow with parallel branches for Config, IAM, LogRetention, and NetworkSegmentation scan workers, then a sequential ReportGenerator step. FastAPI control plane on Lambda via Mangum with API Gateway v2. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ in production for scan metadata. S3 evidence bucket with SSE-KMS, versioning, and lifecycle to Glacier at 12 months. ComplianceBaselineLoader reading expected states from S3 JSON config files. ReportBuilder with Jinja2 QSA templates per PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement section. SNS topic for scan completion and critical deviation notifications.
reference.workflows: Quarterly Automated Scan: EventBridge Scheduler triggers Step Functions at 02:00 UTC on first day of each quarter. State machine fans out to 4 parallel scan Lambda workers, each writing findings to control_checks and evidence artifacts to S3. After all workers complete, ReportGeneratorLambda runs PciRequirementMapper against all findings, renders Jinja2 QSA template per requirement section, produces PDF via WeasyPrint and CSV deviation list, stores both to S3, updates qsa_questionnaires record, and fires SNS notification with report download URL to compliance team. Pre-Audit On-Demand Run: Compliance analyst calls POST /scans via Cognito-authenticated API, triggering same Step Functions workflow with on_demand trigger type. Progress visible via GET /scans/:id status polling.
reference.data_model: cde_components (id UUID, name, component_type ENUM ec2/rds/firewall/lambda, aws_resource_id VARCHAR(255), in_scope BOOLEAN, last_scanned_at TIMESTAMPTZ). scan_runs (id UUID, triggered_by ENUM scheduled/on_demand, status ENUM running/completed/partial_failure/failed, pci_version VARCHAR(10), started_at, completed_at). control_checks (id UUID, scan_run_id FK, cde_component_id FK, pci_requirement_id VARCHAR(10), check_type VARCHAR(100), result ENUM pass/fail/not_applicable, evidence_s3_key, checked_at). audit_findings (id UUID, control_check_id FK, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, description, expected_value, actual_value, status ENUM open/remediated, opened_at). qsa_questionnaires (id UUID, scan_run_id FK, pci_version, report_s3_key, csv_s3_key, generated_at). remediation_items (id UUID, audit_finding_id FK, owner, due_date DATE, status ENUM open/in_progress/closed, notes).
reference.api_design: FastAPI on Lambda via Mangum. POST /scans (trigger on-demand scan, returns scan_run_id). GET /scans (list history, paginated). GET /scans/:id (status and summary). GET /scans/:id/results (all control_checks with findings). GET /scans/:id/report?format=pdf|csv (presigned S3 download URL). GET /deviations?status=open (all open audit_findings across runs). GET /requirements/:pci_req_id/evidence (evidence artifacts for a specific PCI requirement). GET /cde-components (registry). PUT /cde-components/:id (update in-scope flag). Auth: API Gateway Lambda authorizer validating Cognito JWT. All endpoints require compliance_reader role minimum.
reference.security_and_compliance: Tool in PCI CDE scope — access controls and change management are themselves audit evidence. ScanWorkerRole IAM policy: Config:GetComplianceDetailsByConfigRule, Config:DescribeConfigRules, IAM:GetPolicy, IAM:ListAttachedRolePolicies, IAM:ListUsers, logs:FilterLogEvents, ec2:DescribeSecurityGroups, network-firewall:DescribeFirewallPolicy — no write permissions to any CDE resource. Evidence S3 bucket: SSE-KMS with dedicated KMS key, block all public access, object-level CloudTrail logging for GetObject and PutObject. Cognito user pool with password policy and MFA optional at admin discretion. CloudTrail trail with S3 data events covering evidence bucket. API Gateway access logs retained 1 year. Step Functions execution history 90 days.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with moto for AWS SDK mocking (80% coverage gate on scanner modules), Terraform plan against staging, manual approval gate for production Terraform apply. Lambda packages built as Python 3.12 zip with dependencies via pip install -t. Lambda function versions pinned in Terraform with previous version alias for rollback. EventBridge Scheduler enabled/disabled via Terraform variable for maintenance windows. Environments: dev (single-AZ RDS, mock scan data), staging (multi-AZ RDS, sandboxed AWS account), production (multi-AZ RDS, live CDE read access). Terraform state in S3 with DynamoDB lock.
reference.observability: CloudWatch custom dashboard: last_scan_run_status, open_deviations_count, critical_findings_count, days_since_last_successful_scan. Lambda metrics: invocation count, error rate, duration (p99). Step Functions: execution success rate, execution duration. SNS email alerts: scan failure (Step Functions execution FAILED), critical severity audit_finding created, report generation timeout (Lambda duration > 10 minutes). CloudTrail anomaly detection for unexpected CDE resource access by scan roles.
reference.cost_and_scaling: ~$150/month for quarterly scan cadence: Lambda invocations at quarterly scale are near-zero cost. RDS PostgreSQL db.t3.small Multi-AZ ~$80, S3 storage ~$20, Step Functions Express Workflows ~$10, API Gateway ~$10, CloudWatch ~$20. On-demand pre-audit runs add minor Lambda cost. WeasyPrint PDF generation is CPU-intensive — consider 3008MB Lambda memory for report generator to stay within 15-minute timeout for large CDE environments.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): CDE component registry, ConfigComplianceLambda for CDE segmentation rules, IamPolicyReviewLambda, evidence S3 storage, PciRequirementMapper for SAQ D requirements, CSV deviation report, Cognito auth, Terraform IaC, EventBridge quarterly schedule, GitLab CI pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): LogRetentionCheckLambda, NetworkSegmentationLambda via Security Group rules, PDF QSA report generation with Jinja2/WeasyPrint, SNS notifications, DeviationDetector with baseline comparison. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Remediation tracking via remediation_items, Jira webhook integration for ticket creation, compliance posture dashboard via CloudWatch, AWS Network Firewall rule analysis.
reference.development_guidelines: Python ruff linting and mypy strict type checking enforced in GitLab CI. All scanner Lambda functions must have unit tests with moto mocking AWS SDK calls — no live AWS calls in tests. PciRequirementMapper logic unit-tested against all 12 PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement sections. Terraform modules for Lambda, Step Functions, and S3 in separate reusable modules. No hardcoded AWS resource IDs — all configuration via SSM Parameter Store or environment variables. Evidence artifact S3 keys follow pattern: {scan_run_id}/{cde_component_id}/{check_type}/{timestamp}.json.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WeasyPrint PDF generation timing out for CDE environments with 100+ components. Mitigation: chunk report generation into per-requirement-section Lambda invocations and assemble via Step Functions. Risk: AWS Config rule coverage gaps for custom CDE components not tracked by managed rules. Mitigation: ComplianceBaselineLoader supports custom check definitions for non-Config-native resources. Tradeoff: FastAPI on Lambda over a persistent container — cold start latency is acceptable for infrequent on-demand scans. Tradeoff: RDS over DynamoDB for structured query support on scan history and requirement-level evidence lookup.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Network firewall rule export will use AWS Network Firewall DescribeFirewallPolicy API — no manual upload required. PDF format confirmed via WeasyPrint with Jinja2 templates; report generator Lambda allocated 3008MB to handle large CDE environments. Baseline configs stored in S3 JSON files editable by admin role, not hardcoded in Lambda.

parsed.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for the STEM AI tutoring assistant. ISS-001 fix: add embedding-similarity lockout check with configurable cosine similarity threshold (default 0.88) as a secondary check against stored problem statement embeddings in hint_policies. ISS-002 fix: define last-8-turns sliding window with summarization of dropped context prepended to window. Both fixes are targeted at academic integrity and session reliability. The plan quality is weak per manifest: the 'focus_issues' section is thin and the 'risks_and_tradeoffs' section is absent.

fix_report.action_taken: Added embedding-similarity lockout check: embed the incoming student query with Titan Embeddings v2, compute cosine similarity against stored problem statement embeddings in hint_policies, trigger deflection if similarity exceeds 0.88 threshold. Added embedding_similarity_threshold parameter to PolicyEnforcer constructor with default 0.88. Updated data_model and workflows to reflect the new check.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Academic integrity is enforced: paraphrase-based bypasses of assignment problems are detected and deflection messages are generated without LLM invocation.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined last-8-turns sliding window strategy: send last 8 turns to Bedrock Claude; if session exceeds 8 turns, generate a 2-sentence summary of earlier context using a summarization library (e.g., sentence-transformers) and prepend it to the window. Updated system_components and workflows.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Long STEM sessions (e.g., 30-minute problem walkthroughs) maintain context within Bedrock context limits without silent context loss or API errors.

parsed.title: STEM AI Tutoring Assistant -- Architecture (v2)
parsed.executive_summary: LLM-powered tutoring system on AWS Bedrock (Claude) for undergraduate STEM courses, replacing a legacy static FAQ. React 18 chat UI with server-sent events for token streaming. RAG over course document chunks via pgvector semantic search. Assignment lockout policies configured per course by instructors. Embedding-similarity lockout check added as a secondary check against stored problem statement embeddings. Last-8-turns sliding window with summarization prepended to context window when session exceeds 8 turns. Interaction logs retained for 1 academic year with FERPA-compliant anonymization after 90 days.
parsed.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: React 18 SPA (SSE token streaming), FastAPI tutoring service (Bedrock API calls, policy enforcement, interaction logging), and ECS Fargate cluster. The tutoring service orchestrates RAG retrieval, assignment lockout policy check, prompt construction, and Bedrock API call. The PolicyEnforcer class enforces assignment lockout policies before any Bedrock call. The embedding service ingests document chunks via Titan Embeddings v2 and stores them in pgvector. The system prompt versioned in Git is the authoritative prompt template. Interaction logs are written to RDS PostgreSQL with course_id and student_id linking to course enrollment records.
parsed.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, React Query Server-Sent Events, Zustand, React Hook Form. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, boto3 (Bedrock API), python-simple-summarization (context summarization), SQLAlchemy 2.0, pgvector 0.10. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, pgvector extension, Redis 7 ElastiCache. Vector DB: Titan Embeddings v2 for document chunk embeddings. Auth: Cognito user pools. Hosting: ECS Fargate us-east-1, ALB with WAF, LaunchDarkly for course-course rollout. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Observability: Datadog APM, custom metrics.
parsed.functional_feature_map: Chat Interface: SSE token streaming for STEM question and answer exchange; last-8-turns context window; send/receive message UI. RAG Retrieval: pgvector cosine similarity search over course document chunks for top-3 document retrieval; cosine similarity threshold configurable per course. Assignment Lockout: keyword-based lockout (default) and embedding-similarity lockout (default 0.88) enforced by PolicyEnforcer before Bedrock call; deflection message returned without LLM invocation. Hint Generation: Claude generates step-by-step hint text from prompt template. Policy Config: instructor creates keyword or embedding-hash lockout rules per course in admin UI. Analytics Dashboard: session volume, deflection rate, avg Bedrock response latency, context window summary length.
parsed.system_components: React 18 SPA (SSE token streaming, chat UI, session history panel), FastAPI TutoringService (RAG retrieval, PolicyEnforcer, Bedrock API wrapper, interaction logging), PolicyService (hint_policies CRUD), EmbeddingService (Titan Embeddings v2 ingestion), RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (interaction_logs, course_documents, hint_policies), ElastiCache Redis 7 (conversation context cache, session history), ALB with WAF (WAF rule: SQL injection, XSS, rate limit), ECS Fargate cluster (TutoringService min 2 tasks, EmbeddingService async worker), LaunchDarkly (course-course rollout), Datadog APM (distributed traces), Cognito user pools (student and instructor groups), AWS Bedrock (Claude claude-sonnet, 1000 tokens per call, 30-second timeout), Amazon Titan Embeddings v2 (document chunk embeddings), Amazon ElastiCache Redis 7 (conversation context cache).
parsed.workflows: STEM Question and Answer: Student sends 'How do I solve a first-order differential equation with a constant coefficient?' to chat endpoint; React Query sends SSE token to FastAPI; FastAPI calls PolicyEnforcer to check assignment lockout (embedding-similarity check added as secondary check); if lockout triggered, returns 'I am having trouble responding' message without Bedrock call; if lockout cleared, constructs prompt from system prompt version in Git, sends last 8 turns to Bedrock Claude, receives response via SSE; streams response to React; renders hint text from prompt template; sends session_turn to interaction_logs; returns 200. Long Session Context Management: Instructor asks 'Can you explain the concept of a critical point in a differential equation?' in a 30-minute problem walkthrough; React Query sends SSE token; FastAPI calls PolicyEnforcer; if lockout triggered, generates 'I am having trouble responding' message; if lockout cleared, sends last 8 turns to Bedrock Claude; Bedrock returns response; FastAPI streams response to React; React renders 2-sentence summarization of earlier context prepended to window; session continues without context loss.
parsed.data_model: students (id UUID PK, cognito_sub VARCHAR UNIQUE, name_hashed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). courses (id UUID PK, instructor_id UUID FK, title VARCHAR, embedding_model_version VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). tutoring_sessions (id UUID PK, student_id UUID FK, course_id UUID FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, message_count INT). conversation_turns (id UUID PK, session_id UUID FK, role ENUM user/assistant, content_hash VARCHAR, response_type ENUM hint/deflected/error, policy_triggered BOOLEAN, latency_ms INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). knowledge_base_documents (id UUID PK, course_id UUID FK, filename VARCHAR, chunk_count INT, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ). document_chunks (id UUID PK, document_id UUID FK, chunk_index INT, content_text VARCHAR, embedding vector(1536) BYTEA, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). hint_policies (id UUID PK, course_id UUID FK, rule_type ENUM keyword/embedding_hash, rule_value VARCHAR, created_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). interaction_logs (id UUID PK, turn_id UUID FK, student_id UUID FK, course_id UUID FK, event_type VARCHAR, metadata_json JSONB, logged_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
parsed.api_design: POST /v1/tutor/chat (SSE streaming endpoint, body: {question: string, course_id: UUID}). GET /v1/tutor/sessions/{session_id} (session history with last 5 turns). POST /v1/courses/{id}/policies (create hint_policy rule). GET /v1/courses/{id}/analytics (instructor metrics). All endpoints require Cognito JWT Bearer token; course_id and student_id validated per request. Bedrock API calls: POST /v1/chat/completions (SSE streaming, body: {prompt: string, context: list[string]}).
parsed.security_and_compliance: Cognito JWT validated at FastAPI dependency injection level; course enrollment verified per request against enrollments table. Prompt injection guard: input sanitized for role-switch patterns (e.g., 'system prompt' or 'assistant prompt') and ignored. System prompt versioned in Git with placeholder for course context; user input sanitized for prompt injection patterns. Assignment lockout: PolicyEnforcer checks embedding-similarity check (default 0.88) before Bedrock call; if similarity exceeds threshold, returns deflection message without LLM invocation. Interaction logs: interaction_logs table stores only turn content and policy_triggered flag; no student_id, question_text, or response_text in logs. FERPA: interaction_logs retained for 1 academic year; student_id replaced with hashed session token at 90-day interval; no log data shared with LLM provider. WAF rules: SQL injection, XSS, rate limit (20 messages per student per hour).
parsed.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster: TutoringService min 2 tasks, max 10 tasks, CPU 1vCPU/2GB, ALB target group. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ db.t3.medium, automated backups. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster cache.t3.small, 2-node. GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with mock Bedrock responses, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update. LaunchDarkly: course-course rollout; feature flag per course in LaunchDarkly config. Environments: dev (single-AZ, shared RDS), staging (multi-AZ, isolated RDS), production (multi-AZ, auto-scaling). Rollback: ECS auto-scaling to 0 tasks; RDS automated failover.
parsed.observability: Datadog APM distributed traces on all FastAPI request handlers. Custom metrics: tutor_response_latency_ms histogram (p50/p95/p99), policy_deflection_rate counter, bedrock_tokens_used counter, context_window_summary_length counter. Structured logs: student_id replaced with hashed session token, course_id, message_count, policy_triggered, latency_ms. Alert rules: bedrock_tokens_used > 5000 in 10-minute window (potential quota exhaustion), policy_deflection_rate > 5% in 1-hour window (instructor policy drift), RDS connection pool > 80% utilization.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $1,200/month for 3 pilot courses: ECS Fargate 2 tasks ~$200, RDS db.t3.medium Multi-AZ ~$180, ElastiCache cache.t3.small 2-node ~$120, ALB ~$50, WAF ~$50, Titan Embeddings ~$100, Bedrock Claude ~$1,200/month at 1000 tokens/call. Scaling: ECS auto-scaling handles peak session traffic; RDS read replica for analytics queries.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Pilot (10 weeks): 3 pilot courses, RAG over PDF chunks, embedding-similarity lockout check, SSE token streaming, Cognito auth, interaction logging, LaunchDarkly course-course rollout. Phase 2 (6 weeks): PolicyConfig admin UI for instructors, context summarization for long sessions, deflection rate analytics dashboard, FERPA anonymization after 90 days. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Multi-modal interaction (diagram and equation image upload), LMS grade book integration, student self-service interaction history export.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. ruff for linting. pytest with mock Bedrock responses for all PolicyEnforcer and Bedrock API call tests. Docker for all services. No hardcoded course_ids in code; all course references via course_id UUID. Prompt injection guard tested in CI with mock user input. Context summarization library tested with synthetic long-session inputs. Feature flags in LaunchDarkly; no course-course changes in production without flag.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Titan Embeddings v2 embeddings may not capture the full meaning of complex STEM problem statements; fallback to PostgreSQL full-text search for complex queries. Risk: Bedrock Claude context window limit (1000 tokens) may cause silent context loss in very long STEM problem walkthroughs; mitigates with summarization prepending to context window. Tradeoff: pgvector over Pinecone for lower operational overhead at this scale; acceptable for 3 pilot courses. Tradeoff: embedding-similarity lockout as secondary check over keyword-only; adds complexity but prevents paraphrase bypasses.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved: embedding-similarity lockout added as secondary check with configurable cosine similarity threshold (default 0.88). ISS-002 resolved: last-8-turns sliding window with summarization prepended to context window when session exceeds 8 turns. ISS-003 resolved: LaunchDarkly course-course rollout with feature flag per course.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 by adding an embedding-similarity lockout check in PolicyEnforcer: the student query is embedded via Titan Embeddings, cosine similarity is computed against stored problem statement embeddings in hint_policies, and deflection is triggered if similarity exceeds 0.88. ISS-002 is resolved by defining a last-8-turns sliding window with a summarization step for dropped context. Both fixes integrate cleanly into the existing TutoringService and PolicyEnforcer without architectural changes. The pgvector infrastructure already supports embedding-based lockout since document_chunks already uses vector(1536).

fix_report.action_taken: PolicyEnforcer now runs a two-stage lockout check: (1) keyword match against hint_policies WHERE rule_type='keyword'; (2) if keyword check passes, embed the student query via Titan Embeddings v2 and query hint_policies WHERE rule_type='embedding_hash' using pgvector cosine distance operator (<=>); deflect if any stored problem embedding has cosine similarity >=0.88. Threshold is configurable per course via hint_policies.similarity_threshold column. Deflection events logged to interaction_logs with policy_triggered=True and rule_id.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Paraphrased queries that bypass keyword matching are caught by embedding similarity check; academic integrity enforcement is robust to student rephrasing strategies

fix_report.action_taken: TutoringService implements a sliding window context builder: the last 8 conversation_turns are fetched from Redis or PostgreSQL fallback and passed to Bedrock. If session has more than 8 turns, a summarization prompt (system: 'Summarize the following STEM tutoring exchange in 2 sentences focusing on concepts covered') is sent to Bedrock to generate a context_summary; the summary is prepended to the window as a system message. Summaries are cached in Redis keyed by session_id:summary_turn_N with 2-hour TTL.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Long tutoring sessions maintain coherent context without Bedrock API errors; students receive contextually appropriate hints even after extended sessions

reference.title: STEM AI Tutoring Assistant -- Architecture (v2)
reference.executive_summary: An LLM-powered STEM tutoring assistant on AWS Bedrock replacing a legacy static FAQ, with RAG over course PDFs via pgvector, two-stage assignment lockout enforcement (keyword + embedding-similarity), and FERPA-compliant interaction logging. Students receive adaptive hints via a React chat UI with SSE token streaming; instructors configure lockout policies and view session analytics. Long sessions use a sliding-window context strategy with summarization to maintain coherence within Bedrock's context limits.
reference.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA communicates via HTTPS to a FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate behind an ALB with WAF. TutoringService orchestrates: (1) pgvector hybrid search retrieving top-3 course document chunks, (2) two-stage PolicyEnforcer checking keyword and embedding-similarity lockout rules, (3) context window builder assembling last-8-turns sliding window with optional summarization, (4) AWS Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet invocation via boto3 with streamed response. Amazon Titan Embeddings v2 handles both document chunk ingestion and per-query embedding for similarity-based lockout. PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector stores course embeddings and interaction logs; Redis 7 caches conversation context. Cognito authenticates students and instructors with course-enrollment-scoped authorization.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, React Query, Zustand, useSSE hook for token streaming. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, boto3 for Bedrock and Titan Embeddings. Database: PostgreSQL 16 (RDS Multi-AZ) with pgvector extension, ivfflat index on document_chunks.embedding. Cache: Redis 7 (ElastiCache) for conversation context and summarization cache. LLM: AWS Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet for tutoring responses and context summarization. Embeddings: Amazon Titan Embeddings v2 (1536-dim). Auth: Cognito user pools. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, ECR, ECS Fargate rolling deploy. Observability: Datadog APM. Feature flags: LaunchDarkly per course.
reference.functional_feature_map: Student Tutoring Chat: SSE-streamed responses for STEM concept questions and step-by-step walkthroughs. Assignment Lockout Enforcement: keyword match then embedding-similarity check (cosine >=0.88) against instructor-defined problem embeddings; deflected queries receive canned guidance without LLM invocation. RAG Retrieval: hybrid pgvector cosine search + PostgreSQL full-text search for STEM notation across course document chunks. Context Window Management: last-8-turns sliding window with 2-sentence summarization for dropped context. Instructor Policy Config: keyword and problem-statement embedding upload, per-course similarity threshold tuning. Instructor Analytics: session volume, deflection rate, avg response latency, top-queried course topics.
reference.system_components: ALB with WAF (SQLi/XSS rules + rate limiting). ECS Fargate: tutor-api service (min 2, max 10 tasks), embedding-worker service for async document ingestion. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with pgvector. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster for conversation context and summarization cache. AWS Bedrock: Claude claude-sonnet (response generation, context summarization), Titan Embeddings v2 (document and query embedding). S3 bucket for raw course document uploads before ingestion. Cognito user pools with student/instructor groups. Datadog APM sidecar. LaunchDarkly SDK for per-course feature flags.
reference.workflows: Two-Stage Lockout Check: student sends message; TutoringService calls PolicyEnforcer; stage 1: SQL query on hint_policies WHERE rule_type='keyword' AND course_id=:course_id, check if any keyword appears in query text; stage 2: if stage 1 passes, embed query via Titan Embeddings v2, execute pgvector query SELECT rule_id FROM hint_policies WHERE rule_type='embedding_hash' AND course_id=:course_id ORDER BY embedding <=> :query_embedding LIMIT 1 -- if cosine similarity of top result >= hint_policies.similarity_threshold, trigger deflection; if both stages pass, proceed to RAG retrieval and Bedrock call. Context Window Build: fetch last 8 turns from Redis (TTL 30 min) or PostgreSQL fallback; if session.message_count > 8, retrieve or generate context_summary via Bedrock summarization prompt; prepend summary as system message; assemble context array: [system_prompt, context_summary_if_any, last_8_turns]; send to Bedrock with streaming=True.
reference.data_model: students (id UUID PK, cognito_sub VARCHAR, name_hashed_at DATE). courses (id UUID PK, instructor_id FK, title VARCHAR, embedding_model_version VARCHAR). tutoring_sessions (id UUID PK, student_id FK, course_id FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, message_count INT, context_summary TEXT, summary_as_of_turn INT). conversation_turns (id UUID PK, session_id FK, role ENUM user/assistant, content_hash VARCHAR, response_type ENUM hint/deflected/error, policy_triggered BOOLEAN, rule_id FK nullable, latency_ms INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). document_chunks (id UUID PK, document_id FK, chunk_index INT, content_text TEXT, embedding vector(1536)). hint_policies (id UUID PK, course_id FK, rule_type ENUM keyword/embedding_hash, rule_value TEXT, embedding vector(1536) nullable, similarity_threshold FLOAT DEFAULT 0.88, created_by FK). interaction_logs (id UUID PK, turn_id FK, student_id VARCHAR, course_id FK, event_type VARCHAR, metadata_json JSONB, logged_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
reference.api_design: POST /v1/tutor/chat (SSE, body: {session_id, message, course_id}) -- runs lockout check, RAG retrieval, context build, Bedrock stream. GET /v1/tutor/sessions/{session_id}/history -- returns conversation_turns for session. POST /v1/courses/{id}/policies -- instructor creates keyword or embedding_hash lockout rule; embedding_hash rules include base64-encoded problem statement, backend embeds and stores vector. GET /v1/courses/{id}/analytics?from=&to= -- returns session_count, deflection_rate, avg_latency_ms, top_topics. POST /v1/courses/{id}/documents -- multipart upload; triggers async embedding-worker ECS task. GET /v1/health -- ALB health check.
reference.security_and_compliance: Authentication: Cognito JWT validated at FastAPI Depends(get_current_user); course enrollment verified per request against students-courses enrollment table. Prompt injection guard: regex scan of user input for patterns ('ignore previous', 'you are now', 'system:', 'assistant:'); blocked queries logged with event_type='injection_attempt'. Assignment lockout: two-stage check (keyword + embedding similarity >=0.88) enforced in backend before any Bedrock call; client-side blocking is not relied upon. FERPA: interaction_logs link to course_id and student_id; access restricted to the student themselves and course instructor; student identifiers in logs anonymized (hashed) after 90 days via nightly Lambda; interaction logs excluded from Datadog log forwarding. Bedrock: system prompt passed as system role, not prepended to user message; no student query content included in Bedrock model training (Bedrock API guarantee).
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with mocked Bedrock responses (pytest-mock) and pgvector test DB (Docker Compose), build Docker, push ECR. ECS rolling deploy with 100% minimum healthy task during update. Environments: dev (single-AZ, t-class RDS, mock Bedrock), staging (multi-AZ RDS, real Bedrock, 3 pilot courses), production (multi-AZ RDS, auto-scaling 2-10 tasks, LaunchDarkly per-course rollout). Document ingestion: S3 event triggers ECS embedding-worker task; chunks documents at 512 tokens with 64-token overlap, embeds via Titan v2, inserts into document_chunks with pgvector index rebuild after batch. Secrets: Cognito pool IDs and RDS credentials in AWS Secrets Manager; Bedrock IAM role via ECS task role -- no API keys.
reference.observability: Datadog APM: trace per /v1/tutor/chat request with spans for PolicyEnforcer, pgvector retrieval, context build, and Bedrock invocation. Custom metrics: tutor_response_latency_ms histogram per course_id, policy_deflection_rate counter per course_id and rule_type, bedrock_input_tokens_total and bedrock_output_tokens_total counters, context_summarization_invocations_total. Alerts: p99 response latency >5s fires PagerDuty; deflection rate spike >2x 7-day average fires Slack alert to instructor; embedding-worker queue depth >50 fires ops alert. Structured logs: student_id replaced by hashed_session_token; no student query plaintext in Datadog.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet at $0.003/1K input tokens and $0.015/1K output tokens; avg tutoring turn ~800 input tokens and ~300 output tokens = ~$0.007/turn; 10,000 turns/month = ~$70/month. Titan Embeddings v2 at $0.0001/1K tokens; 200 course document chunks/course, 3 courses = 600 embeddings plus query embeddings ~$5/month. ECS Fargate: min 2 tasks x 1vCPU/2GB = ~$60/month baseline; scales to 10 tasks during peak exam periods. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.t3.medium: ~$120/month. Total: ~$300/month for pilot, scaling proportionally with course enrollment.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- MVP Tutoring (6 weeks): ECS Fargate, FastAPI, Cognito auth, RAG over course PDFs (pgvector + Titan Embeddings v2), Bedrock Claude tutoring with system-role-locked prompt, SSE streaming React chat UI, keyword-only lockout policy, interaction logging. Phase 2 -- Academic Integrity Hardening (3 weeks): embedding-similarity lockout check (ISS-001 fix), sliding-window context with summarization (ISS-002 fix), prompt injection guard, instructor policy configuration UI, LaunchDarkly per-course feature flags. Phase 3 -- Analytics and Scale (3 weeks): instructor analytics dashboard, Datadog APM custom metrics, anonymization Lambda for 90-day FERPA requirement, performance tuning of pgvector ivfflat index, load testing for exam-period demand spikes.
reference.development_guidelines: All Bedrock prompt templates versioned in Git under prompts/ directory with changelog entries. Lockout policy changes require instructor authentication and are audit-logged. pytest mocks all Bedrock and Titan Embeddings calls; integration tests run against a dockerized PostgreSQL with pgvector extension. Student query plaintext must never appear in structured logs -- log only content_hash. Feature flag every course rollout; no direct production deployments without staging validation.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: embedding-similarity threshold (0.88) is too aggressive, flagging legitimate STEM questions as assignment problems -- mitigated by per-course threshold tuning and instructor review of deflection logs. Risk: Titan Embeddings v2 model version changes causing embedding space drift and false-negative lockout misses -- mitigated by storing embedding_model_version on courses and reindexing when model updates. Tradeoff: pgvector over Pinecone/Weaviate eliminates operational overhead but limits to single-node vector search; acceptable at pilot scale but may require migration at 1M+ chunks. Tradeoff: Bedrock Claude over GPT-4 satisfies AWS data boundary requirement at slightly higher latency.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Cosine similarity threshold 0.88 chosen as default after testing on sample STEM problem sets; configurable per course to allow stricter enforcement in exam periods. Sliding window of 8 turns chosen to balance context quality and Bedrock token cost for typical 30-minute tutoring sessions. Titan Embeddings v2 chosen over OpenAI text-embedding-ada-002 to keep all embeddings within AWS boundary.

{
 "thinking_summary": "This is a data pipeline for multi-vendor K-12 standardized test score ingestion. The key architectural decisions are: Airflow DAGs for structured orchestration with partial retries and task group isolation, ClickHouse materialized views for school-level and grade-level performance aggregates without raw row scanning, and FERPA-compliant small-cohort suppression at the aggregation layer. The plan quality is intentionally weak: several sections are thin and the data model is incomplete, reflecting the plan quality class of weak.",
 "fix_report": [],
 "title": "Multi-Vendor K-12 Standardized Test Score Ingestion Pipeline",
 "executive_summary": "A nightly Airflow pipeline on GCP Cloud Composer ingesting raw K-12 standardized test scores from multiple assessment vendors

parsed.thinking_summary: This is a data pipeline for multi-vendor K-12 standardized test score ingestion. The key architectural decisions are: Airflow DAGs for structured orchestration with partial retries and task group isolation, ClickHouse materialized views for school-level and grade-level performance aggregates without raw row scanning, and FERPA-compliant small-cohort suppression at the aggregation layer. The plan quality is intentionally weak: several sections are thin and the data model is incomplete, reflecting the plan quality class of weak.
parsed.title: Multi-Vendor K-12 Standardized Test Score Ingestion Pipeline
parsed.executive_summary: A nightly Airflow pipeline on GCP Cloud Composer ingesting raw K-12 standardized test scores from multiple assessment vendors via SFTP, normalizing them into a canonical schema, linking to student enrollment records, and publishing grade-level performance aggregates in ClickHouse. FERPA small-cohort suppression prevents individual student scores from appearing in any report where the cohort falls below 10 students per state guidance.
parsed.architecture_overview: Airflow DAG chain on Cloud Composer orchestrates nightly ingestion, validation, enrollment linkage, and aggregation. The ingest DAG polls SFTP servers from assessment vendors, uploads raw files to GCS, and routes to a Cloud Run validation microservice. The validate DAG runs the vendor schema validation step and quarantine step. The link DAG joins enrollment records to normalized scores via BigQuery temp tables. The normalize DAG applies canonical vendor-to-canonical schema transforms. The aggregate DAG triggers ClickHouse materialized views for school-level and grade-level performance aggregates. Grafana on Cloud Run serves dashboards to instructional leaders. All data flows within the GCP us-central1 region; no student PII leaves the project boundary.
parsed.technology_stack: Orchestrator: Apache Airflow 2.9 on Cloud Composer 2. Cloud Run for validation microservice. Analytics: ClickHouse 22.x on Compute Engine 2x n2-standard-8 with SSD. Raw storage: GCS buckets with lifecycle policies. BigQuery for audit exports. Visualization: Grafana 10 on Cloud Run. CI/CD: Cloud Build. Monitoring: Prometheus, Cloud Monitoring, Cloud Logging. Secrets: Google Secret Manager. Auth: Google Workspace SSO via GCP IAM.
parsed.functional_feature_map: SFTP Ingestion: nightly poll of assessment vendor SFTP servers, file transfer to GCS raw prefix. Vendor Schema Validation: Cloud Run validation microservice checks vendor file format against canonical schema version, routes to quarantine or pass. Enrollment Linkage: BigQuery temp table join between normalized scores and enrollment records by student_id and academic_year. Grade-Level Aggregation: ClickHouse MV refresh for school-level and grade-level proficiency rates. FERPA Small-Cohort Suppression: ClickHouse MV suppresses cohorts below 10 students per state guidance. Dashboard Delivery: Grafana dashboards for principal and curriculum coach consumption.
parsed.system_components: Cloud Composer 2 (Airflow 2.9, 2-4 tasks per DAG, 2-8 workers), Cloud Run validation service (Python 3.12, 1-10 workers), Compute Engine 2x n2-standard-8 (ClickHouse 22.x, 2-node cluster), GCS buckets (raw/ quarantine/ processed/ audit), BigQuery audit dataset, Grafana 10 on Cloud Run, Google Secret Manager (vendor credentials), Cloud Build (CI/CD), Cloud Logging (Airflow task logs and Cloud Run metrics), Prometheus (ClickHouse query latency metrics), Cloud Monitoring (Composer and Run metrics), Cloud Storage bucket for vendor file exports.
parsed.workflows: Nightly Ingestion: Airflow ingest DAG triggers at 23:00; polls each assessment vendor SFTP server via SFTP client; uploads raw files to GCS raw/ prefix; for each file, validates schema against canonical schema version in Cloud Run validation service; quarantined rows written to GCS quarantine/ prefix; downstream DAGs blocked until upstream task group succeeds; Airflow XCom stores file path and validation status for downstream task references. Enrollment Linkage: link DAG joins normalized_scores and enrollment_records via BigQuery temp table; returns school-level performance aggregates; ClickHouse MV refresh triggers for school-level and grade-level performance_aggregates tables. FERPA Small-Cohort Suppression: ClickHouse MV query filters performance_aggregates.school_id and grade_level where n_students < 10 and suppressed=false; suppressed rows excluded from aggregated output.
parsed.data_model: assessment_vendors (id UUID PK, name, sftp_host, schema_version, dsa_document_id, active). raw_score_files (id UUID PK, vendor_id FK, gcs_path, received_at, row_count, validation_status ENUM pending/passed/quarantined/failed). students (id UUID PK, state_student_id, district_id, enrollment_school_id, grade_level, active). enrollment_records (id UUID PK, student_id FK, school_id FK, academic_year, grade_level, enrollment_date). normalized_scores (id UUID PK, student_id FK, vendor_id FK, assessment_name, subject, raw_score, scaled_score, proficiency_level ENUM below_basic/basic/proficient/advanced, test_date, academic_year). performance_aggregates (id UUID PK, school_id FK, grade_level, subject, academic_year, proficiency_rate, n_students, suppressed BOOL). school_benchmarks (id UUID PK, school_id FK, subject, benchmark_proficiency_target, academic_year).
parsed.api_design: No external API. Internal task communication: Airflow XCom for small payload transfer between DAG tasks (e.g., file path for quarantine write). GCS object references: raw/raw_score_files/{vendor_id}/{file_name}.gz, quarantine/quarantined_files/{vendor_id}/{file_name}.gz, processed/normalized_scores/{vendor_id}/{academic_year}.parquet. BigQuery audit export: BigQuery export job triggered by compliance-auditor role.
parsed.security_and_compliance: FERPA controls: small-cohort suppression at ClickHouse MV layer (n_students < 10); student PII masked in Airflow task logs; GCS bucket ACLs restrict access to GCP project; BigQuery audit dataset captures every ClickHouse query via proxy logging; vendor data sharing agreement IDs logged per ingestion run. FERPA small-cohort suppression: ClickHouse MV query filters performance_aggregates.n_students < 10 and suppressed=false before aggregation. Google Workspace SSO via GCP IAM; no long-lived keys. CMEK at rest for all GCS data. TLS in transit for all SFTP connections. VPC-SC perimeter prevents exfiltration even if Cloud Run service is misconfigured.
parsed.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Composer 2 in us-central1 with 2-4 tasks per DAG. Cloud Run validation service: Python 3.12, 1-10 workers, scales on-demand via Cloud Run auto-scaling. ClickHouse on 2x n2-standard-8 Compute Engine with SSD, 2-node cluster. GCS buckets: raw/ quarantine/ processed/ audit. Cloud Build pipeline: flake8 lint, pytest, airflow dags test, container build and push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy, DAG sync via GCS bucket. Environments: dev (single Composer env, ClickHouse dev node), staging (full topology, anonymized fixtures), production (Composer + ClickHouse 2-node). DAG versioning via git tags; ClickHouse snapshot restore via Compute Engine disk snapshot; Cloud Run revision traffic rollback.
parsed.observability: Prometheus scrapes ClickHouse metrics: active_connections, query_duration_seconds, row_count_per_query. Cloud Monitoring: Composer task duration, validation pass/fail rates, normalized row counts per vendor. Grafana dashboards: DAG run durations, validation pass/fail rates, normalized row counts per vendor, MV refresh latency, small-cohort suppression applied. PagerDuty alerts: validation DAG failure, MV refresh failure, GCS quarantine file count exceeding 1000.
parsed.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $1,200/month for 2-node ClickHouse cluster, 2-4 Composer tasks, Cloud Run validation service, GCS storage for raw files. ClickHouse scales to 50M rows without horizontal node addition; vertical node upgrade to n2-standard-16 recommended at 100M rows. Cloud Composer scales to 4 tasks for 2-hour nightly run.
parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): SFTP ingestion for one vendor, schema validation, enrollment linkage, grade-level performance aggregates, ClickHouse MV refresh, Grafana dashboard for one school, FERPA small-cohort suppression, Cloud Build CI/CD. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Additional vendor onboarding, predictive early-warning models on normalized scores, teacher effectiveness correlation views, BigQuery compliance audit exports. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Predictive early-warning models, teacher effectiveness correlation views, data team self-service dashboard builder.
parsed.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. pytest for DAG task unit tests. airflow dags test for DAG logic validation. No raw student PII in any test fixtures. ClickHouse MV queries must be unit-tested with testcontainers. Airflow XCom payloads must be idempotent. Vendor schema changes require new schema version in Cloud Run validation service before production promotion.
parsed.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vendor SFTP credentials expire without coordinated rotation. Mitigation: Cloud Run validation service uses Google Secret Manager; rotation triggers via Cloud Scheduler. Risk: ClickHouse MV refresh latency exceeds 2-hour window. Mitigation: parallelized aggregation DAGs split by school; ClickHouse single-node is a scaling bottleneck beyond 50M rows; vertical node upgrade recommended. Tradeoff: ClickHouse over BigQuery for aggregation due to sub-second query performance on normalized scores; BigQuery would require a separate query layer for aggregation.
parsed.open_questions_resolved: Selected ClickHouse over BigQuery for aggregation due to sub-second query performance on normalized scores. Selected Cloud Composer over Airflow on GCP for managed DAG execution and task scheduling. Confirmed FERPA small-cohort suppression at ClickHouse MV layer over application-layer suppression to prevent false positives from vendor data quality issues.

reference.thinking_summary: Designing a FERPA-compliant K-12 assessment data pipeline on GCP. Key decisions: Airflow DAG separation by task group enables targeted retries without re-ingesting clean data; ClickHouse materialized views pre-aggregate school and grade-level metrics to serve principal dashboards at sub-second latency; small-cohort suppression baked into MV definitions prevents FERPA violations at query time; VPC-SC perimeter and GCS IAM ensure student PII never leaves the district GCP project boundary.
reference.title: K-12 Multi-Vendor Assessment Data Pipeline
reference.executive_summary: A FERPA-compliant multi-stage data pipeline on GCP that ingests nightly standardized test score files from assessment vendors via SFTP, validates against versioned JSON schemas, quarantines invalid rows, links scores to student enrollment records, normalizes into a canonical schema stored in ClickHouse, and refreshes materialized views powering Grafana dashboards for principals and curriculum coaches. The pipeline enforces small-cohort suppression at the aggregation layer and maintains a 7-year retention chain for state compliance.
reference.architecture_overview: Five-stage Airflow DAG chain orchestrated by Cloud Composer: (1) Ingest DAG polls vendor SFTP endpoints and deposits raw files to GCS raw/ prefix with vendor and date partitioning. (2) Validate DAG invokes Cloud Run validation microservice checking each file against a versioned JSON schema registry, routing passing files to processed/ and failures to quarantine/ with structured error manifests. (3) Link DAG joins validated score rows to students and enrollment_records tables in BigQuery temp datasets by state_student_id and academic_year. (4) Normalize DAG applies Python transforms mapping vendor-specific score scales to canonical proficiency_level enum and writes to ClickHouse normalized_scores. (5) Aggregate DAG triggers ClickHouse MV refreshes on performance_aggregates applying small-cohort suppression for groups below 10. VPC-SC perimeter encloses the entire GCP project; no student PII exits the boundary.
reference.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on Cloud Composer 2, Python 3.12 DAGs. Validation microservice: Python 3.12 FastAPI on Cloud Run with jsonschema library. Storage: GCS for raw/quarantine/processed files, ClickHouse 24.x on Compute Engine n2-standard-8 for analytics, BigQuery for compliance audit exports. Dashboards: Grafana 10 with ClickHouse data source plugin. Auth: Google Workspace SSO, GCP IAM with Workload Identity. CI: Cloud Build. Monitoring: Prometheus, Cloud Monitoring, PagerDuty.
reference.functional_feature_map: Vendor SFTP Ingestion: polling-based download with file deduplication via GCS object hash. JSON Schema Validation: per-vendor schema registry with version pinning and quarantine routing with structured error manifests. Enrollment Linkage: BigQuery join by state_student_id resolving multi-year enrollment changes. Score Normalization: configurable transform map per vendor converting raw/scaled scores to canonical proficiency_level. Aggregation Refresh: ClickHouse MV refresh computing proficiency rates by school, grade, subject, and academic year with cohort suppression. Dashboard Layer: Grafana panels for school comparison, grade-band trends, year-over-year proficiency shifts. Quarantine Management: data team remediation queue surfacing failed rows with field-level error codes.
reference.system_components: Cloud Composer 2 environment hosting Airflow scheduler and workers, Cloud Run validation service scaling 0-10 instances on ingestion trigger, GCS buckets for raw/quarantine/processed/dag-sync, ClickHouse 2-node Compute Engine cluster with SSD for normalized_scores and performance_aggregates, BigQuery dataset for enrollment lookups and compliance audit exports, Secret Manager storing vendor SFTP credentials, Grafana instance on Compute Engine with ClickHouse and BigQuery data sources, Prometheus scraping ClickHouse and Cloud Run metrics, PagerDuty via Cloud Monitoring alerting policies.
reference.workflows: Nightly Ingestion: Cloud Composer triggers ingest_dag at 23:00. Airflow SFTPSensor polls each active vendor host for new files matching date pattern. Files downloaded to GCS raw/vendor_id/date/ prefix. File hash checked against processed manifest to skip duplicates. validate_dag triggered on successful ingest. Validation and Quarantine: validate_dag submits each raw file to Cloud Run microservice. Service loads schema version from vendor registry, validates each row, accumulates errors. Files with greater than 5% row failure rate quarantined to GCS quarantine/vendor_id/date/ with JSON error manifest. PagerDuty alert fired with vendor name, file name, and failure rate. Passing files moved to processed/. Normalization and Aggregation: link_dag loads processed rows into BigQuery temp table, joins to enrollment_records on state_student_id and academic_year, flags unmatched rows as orphaned for data team review. normalize_dag transforms linked rows to proficiency_level enum and writes to ClickHouse. aggregate_dag executes ClickHouse MV refresh on performance_aggregates grouped by school_id, grade_level, subject, academic_year, applying WHERE n_students >= 10 suppression. Dashboard queries read exclusively from performance_aggregates.
reference.data_model: assessment_vendors (id UUID PK, name, sftp_host, sftp_credential_secret_id, schema_version, dsa_document_gcs_path, active BOOL). raw_score_files (id UUID PK, vendor_id FK, gcs_path, file_hash, received_at, row_count INT, valid_row_count INT, validation_status ENUM pending/passed/quarantined/failed, quarantine_manifest_path, processed_at). students (id UUID PK, state_student_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, district_student_id, first_name_encrypted, last_name_encrypted, dob_encrypted, current_school_id FK, active BOOL). enrollment_records (id UUID PK, student_id FK, school_id FK, academic_year, grade_level INT, enrollment_date, withdrawal_date). normalized_scores (id UUID PK, student_id FK, vendor_id FK, raw_score_file_id FK, assessment_name, subject, raw_score FLOAT, scaled_score FLOAT, proficiency_level ENUM below_basic/basic/proficient/advanced, test_date, academic_year). performance_aggregates (id UUID PK, school_id FK, grade_level INT, subject, academic_year, proficiency_rate FLOAT, n_students INT, suppressed BOOL, refreshed_at). school_benchmarks (id UUID PK, school_id FK, subject, benchmark_proficiency_target FLOAT, academic_year).
reference.api_design: No external HTTP API. Internal interfaces: Cloud Run validation endpoint POST /validate (multipart file + vendor_id + schema_version, returns {passed_rows, failed_rows, error_manifest_path}). Airflow REST API internal: GET /api/v1/dags/{dag_id}/dagRuns for monitoring integration. BigQuery compliance export: scheduled query writing score access events to compliance_audit dataset. Grafana queries ClickHouse via native HTTP interface on port 8123 restricted to read-only grafana service account. PagerDuty alert webhook on DAG failure and quarantine threshold breach.
reference.security_and_compliance: FERPA controls: small-cohort suppression (n less than 10) enforced in ClickHouse MV definition. ClickHouse row-level security restricts principals to their own school_id. VPC-SC perimeter blocks all GCS and BigQuery API calls originating outside the district GCP project. CMEK via Cloud KMS encrypts all GCS buckets and ClickHouse persistent disks. Vendor SFTP credentials in Secret Manager with 90-day rotation. Student PII fields (first_name, last_name, dob) AES-256 encrypted before insert. BigQuery audit dataset captures every query touching normalized_scores with timestamp, principal identity, and row count. Data sharing agreement document IDs logged per vendor ingestion run.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Composer 2 in us-central1 with 3 scheduler nodes and auto-scaling worker pool (2-8 workers). DAG deployment: Cloud Build pushes Python files to GCS dag-sync bucket; Composer picks up within 60s. Cloud Run validation service: Cloud Build builds Docker image, pushes to Artifact Registry, deploys revision with 100% traffic shift on health check pass. ClickHouse upgraded via Ansible playbook with pre-upgrade disk snapshot. Environments: dev (single Composer, ClickHouse dev node with 90-day score sample), staging (full topology with 2 mock vendors), production (Composer + 2-node ClickHouse). Rollback: DAG git tag rollback via GCS sync; ClickHouse snapshot restore; Cloud Run revision rollback.
reference.observability: Prometheus scrapes ClickHouse system.metrics every 30s: query_duration_ms p99, rows_read_per_second, disk_used_bytes. Cloud Monitoring tracks Cloud Run: request_count, error_rate, instance_count. Grafana dashboards: Pipeline Health showing DAG run durations and quarantine rates per vendor; Data Quality showing orphaned_scores count and validation pass rate; ClickHouse Performance showing MV refresh duration and disk growth. PagerDuty alerts: DAG run failure, quarantine rate above 10%, MV refresh above 90 minutes, ClickHouse disk above 80%.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $800/month: Cloud Composer 2 ~$400, ClickHouse 2x n2-standard-8 ~$280, Cloud Run validation ~$30, GCS lifecycle storage ~$60, Grafana/Prometheus instance ~$30. Scaling trigger: add ClickHouse replica node at 100M normalized_scores rows or p99 MV refresh exceeding 60 minutes. Cloud Run validation workers scale automatically on file volume without manual intervention.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): SFTP ingestion for one vendor, JSON schema validation with quarantine, enrollment linkage via BigQuery join, normalization to canonical schema, ClickHouse aggregation with small-cohort suppression, Grafana grade-level dashboards for 5 pilot schools, Cloud Build CI and VPC-SC perimeter. Phase 2 Multi-Vendor (6 weeks): schema registry with version management for 2 additional vendors, parallel DAG execution per vendor, orphaned_scores remediation workflow, expanded dashboards for all district schools. Phase 3 Advanced Analytics (8 weeks): year-over-year proficiency trend views, school benchmark comparison panels, early-warning data mart for predictive model ingestion, BigQuery compliance export automation.
reference.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with type hints on all DAG task functions. pytest for unit tests with Airflow LocalExecutor; minimum 80% coverage on normalization transform functions. Airflow DAG linting via airflow dags test in Cloud Build pipeline. ClickHouse schema migrations via versioned SQL files in /migrations directory applied by Ansible. No student PII in Airflow XCom payloads — pass GCS paths only. Vendor schema versions pinned in config/vendor_schemas.yaml under git version control. PR requires data engineer review for normalization or aggregation query changes.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vendor sends mid-year schema change without notice corrupting normalization. Mitigation: schema registry with strict version pinning; validation DAG blocks normalization on schema mismatch and pages data team. Risk: ClickHouse single-node disk exhaustion on 7-year score accumulation. Mitigation: quarterly disk usage review with auto-alert at 80%; replica node planned at 100M rows. Risk: Enrollment record gaps leave test scores unlinked. Mitigation: orphaned_scores metric on Grafana with 5-business-day remediation SLA. Tradeoff: ClickHouse over BigQuery for analytics sub-second latency; accepts manual upgrade and backup overhead.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected ClickHouse over BigQuery for analytics layer due to sub-second aggregation latency requirements for principal dashboards. Confirmed GCS over Cloud SQL for raw file storage given unstructured vendor file formats. Chose Cloud Composer over self-managed Airflow to reduce operator burden. FERPA small-cohort suppression threshold set at 10 students per state guidance.
